Non Conformance Title: The control batch was not tested for GC-HPLC analysis for product 9980 and 9990
Wrong documents were transferred to BizzMine eQMS software
Impact Other: The validation of cleanroom gowning was executed in 01.2022 according to the previous version of the DOC1074.1. The difference between the used document version and the current one is one additional test point and a change in the document layout. As validation plan in previous document version has one more  test point than current one, process execution is considered representative. Therefore there was no impact on quality of the executed process.

Non Conformance Title: Stored ELISA readings for 2 plates were identical
Wrong medium used for Test Production
Weekly cleaning in storage room not performed w52/2021 and w2/2022
Weekly environment monitoring on fridge in clean room not performed
Impact Other: Monitoring of the fridge in clean room has been within limit during the past weeks. There is not much activities in the fridge other than taking sealed flasks out and in of the fridge. Environment monitoring on the fridge was performed on 25.Jan.2022 and the results were 0/0 (CFU of Fungi/Bact.)  Thus we can conclude that the issue has no impact on production.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage from CP- ASM
Impact Other: One of the flowpath from same lot was returned to avoid risk of similar problem.

Non Conformance Title: Sirius loggers stop transmitting measurements to JRI
Circulation pump leakage in AcuSyst Maximizer DIA030
Impact Other: Without the increased likelihood of infection, this deviation has no impact on the product quality. During the run there was no suspicion of infection(Change of color, decreased cell viability), despite the  frequent inspections. Since the risk of infection was low, is was decided that testing was not necessary.

Non Conformance Title: Used expired glucose strips ACCU-CHECK
In process ELISA ON EO1 B8124-29 not reactive
Impact Other: The analysis is performed purely for planning purposes and is not used for QC of the product. The analysis is also performed in advance, so the results can be repeated and are not critical for planning. The impact is therefor considered low to none.

Non Conformance Title: Expired Protein standard for SDS-PAGE
Impact Other: No impact on product or product quality since the gel was discarded. New gel was ran again shortly after the non-conformance was discovered with new lot of Precision Plus Standard solution. The standard expired on 13.FEB.2022 and was discovered on 18.FEB.2022. No any SDS-PAGE was performed between 24.JAN.2022 (SDS585) and 18.FEB.2022 (SDS585 and SDS586) so there was no impact of the expired protein standard on any other SDS-PAGE performed.

Non Conformance Title: Cloudy EC medium
Impact Other: The cells in harvest medium will be transported back into BRX together with harvest medium as the pump continue feeding the BRX with medium, but due to low temperature in harvest medium bottle (stored in refrigerator), the number of cells will not expand and the risk of clogging the system is low. Harvest medium bottle was replacement the day before. The harvest medium is expensive, and it was decided not to replace the bottle.

Non Conformance Title: Temperature below lower limit on fridge in clean room
Impact Other: The refrigerator is not used to store product but only media used for cell culture and CL1000. After the discovery of non-conformance, the media in the refrigerator were visually checked. There were not any freezing of medium or the TSA plates. It was thus considered to have no effect on the media.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong version of template for Certificate of Analysis (doc 5011.2) was used
Investigation summary: Most likely root cause: a combination of human error and transfer to a new QMS system.
Impact Assessment: Template on F drive (used) and in Bizzmine (valid version) had the same information expect version number and SOP references according to new SOP numbering system. No impact to product quality or production processes.
Root cause description: Most likely root cause: a combination of human error and transfer to a new QMS system.

Non Conformance Title: Supplier Questionnaire from Abbott 2021 was not answered
MCB1, Freeze054: Froze 6 vials with 3D8-1 cells instead of 18vials
Investigation summary: The PS is for two products.
Impact Assessment: The MCB from FREEZE054 contains too few vials. A MCB has to contain vials from the same FREEZE.  The customer would not get what they ordered. The vial from FREEZE054 will be discarded. The new MCB from FREEZE055 is OK.
Root cause description: Two products in one PS

Non Conformance Title: Yearly calibration of Sirius sensors overdue
Investigation summary: Confusion around which sensors had to be calibrated due to etiquette labeling.  Maintenance list not followed or correctly updated. Training delayed due to illness.
Impact Assessment: Several CL1000 units and cell culture were stored in the CO2 incubators affected by this non conformance.  Late calibration have no product impact. No failure on actual equipment was observed and calibration values differs very little from year to year. For equipments that were calibrated twice, the first calibration is the valid one (implemented limits in the sirius system). Paper document of the second calibration is stored in instrument folder.
Root cause description: Calibration operator did not select the correct equipment to check within due date. Date is supposed to be checke on Oversikt vasking og utstyr - Fornebu 2nd floor" (Location: F:\FORNEBU\Rutiner\Vask og veldikehold). It is not clear whether the operator did check the list or if he misinterpret it. Operator don't remember. It is also possible that the operator updating the spreadsheet incorrectly filled it. The labels on loggers indicating date of calibration, displayed the calibration date and not due date, this was misunderstood (should be both)."

Non Conformance Title: Aseptic validation operation A-B-C: Evaluation of plates and containers done without incubation
Investigation summary: TNG performed tasks that require validation, without one.
Impact Assessment: The tasks performed are primitive and easy to perform without error. TNG have twenty years of experience with aseptic work and had at the time of deviation passed two out of three rounds of validation. The tasks performed was also supervised by trained and validated operators to ensure that they where performed correctly. Hence, this deviation is assessed to be low risk of impact on product. The container was incubated according to SOP1076 AT 30-35 degrees C, and any fungi would most likely also be detected even though the incubation at 20-25 degrees C was skipped.
Root cause description: Miscommunication between operators. The time for when TSA-plates was incubated was not checked. Operator in question is fairly new and not yet experienced.

Non Conformance Title: Temperature out of range- DIA067
Impact Other: No impact on other systems, processes or bathces
Investigation summary: The door was opened several times during stock counting. The operator who performed stock count was not aware that the temperature was exceed its limit.
Impact Assessment: It is difficult to predict the impact of temperature on individual product since every antibody differ in sensitivity to temperature. The maximum temperature reached during the period was -17.10°C which is above temperature limit with <1°C according to SOP and <3°C according to CoA. We have different temperature threshold required for storage of products according to Certificate of Analysis (CoA) and protocol (SOP4045), which make difficult to fully assess how much temperature was off from defined limit. The high alarm threshold set for freezer (DIA067) is -20.90 °C in the sirius monitoring system. According to CoA and protocol (SOP4045), the products should be stored below -20°C and -18°C respectively.     The duration with alarm was less than 2 hours. Since the temperature above threshold was not significantly high and the product were still frozen after counting, the non-conformance is deemed to have little impact on the product quality.
Root cause description: The door of the freezer was opened several times without considering the temperature in freezer.  The operate was not aware that temperature was exceed during stock count.

Non Conformance Title: Cells observed in IC chamber maximizer
Investigation summary: Cells should only be present in IC chamber /circuits. Cells present in IC chamber/circuit indicate either open EC FLUSH IN CLAMP or damage hollow fibers in BRX. EC FLUSH IN CLAMP is confirmed closed, and it is concluded that the hollow fibers are damaged. The supplier complaint was initiated, refer to SCR_068 for further handling).
Impact Assessment: ELISA measurement from 21.Jan.2022 was 0,25 mg/ml. which confirms the loss of antibodies. Production was terminated. The product quality was not affected as no infection was detected in sampled medium taken out from IC port. The Quality Control was performed for the final product, and all criteria were met.
Root cause description: Damage in hollow fibers in BRX during the manufacturing process.

Non Conformance Title: High CO2 alarm in incubator (DIA156 and DIA025)
Investigation summary: When the incubators (DIA025 and DIA156) are low in CO2 (for eg. by opening door) and loaded with many CL1000 flasks with new medium, they have tendency to raise CO2 above set point. The problem was solved either by opening door of the incubator or performing auto-zero. It can be some problem in the CO2 sensor which is supposed to prevent CO2 from going above set point.
Impact Assessment: Cell culture medium (RPMI1640 and Hybridoma SFM) can maintain physiological pH at the range of 5-10% CO2. Thus the incident has no impact on cells since CO2 level was not very high.
Root cause description: The incubators sensor failure.

Non Conformance Title: Cell line ABS 014-22 from Bioporto  was positive on Mycoplasma test
Investigation summary: The cell line ABS 014-22, project 2022-16 was infected before arrival at Diatec.
Impact Assessment: No product have been produced from infected cell line. In addition, all other cell lines in the same incubator were discarded to avoid culturing infection.  The new production will be started when a new cell will be recived and test negatrive for mycoplsma. The customer will send a new  non infected ampoule of the cell line ABS 014-22. The cell line will be cultured in quarantine incubator and mycoplasma tested after 7 days. The second ampoule from the other cell lines will be thawed at almost the same date as ABS 014-22. Aim to culture all the cell lines in parallels.
Root cause description: The cell line ABS 014-22 were infected before arrival at Diatec. In addition the SOP 1070  was not clear on how to handle the cell culture which was stored with infected flask.  The infected flasks were transferred first into the clean room, as there is no procedure for this type of scenario, as mycoplasma infection is rarely detected in the production. Moreover, more than one cell line is rarely received from the customer at this same time. The production team highlighted that there is no straightforward procedure for what should be done with cells line stored together with infected cell lines within this same incubator. Even if the risk of cross-contamination between the flasks is considered very low, a proper routine for this kind of scenario should be established.

Non Conformance Title: B8194, CO6F8F/2/1 One of the samples was contaminated on the SDS QC gel
QC SAH duplicates not comparable B8192 (1A12/1/4) and B8193 (UCUS99.05)
Project 2022-01 EO1: wrong SOP references and IEF listed in QC section
Impact Other: No impact to other batches, the previous batch produced was in 2020 prior to termination of IEF.  New document ID for procedures was implemented in 2021.
Investigation summary: Conclusion investigation:  - IEF method added to PS because it was listed as one of the QC methods in Purchase agreement - Wrong SOP reference was added due to human error
Impact Assessment: IEF method is terminated and is no longer available at Diatec. Customer is informed of this change in May 2020 as part of CMR198 implementation. No impact to product quality.    It is no valid SOP1080 in the QMS system in the period of running project 2022-01 so it is no risk that the wrong procedure has been used. Operator has used the correct SOP for purificaiton of B8209 and B8210 (final batch B8211) and it is documented in Doc0080.  No impact to product quality
Root cause description: Root cause idenfifed:  - IEF method added to PS because it was listed as one of the QC methods in Purchase agreement. Root cause identified: documentaition - Wrong SOP reference was added due to incorrect typing. Root cause identified: human error.

Non Conformance Title: Monthly cleaning analyse lab one day delayed
Leakage from the RO-water tank
Investigation summary: The leakage was caused by weekend tubing in the water purification system.
Impact Assessment: The RO-water supply worked fine directly after stop of leakage. Elevated controls values could not be observed on environment monitoring. The cold room floor is completely sealed, no potential of damage of cold room interior. However there is still a possibility that water could remain underneath cold room, even if it is already checked. But this may be visible from results from future environment monitoring. No product was affected.
Root cause description: Breakage of tubing and service technician probably did not see weakness on tubing.

Non Conformance Title: Purification column loaded with1500 mL instead of 800 mL for batch B8214, clone MY-11
Impact Other: This deviation had an impact on the company's reputation and finance. The new production was started at the Customer's request. The Customer currently purchased approximately 25% of the batch B8214 and is not sure at this point if the rest of this batch will be sold. The Customer offered to accept some of the vials with batch B8214 until a new batch, will be produced.
Investigation summary: The customer had additional requirements for the purification process which differs from most usual runs defined in the SOP. Requirments were included in the Product Specification however operator forgot to check the Product Specification for additional information.
Impact Assessment: Incorrect load volume did not impact product quality. The Customer performed testing of the antibodies on their site and the batch in question fullied their acceptance criteria.  The Quality Control of the final batch was performed at Diatec using BIORAD Stainfree SDS method. Determined product purity was well within defined product specification criteria (see attachment SDS610 for reference).
Root cause description: The operator forgot to check if additional information for the purification process was included in the Product SPecification or Control Plan. During this time the workload in production was very heavy and the Operator followed only Standard Procedures for this operation.

Non Conformance Title: Sirius logger stopped transmitting data to DIA245 refrigerator
Investigation summary: The dropped visible on the temperature chart was caused by a loss of signal from the temperature logger. The destruction in signal transmission was related to sensor malfunction.
Impact Assessment: The drop in temperature was probably only a lack of the signal, and not actually a change in temperature. This is indicated by the short time used for temperature drop and recovery. The stop in transmission happened on Sunday evening. The refrigerator is in use every Monday by the operators, who will be spotted if the inventor would have been frozen. Therefore, the incident doesn't have an impact on product quality.
Root cause description: The true reason can not be defined at this point. The supplier was contacted and pointed out the low battery could be the reason for stopping in data transmission. However, the fact that the sensor started to transmit a signal after 34 hours without any restarts or change of battery contradicts this explanation. This has happened before, ref NC_8, and the root cause was not defined for that NC. Currently, we can not define the root cause to be the incidence. CAPA will be initiated to search for a notification system for superusers. The super user will explore if it is possible to set up the alarm notification to alert the user when the sensor stops transmitting the signal. The same notification system is already created for temperature, CO2, and battery status. What is more, an individual DIA number was assigned to each of the loggers to improve tracking of instrument underperformance.

Non Conformance Title: Control batch for Tc3C4 used in SDS582 was not an approved batch
Investigation summary: There is no robust system for how to handle QC samples.  Information of approved and not approved (rejected) batches are not easily found.
Impact Assessment: No impact to B8115 Tc3C4, batch was re-analysed 17.JAN.2022 and saved as SDS583. B8049 was used as control batch.
Root cause description: Not robust system for how to handle QC samples and overview of approved/rejected batches lacking.

Non Conformance Title: Pipettes DIA241, DIA124 and DIA126 failed As found" on 6 months service control"
Mix-up of shipments to Exbio and Radiometer
Impact Other: This incident might hurt our reputation with the customer
Investigation summary: Two shipments for Exbio and Radiometer were picked up by Atlas the 2nd of May.  The boxes were marked with customer name, when departing Diatec facility. When Atlas prepared the boxes for pick-up by courier at their location,  they mixed up the two boxes, and re-labeled with the wrong customer name. This led to the product intended for Exbio, was sent to Radiometers facility in Finland and the product intended for Radiometer, was sent to Exbio.
Impact Assessment: 09.05.22 MIB:  Batch B8179 was shipped cool and received by Radiometer on the 4th of May. Radiometer asked if they should restock with cool elements before returning, but it was decided by Diatec to return the shipment as is with minimal interference.  Atlas received the return package at Friday the 6th of may at 11 o’clok. The material was then placed cool and delivered to Diatec on Monday the 9th of May. It was decided to discard the product as we have no control over the temperature after the product left Radiometer. It was discussed to re-run QC and offer the product at a discounted price, but since  we have extra material produced it was decided that the cost and risk is less by discarding the product. New product has been shipped to the customer.  Batch B8214 was shipped cool and recieved by Exbio on the 4th of May. Exbio confirms that the product has been stored cool since. The batch is for Radiometer to test in their assay. When batch is returned it will be re-sent to Radiometer for testing.
Root cause description: Human error at our freight forwarder Atlas, they did not properly chek the labeling on the box

Non Conformance Title: Medium-cap tube was connected to inlet in preparation of autoclaving
Investigation summary: Both air in the machine and turning down harvest and F3 pumps can have caused the clogging of EC-chamber. It can not be deduced which is the causing factor.
Impact Assessment: The flow path has not lost its sterility and is of no risk to contamination. The runs output could have been affected by this non conformity.
Root cause description: Wrong assembly of medium cap before autoclaving.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage from outflow tubing during pre-inoculation phase in DIA029
Customer Audit - BioPorto: Supplier monitoring program was not followed up
Impact Other: The internal procedure was not followed.
Investigation summary: The established routine for supplier qualification is not well defined or not well documented. The current system, which is excels based, does not provide a clear overview and timeline for supplier re-qualification.
Impact Assessment: This non-conformity has to impact product quality. All supplier raw materials ongoing incoming material control before being used in production, therefore the risk of using material from an unqualified supplier is very low.
Root cause description: Lack of robustness instruction on how to maintain and follow Supplier Qualification Program.

Non Conformance Title: Customer Audit - BioPorto, The SOP5029 does not define frequency of the internal audit program
Investigation summary: The current SOP is not in compliance with ISO9001:2015 norm.
Impact Assessment: The lack of compliance in this area has no direct impact on product quality
Root cause description: The current procedure SOP5029 Audit doesn't provide clear instruction on the internal audit frequency.

Non Conformance Title: Clamping error of flowpath before fill flush (1A12/1/4- B8256)
Investigation summary: Clamping error and operator performed the procedure alone, when it is recommended with two people. The error was corrected, flushed with medium overnight and cleared for use.
Impact Assessment: The flow path is scheduled to be inoculated in week 25, so the deviation caused no delay in production. This is also more than enough time to flush the bioreactors. Since this NC is that of a pre-inoculation procedure, this deviation has no impact on the product. The deviation could however be a critical error if it occurred a different place in the flow path. The procedure is to complicated to be performed alone and needs to be updated.
Root cause description: SOP0060 is a large and complicated procedure and should not be performed alone, this can cause similar deviations. The procedure requires the operator to perform instructions from SOP, two manuals, Acusyst maximizer display while working aseptically. This is a risk to the accuracy of performing the procedure as well as the sterility.

Non Conformance Title: Validation of Aseptic Cell Culture Processes and Validation of Cleanroom Gowning for GMP production and QC team
QC SDS606 do not fulfil technical requirements for test samples and precison plus standard
Investigation summary: It is difficult to define the exact reason for the issue. Still, since both the precision standards and samples were affected, there had to be system failure during the run. The most probable reason was that the lid on the electrode assembly probably did not fit perfectly in place before executing the run.
Impact Assessment: No impact on product quality. The analysis was re-analyzed by SBa (SDS607) 13.jun.2022 and the results is within specification The SDS run 607 is attached.
Root cause description: Lid on electrode assembly was most likely not fit perfectly in place.

Non Conformance Title: DIAXXX DARK MK BOX AD18278352 outside temperature threshold specifications
One additional protein band detected on SDS610 for LAW 268:29 B7897
Investigation summary: Product Law 268:29 is unstable overtime and reference batches for quality control of Law 268:29 shall be chosen from another product with same isotype.
Impact Assessment: No impact on product quality. The two parallel test batch B8287 (attachment 1) is of approximately same size as the parallel reference batch B7897 in the quality control data for test batch B7897 (3 respectively).
Root cause description: Product Law 268:29 is degrades over time.

Non Conformance Title: Several invalid runs in in-process control ELISA
Precipitation observed in final product UCUS
Impact Other: No impact on the company reputation as precipitation was observed before product shipment. Customer is also aware that this type of phenomena can occur and will likely not file customer complains.
Investigation summary: Observed precipitation is like pure antibodies (product), however reason for the precipitation can not be fully defined. This is known characteristic of this product and clone which reoccurred from time to time.
Impact Assessment: The product was reprocessed according to the procedure for revealing. The quality control was performed and all criteria were met. Reference attachment: SAH specificity test by ELISA_E6036_B8336    The concentration of the reprocessed product was determined.
Root cause description: White precipitation was observed in several bottles of the final UCUS product (B8236). The reason of the precipitation can not be fully defined but is a known characteristic of this product and clone.

Non Conformance Title: Standard used for in-process ELISA was not an approved product
Investigation summary: The standard used for ELISA was the product waiting for approval from QA for batch release.  The operator did not check if the standard sample selected was a QA-approved product.  The weekly plan is not always filled with the batch number of the standard sample to be used.  The SOP doesn't specify the specific criteria for the selection of a standard sample.  Mouse coating and conjugate were used instead for rat coating.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance has a minor impact on product quality as the ELISA performed was an in-process control to check the production status of cells and if the cells are producing as expected. The production status helps to estimate the amount of product produced and decide the duration of production. No other parameters will be affected for the production by the non-conformance. The production status is usually estimated by another round of ELISA as well from different rounds of harvest. The non-conformance will, therefore won't impact the production duration or production itself.  ELISA for the sample was repeated for harvest H5 with approved batch (B7395) as a standard sample on 02/08/2022.  The use of mouse, instead of rat, coating and conjugate will not have much impact for the analysis as long as the sample and the standard are from the same clone. The concentration is measured in relation to the concentration of the standard. Since both the sample and standard were measured in the same condition, the binding capacity and other parameters were similar for both the standard and test batches. In addition, as explained earlier, it is an in-process control to check the production status and does not affect production or product quality.
Root cause description: The operator did not check if the product was approved by QA. The field for standard to be used for ELISA in weekly plan was empty and the operator took the most recent vialed product as standard control. The SOP doesn't specify the specific criteria for selection of standard sample.

Non Conformance Title: Kinked tubing between IC sample line and DO IN line 1A12/1/4 B8256
Investigation summary: The kink was most likely cased when the lid on maximizer was closed after in process control on Friday week 30.
Impact Assessment: According to standard procedure: DO (dissolved oxygen) probe are not in use, and the PRE and POST BRX DO clamps are positioned correct (See attachment 1). The positions of PRE and POST BRX DO clamps decide the flow rout of medium in the loop as marked with arrows in attachment 1. The loop is a secondary circuit and the purpose is to decide if the IC sample and DO measure (when applicable) should be measured before or after the medium has passed through the BRXs. The primary circuit is not affected by the kink and the incident has therefore no short- or long term problems for the cell culture. All of the oxygenation and pH control occur through the primary circuit  - see mail (Attachment 2) from Curt Gleiter which is the service person from supplier company Cell culture company. See attachment 3 for primary circuit.
Root cause description: The kink in tubing line was caused when maximizer lid was closed.

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect column materials used for purification of ABS 012-35
Impact Other: No other impact on this clone.  If this mistake was done with another clone/customer there might have been a impact to product quality and/or customer not being able to use the product
Investigation summary: Six test productions started at the same time. Similar clone names and productions  This clone was likely purified on protein G, because operator chose the same as the four other clones and did not check the PS
Impact Assessment: The customer was notified, and have no problem with the mix up of protein A/G. (see email attahced)  They are  concidereing purifying all clones on protein G as they see a better yield.
Root cause description: Operator did not follow the information in the PS

Non Conformance Title: Blown fuse turned off DIA107, DIA226 and DIA023
Investigation summary: Power to DIA023, DIA107 and DIA226 was shut down by a circuit breaker, caused by DIA170, an orbital shaker or by overloading the circuit by the addition of DIA170. DIA170 was removed from the laboratory and placed in quarantine. To prevent other equipment to overload the circuit capacity the outlet was covered with outlet-blockers. All the affected equipment were back online after approximately 16,5 hours. The cells incubated in DIA107 belonging to projects 2022-42 and 2022-44 continued as usual, and the cells were expanded until mycoplasma testing could be performed  10.08.2022. The cells was microscoped, counted and viability was determined (see worksheet attachment for each project). The deviation had no consequences for the projects at hand.
Impact Assessment: Cells from projects 2022-42 and 2022-44 were monitored to investigate if the deviation was fatal. No infection or decrease in cell viability were deted. Both projects was unaffected and the deviation had no additional impact.
Root cause description: Power was shut down by a circuit break caused by DIA170, an orbital shaker. either by overloading the circuit or because of a defect in the equipment.

Non Conformance Title: Visual bands in control batch during SDS-PAGE of IgM
Investigation summary: The control (B7343) used during SDS deviated from the approval criteria but not the sample. It is most likely that the control has degraded over time during storage. The control is a QC approved sample (Ref. SDS490) and SOP1066 doesn't specify the criteria for control batch's approval. Thus this SDS run is actually not a non-conformance but it is recommended to start a CAPA to have a procedure regarding the use of IgM control.
Impact Assessment: The deviation has no impact on the product itself.  The test batch showed no sign of degradation or impurities, as no bands were detected on the line where the test batch was loaded. The performance of the gel was correct as the IgG and precision standard, which were run simultaneously with the test batch, showed the expected pattern of protein separation.  The additional band observed for the control batch indicates that the product would degrade within three years. The Diatec doesn’t claim shelf life for that product, therefore, observed control batch degradation has no impact on the already produced product.
Root cause description: The control used for SDS-PAGE might have degraded over time. The SOP doesn't specify what to select as a standard and there is always a chance that previously approved IgM batch as control will degrade overtime.

Non Conformance Title: Product shipped to Bisosense AS 08.AUG.2022, not received by customer
Impact Other: Business - new productions will be started,extra cost on resources and raw material
Investigation summary: 18.08.2022 MIB First mistake done by Ramberg, the courier. Shippment was misplaced and not scanned in correct system  Seccondly, Atlas did not provide tracking link to Diatec the same day or day after pick-up of shipment.  If tracking link had been provided sooner, the mistake could have been detected earlier and the product might have been saved in time. In addition, Atlas placed the shipment with Ramberg and not Bring as asked for by Diatec. Previous shipments to Biosense has been with Bring without any issues.
Impact Assessment: 18.08.2022 MIB The shipment was packaged according to SOP5024. Enough cooling elements was used to keep product cool until thursday morning (11.08).  1 week later the package is still no located and no reccord to show how it has been stored. It is assumed by Diatec,Atlas and Biosens that it has not been stored cool and that the product quality has been impacted by this.  The product will be discarded upon return to Diatec.
Root cause description: Package was misplaced and not scanned correctly during handling of shipping

Non Conformance Title: QC Elisa failed, aE11 a-C5b-9 ALP conjugated product
Investigation summary: Mixup of some buffers may have occured, not possible to confirm. There is no reason to suspect the coating failure, but it is possible. Also is the possibility of defect of actual plate.
Impact Assessment: No impact on product quality. The analysis was repeated twice, and specification criteria were met for both retests.
Root cause description: Elisa assay failure, not possible to find exact operation. The cause is operator failure or equipment failure.

Non Conformance Title: SDS619 produced a too week standard ladder to be detected by the software
Outer medium for CL1000 was inserted only 30 minutes before inserting cells clone 581, batch B8304.
Drop in CO2 level DIA156 incubator
Investigation summary: The incubator stopped working and did not have power due to technical failure. The instrument was kept in Quarantine" for reparation from external service provider.  "
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance did not have any impact on the product. The CL1000s were transferred from incubator DIA156 to DIA025 on 9.MAY.2022 before CO2 level was below the alarm limit.
Root cause description: Under assessment by service personnel, technical failure in the PCB transformer was observed.

Non Conformance Title: Heat inactivaition date not documented in registrer for M220706A-M
Missing QA approval for validation of aseptic culture process
Artifact band on the well of SDS-PAGE
Impact Other: The batch release was delayed for one week due to the time needed for NCR investigation and reanalysis.
Investigation summary: The additional band visible for the tested sample originates from the gel or loading buffer and not the product itself, therefore, it's considered an artifact. Those bands are reoccurring artifacts for that technique, therefore, an area near the well should be excluded from the gel analysis. The instruction on how to cut gel pictures and interpret gel are not well described in the procedure, especially for IgM products which are rarely manufactured at the ISO site.
Impact Assessment: The additional band visible for the tested sample originates from the gel or loading buffer, not the product itself. What is more, are visible only one duplicate of the tested sample and also on other samples run on this same gel.    Therefore, an additional band is considered an artifact. The sample was reanalyzed once (reference SDS625) The reanalysis was performed using freshly released IgM of different clones, therefore, no sign of degradation was visible either on the tested or reference batch.
Root cause description: The gel was analyzed including the sample application well. The wells are generally not included in analysis by several operators but the SOP was not clear about it.

Non Conformance Title: C02 supply to laboratory equipment halted
Investigation summary: Prior to this NC occurring it was noticed by ØHR on 22.AUG.2022 that the pressure gauge for the cylinders on side 1 showed they were not full, this was notified to BKP at the time. NC occurred 24.AUG.2022 where there was no CO2 available in the analytical lab for a period of 2-3 hours. Both tanks and automatic switch were investigated 25.AUG.2022, a summary of possible causes for this NC is outlined below: Possible causes: Side 1 cylinders may be close to empty when set into position, if so CO2 may have been used by Diatec in the lab previously (previous CO2 setup). It is unlikely that the producer supplied pre-used cylinders to Diatec as cylinders are received with a plastic seal. Not likely, but possible that it is an error in side 1 of the Automatic CO2 switch. Still, mounting of new cylinders on side1 did show a correct startup pressure with no fading after some days. Side 2 of the switch seems so work fine, side 2 cylinders were emptied in the correct way, and the level was acceptable some days after the incident.
Impact Assessment: Since the incident happened during working day the impact was limited. No impact on Maximizer production, parameter Gas number is on 1, means no use of CO2 at that moment. Incubators DIA022 and DIA024 had lack of CO2 over too short time to get any impact, see sirius curves attached. Other incubators on the Attachment list did not fall below alarm threshold and therefore there is no impact to the materials/samples that were stored in these incubators. Cells can survive without CO2 for a while, according to SOP4008. Also CL1000 in good production state can survive even much longer due to naturally high acidity. 5% CO2 in atmosphere is buffering to the acidic side. Therefore there is no impact to these cells. Running pressure on CO2 line from cylinders and into wall mounted regulators is normally 13-16 bar. When the CO2 was back, almost immediately the pressure at the end points were back to normal due to this high pressure.
Root cause description: 1. CO2 cylinders on side 1 were near to empty prior to use. Tanks can be connected by the delivery person (from the supplier) or by Diatec staff. As it is not possible to determine who changed the tanks it is not possible to confirm if the tanks were ‘full’ when connected. Approved supplier is expected to deliver full tanks. Staff authorised to change tanks have been given practical training and would report non-conformities if observed during the connection process. Delivery person may have poorly connected the tanks, unable to confirm at this time. Most probable root cause, tanks were not full when connected, could be due to issue with tanks, issue with caps on tanks etc.This issue will be monitored for recurrence. Newly connected and opened cylinders will at once build pressure up to 40-50bar, should be indicated on gauge.   2. Issue with automatic switch when switching to side 1. To confirm if this is the issue a CAPA will be raised to check the automatic switching process once the side 2 tanks are empty. It is expected this will occur in October (as it takes approximately 2 months to empty 2 CO2 tanks). Unconfirmed root cause.           Still we could have an issue that is silent at the moment since we have not found an actual reason for the incident of empty tanks on side 1.

Non Conformance Title: Pipette DIA114, 0.5-10uL failed as found on 6 month service controll
Trend review not performed for incubator DIA169- Q2 2022
Investigation summary: This incubator's trend review document was missing which can possibly be due to miscommunication during task assignment and that the SOP4032 is unclear as to what to do when the equipment is switched off at the time the trending review is required to be performed.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance did not have any impact. The Sirius logged data were reviewed on 26.08.2022 and no deviation related to the data trend was observed. Sirius system are checked by operators routinely for alarms or malfunction.  However, such non-conformance can always pose a risk of not detecting the trend of instrument failure if the maintenance routine is not followed.
Root cause description: Miscommunication between operators while the task was assigned and that the SOP4008 is unclear as to what to do when the equipment is switched off at the time the trending review is required to be performed. Suggested improvement to update SOP4008 to specify the Sirius logger trend review regime when instrument is switched OFF.

Non Conformance Title: Particle found in H4 of 1A12/1/4 B8256
Investigation summary: Sampling of infection was performed and the investigation found no positive results.
Impact Assessment: Deviation has no impact on product quality or project timeline. Some working hours and resources were utilized to perform the Sampling of infection" procedure. Normal monitoring of the maximizer run was deemed good enough to observe how the cells handled the machine stops."
Root cause description: The particle is of unknown material and origin, Its presents however has no impact on the sterility of the flowpath or product. The pH probe connections has most likely loosened with time and started leaking close to discovery. The circulation pump ring is most likely human error at installation of the flowpath, however it is not a certainty.

Non Conformance Title: Missing validity date on printed SOPs
Investigation summary: The bookmarks were correctly incorporated in the document template but were unintentionally completely or partially deleted during the document update.
Impact Assessment: Only valid document version is accessible for the operator via 1-Document Control collection in eQMS software. Production procedures are also available for operators in the lab in the printed version. When a new version is released production team replaces the paper version in the lab. The system is in place and the SOP periodic review will not be missed.
Root cause description: Probably the bookmarks were unintentionally deleted during the document update. Several procedures were updated into new templates in the period December 2021 - February 2022 as a part of the execution of the Change Control CMR218: “New document ID”. The change was executed in a short time, which could increase the chance of errors. All employees involved in the document update process were new to BizzMine software, which was implemented just a few weeks earlier. The header content of the documents template can be accessed by all employees involved in document updates (as an approver, verifiers, reviewers, or authors). This increase the chance of unintentional changes and it's not addressed in the SOP5042 Document Management.

Non Conformance Title: Vivaldi software, supplier of eQMS BizzMine software is not approved supplier
Investigation summary: The qualification of the software provider was not included as an action of change control. The impact of the change CMR204 was not identified during the change revision and approved for execution.
Impact Assessment: The software is recognized by the industry and used by several Pharma, Med Tech, and Food companies, which must comply with strict Computer System qualification requirements. Therefore risk associated with the underperformance of the software or service is considered minor. The currently implemented used software collections were tested before being released to the user (refer to CMR204 and CMID10) to prove their functionality. All discovered bugs in the software performance were corrected or addressed during validation. The software is not used in laboratory data management (LIMS) and not as an electronic master batch record, therefore, its direct impact on production and product quality is minor.
Root cause description: The potential root causes can be narrowed down to: -	Insufficient training in change management. All the employees receive general training in Change Management as part of Introduction training (refer to SOP5047 Handling of training). However, additional, more comprehensive training should be given to department heads who are revising the change control documents and the QA team approving the execution change.  -	Change control process -  the change often impacts several areas of the organization. Therefore change should be revised/consulted by Project Team. This is stated in the procedure, however, it is not reflected in the internal process or change management collection workflow. The change is only revised by the department head selected during the change registration step.   -	Documentation – The procedure for change management SOP5038 Change Management and the associated worksheet doesn't provide clear guidelines on identifying areas that a change can impact. The Change Management collection replaced the worksheet, however, this aspect was not improved, and it's not reflected in the current collection design.

Non Conformance Title: ASX shutdown over weekend during fill flush procedure
Investigation summary: The power sockets were not well-labelled. Labelling of sockets while building the lab is a responsibility of electrician. Because the sockets were not well-labelled, both the incubator fridge plug and the pump control electronics plug were connected to sockets on same circuit in MCB. This led to overloading in MCB and current flow was stopped.
Impact Assessment: During the pre-inoculation phase of ASX, the flowpaths are prepared to provide optimal conditions for cells inoculation such as, pH calibration and removal of air bubbles from the system. These parameters must be changed several times before the system is ready for inoculation. Due to power interruption, the machine stopped measuring the parameters which delayed the time required for the system to be ready for inoculation. After ASX was switched ON again, all the parameters mentioned above were checked routinely. When pH and temperature was close to setpoint the system was confirmed to be ready for inoculation phase. Pre-inoculation phase does not contain cells in the system, thus it is the phase to prepare bioreactor with environment suitable for cells. Therefore, the non-conformance doesn't have any impact on product and the product quality. The incident did not impact scheduled inoculation of cells as the machine was started long before the cells were ready for inoculation. So it was enough time to adjust parameters before inoculation and the inoculation was performed on 12.SEP.2022 as scheduled.
Root cause description: The power socket was not well-labelled and there were free power outlets available on the socket. So both the power cables from DIA277 were connected to the same circuit.

Non Conformance Title: Infection in cell culture before inoculation in Xcellerator DIA309
Investigation summary: The samples from the flasks that were considered contaminated under light microscope were confirmed to be microorganism infected by streaking the samples out on TSA plates and incubating for 3-5 days at room temperature and 4-7 days at 30-35 degrees celcius.      There are several steps involved that can be a cause for infection which are mentioned below:     a) Increased number of cell culture flasks to handle. The operators involved in the process were all experienced working with cells and validated for aseptic works in clean room.     b) Workflow during aseptic work. The operators had to bring things in and out of LAF bench several times during expansion of flasks which can disturb the laminar air flow of LAF bench. Even though this is a normal procedure while working in the LAF bench, the disturbance in the airflow can have more impact on the cell culture than other processes like CL1000.
Impact Assessment: As this is an internal production this had no impact on customer, no impact on product or product quality as the infected cells were discarded prior to inoculation. Only impacts timelines of an internal project and workload for operators. The non-conformance delayed inoculation procedure during test-production of 1A12/1/4 in ASX. Inoculaion was planned to be performed on 08.SEP.2022 but was postponed to 12.SEP.2022.  No signs of infection is seen on the cells inoculated on ASX until on 21/10/2022. B8353 was terminated early, but due to cells observed in IC (NC74).
Root cause description: The exact source of contamination is difficult to confirm. Based on the analysis, the most likely root cause can be considered that operator had to handle large number of flasks as well as more turbulence within the LAF bench. Cell culture is a very sensitive procedure and infection can occur from a very slight compromise of aseptic procedure which is very difficult to completely eliminate. Infection is rare in our production however, there is always a slight risk of infection during cells handling.

Non Conformance Title: Annually service was not performed for cold room DIA283.
ELISA- Incorrect dilution of standard
Cells observed in IC chamber of Xcellerator
Investigation summary: Investigation identified leakage in one or more of the bioreactors in the flowpath, most likely due to issues in manufacturing. It was tried to locate the leakage, but with no conclusive results. The test production was terminated due to low productivity and resources.
Impact Assessment: B8353 is a test production utilizing expired materials and was not meant for release. Hence, there is no impact to product quality nor production timeline. However, it has affected the materials used in the cell expansion phase, resources delegated towards the project and has resulted in a delay in the qualification of Acusyst Xcellerator. PQ of Xcellerator requires a successful run, therefore the instrument is not qualified for use.
Root cause description: As established in the investigation the main root cause of this non-conformance is considered to be material. More specifically, leakage in the bioreactors of the flow path due to damage of the hollow fibers during manufacturing of bioreactor. This test production was performed to validate the AcuSyst Xcellerator in our production (Ref: CMID8). Expired materials was used to reduce cost and was supplied by the GMP site.  The risk associated with use of expired materials was taken by production management  and evaluated in correlation with CMID8.

Non Conformance Title: pH probe autoclaved in not approved instrument
Investigation summary: Due to this error in the autoclave machine, pH probe used in ASM (DIA209) was autoclaved in SurViva AS. The operators were not aware of the criteria for using external instruments for production. The autoclave in SurViva AS facility is not a validated instrument for ISO production facility.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance does not have impact on the product since the pH probe which will be used in production was autoclaved according to the requirement in SOP4037 (Ref Attachment: pH probe, pH probe1). The autoclave was performed by a trained operator in SurViva AS.   No signs of infection was observed in the production batch until today 01.11.2022. Inoculation of ASM is planned on 02/11/2022.
Root cause description: The operators are trained in the SOP5043 for instruments shortly after starting working in the company but there is no routine for regular re-training on the SOP.    It was a common practice to send laboratory equipments for autoclave in SurViva AS. The operators were not aware about the procedure regarding instruments that are registered and activated for production.

Non Conformance Title: Planned non conformance: use of an unqualified autoclave for ISO production
Lost Power Supply for 2D2 batch B8385 DIA029
Impact Other: Risk of not meeting deadline for product delivery for customer, potentially impacting further trade with said partner.
Investigation summary: The wrongful shut down of a circuit breaker caused a Acucyst Maximizer unit at max productivity to shut down, stopping the processing of a vital product. This caused the cells of the process to die, requiring the Maximizer to be re-initiated according to SOP0060(Version 3.0) and the start-up of multiple CL1000 to reach product deadline.
Impact Assessment: Creating a lot of extra work to restart production in accordance to SOP0600 as well as multiple new CL1000 in accordance to SOP0042 in order to meet customer's expected delivery date for product. Loss of product that would be produced by the Maximizer between 05.11.2022 and 16.11.2022 as the output from this period was discarded due to unknown quality of antibodies produced by stressed cells.  Harvest 1, batch B8449, was not affected by the loss of power since it was immediately extracted from ASM DIA029 on 04.11.2022 and purified along with CL1000 flasks that were started 19.10.2022 as a backup in case any unexpected events would adversely influence the ASM production.
Root cause description: Access of service workers from outside of Curida Diatec without supervision. The dashboard is also not labeled in a clear, informative way. Loss of necessary power to keep production alive and lack of a remote alert system for major deviations to available staff.

Non Conformance Title: The maintenance and monitoring of suppliers are not meeting the requirements of Diatec Supplier Quality Program SOP5026 rev. 2.0
Trend review not performed for refrigerator (DIA227) in clean room- Q3 2022
Impact Other: The operator involved had to use the extra time to search for the missing document and review the trend.
Investigation summary: This refrigerator's trend review document was missing which can possibly be misplaced. The system for placing sirius documents in their respective folders can lead to misplacing of documents.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance did not have any impact on product and product quality.  The Sirius logged data were reviewed on 09.11.2022 and no deviation related to the data trend was observed. Sirius system are checked by operators routinely for alarms or malfunctions. However, such non-conformance can always pose a risk of not detecting the trend of instrument failure if the maintenance routine is not followed.
Root cause description: System for archiving sirius trend review is not sufficient to maintain proper control over documents.

Non Conformance Title: Harvesting started earlier than expected in the pre-inoculation phase
Investigation summary: Mistyping from an operator during Starting of AcuSyst Maximizer process led to the incident. The harvest delay time was set to 5 instead of 50 days.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance occurred before inoculation of cells. So there was no production while the incident occured.  The harvest pump pumps the harvest out from the flowpath and nothing was pumped towards the flowpath. So less chance of contamination in the flowpath.  After the incident was discovered, the harvest pump was stopped and pressure was released from the harvest tube by opening clamp to a flask. The tube was aseptically cut off at the swollen region and reconnected as the swollen region could have been fragile. This was performed to ensure avoiding leakage during run. In addition, the swelling occurred next to the rigid peristaltic pump tubing. So, cutting only a small region of harvest tube was enough and the rest of the tube was easily reconnected to the peristaltic pump tubing. Cutting a small part of the harvest tubing didn't impact connection to harvest flask because the harvest flask is kept on the under shelf of the same table and the tubing is extra long when manufactured.   Then the harvest pump was run at 100 ml/hour for an hour to wash the tubing with medium which was collected in a flask. This ensures that the tube was properly flushed after the cut and connect procedure.   The ASM was followed closely until inoculation on 18.05.2022. No leakage or infection were detected before the inoculation.  This non-conformance, therefore, doesn't have any impact on the production or product quality.
Root cause description: Mistyping in ASM can occur during the procedure and the SOP doesn't have a step to double check what was typed in.

Non Conformance Title: Failed to complete weekly maintenance of LAF bench
DIA285 freezer out of temperature specifications
Investigation summary: No critical samples or reagents are stored in the freezer, the smallest of the vials was not fully thawed after the temperature increase.
Impact Assessment: The impact of the reagents is low, they are frequently thawed and frozen during production.
Root cause description: Operator did not close the freezer door properly.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA worksheet misplaced
Investigation summary: Missing in process analysis in batch documentation for B8248, B8389 and B8211. The missing document for B8248 and B8389 was noticed during batch review, but was not possible to locate. The same document is relevant for B8211, but  it was not discovered during release of B8211 that the worksheet was missing. This proves weaknesses in document handling regarding ELISA  in process analysis, and the batch review of the same documents.
Impact Assessment: The ELISA analysis is an in process" analysis that's performed during production to estimate the production time-line. B8248, B8389 and B8211 are terminated and finalized, a missing in-process analysis will have no effect on the product. The results from ELISA analysis is used together with different measured parameters, such as cell number or glucose concentration.   However, this deviation is an error in our systems ability to uphold the tractability of documentation and its corresponding raw data."
Root cause description: Root cause is our systems failure to locate missing document, by ensuring its traceability and/or information available to operator performing batch review.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA worksheet not a part of final batch review
Investigation summary: For batch B8326 it was one analysis present in the Batch documentation, however, 2 ELISAs runs were performed on B8326, the first on H2 and H5, the second on H5 and H8. Unlike all other assays performed at the ISO-facility, ELISA analysis is not logged in our run-number register with appropriate information. due to this,  it is difficult to know if all in process analysis are present with the batch documentation. Documentation is in constant use, and it can be challenging to find the correct pocket for the documents. As the ELISA documents for B8326 was discovered with another batch of documentation, this is determined to be the main root cause.
Impact Assessment: The ELISA analysis is an in process" analysis that's performed during production to estimate the production time-line. B8326 is finalized and released, and all QC testing were within specification, including final concentration of product. However this deviation is an error in our systems ability to uphold tractability of documentation."
Root cause description: Main root cause is determined to be the amount of documentation in use at the same time belonging to different projects, and can easily be misplaced with another project.  Contributing root cause is the lack of system to ensure detectability and traceability between in-process performed and its documentation prior to batch release.

Non Conformance Title: Clean room not washed with Peroxide week 47
Wrong information in Project specification regarding serum amount in IC medium
Investigation summary: Information on serum amount in PS only relevant for CL1000 production, while both CL1000 and maximizers were used. Production team did not read the PS.
Impact Assessment: The correct amount of serum used for maximizer run is 0.5%, as is stated by SOP2016. Since operators used 0.5% serum on autopilot despite instructions in PS, the correct amount of serum was used and this has no impact on production or product quality of batches produced by B8385. Furthermore, increasing the serum to 1% for IC compartment has no impact on the production itself, as 2D2 clone grown in CL1000 uses 1% outer medium (standard for most CL1000 productions). To use 0.5% serum in tanks prepared for maximizers is a buisiness decision as serum is expensive and the molecules do not cross the membrane between EC and IC side, using more than 0.5% is considered a waste.
Root cause description: Root cause is determined to be non-optimal information in PS which can cause confusion and the amount of people involved in writing the PSs without any standard way of making the document.

Non Conformance Title: Microclave on AcuSyst sampling lines not changed in time
Investigation summary: Microclave not changed within timeframe, due to non-optimal documentation and lack of system to ensure such tasks are performed.
Impact Assessment: No leakage or infection has been observed and EVE reported after visual and functional inspection that the replaced microclave was still functioning as it should. Therefore no future batches produced from B8385 are impacted from this Non conformance, including B8449.
Root cause description: Non optimal documentation and lack of system for followup

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect version of SOP1007 documented in ELISA worksheet
Investigation summary: All standard Batches used on these runs was approved and therefor no diversion from previous version 2,0
Impact Assessment: There were two changes made in v03 from v02 of SOP1007 1.  Pt. 8.2.2: Added Previously approved batch" to specify use of an approved batch as a standard. However, previously approved batches were used as a standard for both B8449 and B8412, .  2. Pt.9.11.1: Changed to LAB instead of FOU. LAB disk was used as this was already in use at the time and raw data was saved in the correct place. ELISA for both batches was an in process control, where only yield is estimated from the results. Both final batches B8449 and B8412 are within yield specification according to their product specification. Therefore this Non conformance has no impact on product quality or production."
Root cause description: The lab's responsibilities for handling authorized copies are defined in SOP 5042 section 5.2.7. The team leader holds responsibilities for switching documents in laboratory binders when a new SOP is released. The team leader should delegate the task to the Production team, however, the system is not well followed and seems not robust.  The task is not followed up when a team leader is absent from work.   Root cause: a not robust system for switching document copies in the lab. The document system process lacks a notification or remminder system which will notify the production team. The current system requires that the team leader follow up on document updates but creat a risk that task is not followed when a team leader is absent.

Non Conformance Title: Customer audit, Calpro AS: Temperature control system
ELISA QC Analysis saved as file E6613 instead of E6123
ELISA washer used without sufficient cleaning-buffer
Broken instrument not labelled according to the procedure
Investigation summary: DIA081, a broken instrument, was not labeled according to SOP5043 Version 4.0. Investigation into the issue showed that there was no training in responsibilities and labeling of equipment outside of initial R&U. Equipment was not used despite the lack of proper labelling. DIA 081 was labeled and removed from the premisses following this observation.
Impact Assessment: DIA081 was not used despite the lack of proper labelling, hence there is no impact to any production or product.
Root cause description: Discussion with involved operators show that training in SOP5043 is not sufficient. Since several operators were involved in handling of the failed equipment and no concerns was raised about labelling it. As most involved personnel are relatively new additions to the team, the proper understanding of roles and responsibilities within SOP5043 was not clear. The current introductory training in SOP5043 will need to be re-evaluated, since no specific training module exists for this procedure. Root cause is therefore considered to be lack of understanding in SOP5043 and corrective action is proper training of all production personnel in SOP5043, including roles, responsibilities and status of equipment (CAPA117), as well as a new training module will be initiated to ensure all future employees receive this training (CAPA127).

Non Conformance Title: Instrument in guarantee used in the production process.
Investigation summary: Quarantined equipment was temporarily used to store EC-medium and harvest from B8394 when minibar DIA081 was defect. The storage itself does not breach procedure since there is no requirements for storage conditions in protocol. However the use of quarantined equipment breach with our procedures. Team leader decided to use use the equipment, without opening a temporary change.
Impact Assessment: There is no risk to product quality from this deviation. Both the harvest and EC-medium has no requirements for its storage during production as was assessed in QRM-2020-129, however, it is considered more beneficial for crude to be stored cool, and final product storage temperature is usually 2-8 degrees. In general most refrigerators operate in 2-10 degrees C.
Root cause description: The lack of understanding surrounding usage of change management within our QMS system. A similar non_conformance lead to CAPA92 being created. This is deemed a sufficient preventive action to this non conformance.

Non Conformance Title: Failed to do Weekly maintenance on time for incubator
Suspected infection in CL1000 (B8405)
Investigation summary: Contamination was confirmed on streaked out settle plates by Curida Diatec QC Services.  Contamination is most likely the common human skin-related bacteria Staphylococcus or Streptococcus due to the yellowish hue and turbidity of the outer medium. The most plausible cause of this contamination is during the handling of the flask.
Impact Assessment: JSS 03.01.2023 Infection was discovered 23.12.2022. No other infection was discovered in near-laying CL1000 flasks at that time or since that time. Both Staphylococcus and Streptococcus (the most likely contaminating agents) are non-sporulating and are very sensitive to the cleaning agents used in our lab (ethanol and peroxide). There is, therefore very low risk of any further re-contamination derived from the infection examined in this NC. Product quality: The previously harvested crude from B8405 was pooled together with other harvests from 2D2 CL1000 to produce batch B8455 prior to the discovery of infection. As the operators are trained in the detection of infection (as apparent by the detection of infection in B8405), there is a low risk of the unit being contaminated at the time of the last harvesting. Furthermore, even if there was a beginning of infection the elution at a low pH would destroy any growth in the product. Finally, the 0,2 µm filtration would eliminate any possibility of microorganisms growing in the final product. Therefore infection of the final product is considered highly unlikely, and product quality is not affected. Production timeline: The affected CL1000 flask was discarded. The flask was in one of the final weeks of production and, combined with the other lines of production, should have enough product for a customer. No need to start new production.
Root cause description: JSS 03.01.2023 Not able to identify a root cause. Most likely due to accidental strafing during handling of CL1000 harvest or medium exchange.

Non Conformance Title: Medium number M221109A used twice.
Investigation summary: Wrong labelling of the medium bottle used for CL1000 outer medium. Most likley root cause is the use of excel spreadsheet which does not secure traceability of changes.
Impact Assessment: This occurrence has no impact on the process or the product quality. Medium used was correct, but labeled incorrect.
Root cause description: Most likley root cause is the use of excel spreadsheet which does not secure traceability of changes.

Non Conformance Title: SDS analysis for 2D2 product displayed smaller band than expected
Investigation summary: Results for SDS-Page with running number SDS637 were out of expectation compared to previous batches, where each band was approximately 120 kDa in size instead of  earlier batches have been around 175 kDa in size. Investigation determined that the product is within specification (150 kDa +/-) as the range is not determined and that the criteria does not reflect the possible variation in method.
Impact Assessment: No impact on production or product quality is identified, as this is related to poorly defined criteria with an undefined range.   Both batches, test and reference, show similar mobility, around 125 kDa, when compared with the precision standard used. Therefore, product failure can be excluded in this investigation.   The precision standard used for SDS-PAGE runs consists of a set of proteins that may not migrate exactly like the tested product. The migration of proteins in SDS-PAGE depends primarily on their molecular weight. Factors such as protein conformation or how SDS binds can also slightly influence migration. Molecular weight markers (precision standards) migrate according to their size and structure, therefore, minor differences in mobility between the tested product and the standard are expected and do not indicate a difference in actual molecular weight.   Criteria for product purity are currently defined as ±150 kDa in the Product Specification. These criteria are not well defined, as they do not specify an acceptable range around the nominal value. The defined limits do not account for method-associated variability and can affect the interpretation of results. It should be highlighted that SDS-PAGE is not a quantitative method for protein size determination and provides only an approximate estimate of molecular weight.
Root cause description: Test and reference batch batches have identical mobility, around 125 kDa, compared to the used precision standard. Therefore, product failure can be excluded in this investigation. The criteria for the method are not well defined, which causes the product to be initially interpreted out of specification by operators.,

Non Conformance Title: Tanks for maximizer prepared without filter and autoclaving the tubing used.
Investigation summary: Due to shortage of filter, operator decided to follow used, but not documented approach  for tank preparation. The approach was used in production for long time, first the shortage was first experienced. However the actual date can not be confirmed as the change was not raised at the time. The fact that the change was not raised resulted in the procedure not being updated. What is more, according to procedure for documentation management all procedures should be reviewed periodically and the approach to be adjusted if needed. However the procedure had not been fully revised for several years (only immediate updates performed). At the lab meeting no concerns were raised using this approach as it was used in the last few years. It is not clear to production team which have to be initiated according to change management procedure.
Impact Assessment: Production was monitored for microbiological growth, Double samples were taken from IC, EC medium from B8421 and B8394 and harvest medium for B8385, and incubated and there was no sign of infection. There is no impact on product quality or production.
Root cause description: Change control not initiated the first time production experienced shortage of filters. Therefore this approach was not incorporated into the procedure at the time. All the operators receive training in change management as apart of their introduction training into the quality system, however this training seems not to be sufficient. Furthermore, document was not revised periodically which indicated that the process is not working. Finally the production team consists on newly hired personnel, including the head of department, therefore concerns were not raised when issue was brought up.

Non Conformance Title: Extra bands for SDS control B5596 clone 5G8. Run SDS626 for B8388.
Change CMR20 implemented before QA Approval
Performed reinoculation of ASM flowpath (B8256)
Investigation summary: AcuSyst maximizer run B8215 was unsuccessful with low cell growth and poor yield. It was decided in accordance with production management to perform re-inoculation, a procedure not described in SOP0060. The need for a change management report was not identified at the time and due to time constrains maximiser was re-inoculation. A change management report should have been initiated to include the process in our procedures.
Impact Assessment: Performing re-inoculation, a non-approved procedure increases the risk of it being performed incorrectly. Re-inoculation is identical to inoculation which is included in our procedures, and the manual explains re-inoculation. Several of the AcuSyst team were present when procedure was performed. SBA and IKM are experienced operators, in addition one operator under training also observed the procedure Therefore, training of operators was not considered necessary to perform re-inoculation. To clarify any questions operators had before performing the re-inoculation, a representative from Cell Culture Company, was contacted by phone..  The risk of infection is increased due to more steps in production, however all involved operators are trained and experienced in aseptic work as well as spotting and handling infection. No infection was detected during production of 1A12 B8256. The product quality is not impacted by performing the re-inoculation since all criteria for inoculation was met. All parameters during production was within acceptance criteria. And all IPC and QC analysis was according to technical requirements and product specification. All parameters in B8256 also surpassed that of B8215 concerning productivity, which is a testament to the cells and therefore the products quality.
Root cause description: Use of multiple documents during Acusyst production, including manual, leads to confusion on when it is acceptable to use manual vs SOP. This together with a lack of understanding of the change control process lead to the process being performed without the necessary approvals and documentation.  This is the second time within a short time period that production works outside our protocols without an approved change management report (Ref: NC_98). This further substantiates the lack of understanding of our QMS system as well as the necessity for training in change management. CAPA92, mandatory training in change management for all employees is created as a preventative measure.

Non Conformance Title: Water leakage from roof within Diagnostics lab area
Impact Other: Minor impact on resources used in cleaning of contaminated area
Investigation summary: Leakage of dirty water from the roof of Technopolis building down to the Diagnostic area and lab, due to heavy snow melting combined with increased rainfall.
Impact Assessment: The area in analytical lab exposed to leakage is used for vialing and purification. The vialing of products and cellwork are done in the LAF, including 0,2 µm filtration. This minimizes the possibility of contamination. The leakage nearby the personnel locks leading into the cleanroom have the potential to lead to contamination of cleanroom.  However, both analytical lab and cleanroom area are monitoried for microbial growth every week according to SOP1076 Environment monitoring. There have been no findings exceeding the alarm or action limits in this time period within either lab area, and no increase in infection in production has been observed. therefor this has no impact on product quality or timelines. As more cleaning and some refurbishing was required to mitigate the risk of contamination, there was a minor impact on resources.
Root cause description: The weather had started warming up, in combination with increased precipitation. The melting snow combined with the increased rainfall may have slipped through the structural integrity of the Technopolis building, entering both the office area and the working Diagnostics lab area.

Non Conformance Title: CO2 deviation on DIA025
Investigation summary: High CO2 levels detected. Service personell discovered that white cap on CO2 sensor was removed by accident, and there was a leakage from the CO2 tube. White cap was replaced back on the CO2 sensor and CO2 tube was replaced. All CO2 values appear stable and within acceptance criteria.
Impact Assessment: High CO2 levels can lead to acidulous medium which can ultimately kill the cells and affect the production timeline. The cells was inspected under microscope and was concluded to be healthy. CL1000 flask was also inspected for color change, the color was red indicating optimal pH. Hence, the hydrogen carbonate buffer within the medium maintained the pH despite the conditions in the incubator. Production continued as normal with no delay to the timeline, hence no impact was identified from this non conformance.
Root cause description: Faulty CO2 tube and removal of white cap over CO2 sensor were the identified root cause for the unstable CO2 values within the incubators. Instrument has been repaired, operators informed of the findings and as this is non conformance is a on-off occurrence no further actions are deemed necessary.

Non Conformance Title: DIA143 CO2 supply goes outside instruments acceptance criteria
Investigation summary: DIA143 malfunctioned and reached high CO2 levels, the display did not match Sirius data. The Sirius data was validated with reference values. The contents of the incubator was moved and inspected through microscope and by visual inspection of media color. The cells did not appear to be effected by exposure to high levels of CO2. Service personnel is contacted for more information.
Impact Assessment: High CO2 levels can lead to acidulous medium which can ultimately kill the cells and affect the production timeline. The cells was inspected under microscope and was concluded to be healthy. CL1000 flask was also inspected for color change, the color was red indicating optimal pH. Hence, the hydrogen carbonate buffer within the medium maintained the pH despite the conditions in the incubator. Production continued as normal with no delay to the timeline, hence no impact was identified from this non conformance.
Root cause description: CO2 incubator DIA143 malfunctioned with non representative display values and increasing CO2 above acceptance criteria. Service is necessary to conclude the instruments defect (See CAPA116). The equipment was purchased in June 2015 and the last two years two non conformances was identified. NCR2079 and NCR2058. Only NCR2058 was equipment error, due to a broken CO2 sensor. Apart from these deviation, DIA143 performs as expected. DIA143 and all equipment like it is serviced once a year and equipment data reviewed every quarter. No risk is identified at this moment and routine is deemed robust enough to identify any trend concerning equipment malfunction.

Non Conformance Title: Pipette DIA121 failed As found" before 6 month service control"
Continue decreased use of disinfectants, continue skipping wiping with peroxide before harvest of CL1000.
AcuSyst maximizer production B8421 run with empty medium tank
Impact Other: The production timeline is delayed due to time and resources used for additional monitoring, risk mitigation, as well as time for the production to recover. From when the deviation occurred to all parameters had normalized took approximately 1 week. in despite of this, the product can still be delivered on schedule.
Investigation summary: B8421 was without medium for approximately 31 minutes, pH was outside acceptance criteria for 21 minutes and the pH reached 6,06 before the medium was reinstated. Several actions were taken to ensure production and mitigate the risk associated with the deviation. Including increased monitoring to establish the impact of this deviation on the production, separation of product to quarantine until the impact was established and segregation of pre- and post-deviation harvest. Cell removal was performed to minimize the effects of the dead cells that accumulated in the bioreactor.
