Supplementation of bioreactors was done to supply the cells with nourishment and growth signals to counteract the pro-apoptotic signals from dying cells. a AcuSyst-team meeting was organized to establish a root cause and identify weaknesses in the routing. The data show that all production parameters dip before re-establishing to a level comparable with the same parameters before the deviation. Root cause investigation established that a calculation error lead to the deviation. Further, the team meeting unveiled that routine is good enough, but tools for the calculation of tank volume would be useful and preventative for deviations like this.
Impact Assessment: Data obtained through additional monitoring of the production unit indicates that the impact of this non conformance on product quality is minimal. B8421 was monitored frequently for about one week after the incident, and the data collected support that production is comparable to before the non-conformance occurred. Cells are viable, in high concentration, and produce expected amounts of antibodies.  Two harvest bottles were connected to the production during the incident (H5) and the monitoring time after (H6). H5 was segregated from production 1 hour and 16 minutes after the incident occurred, with data collected in the day following the incident showing that the production was not affected until 24 hours later. The H5 collected 11,25ml of crude produced under non-optimal conditions. This is only a fraction of the total volume of crude (750ml) and as there is a natural delay from the production of the antibodies within the bioreactors, until they reach the harvest, the product quality of H5 is not affected by this deviation. H6 was harvested during the investigation of this non-conformance, where conditions were not as optimal as usual in this phase of the production. Cells are clearly affected by medium restriction, but this is reflected in the antibody production not the antibody quality. All parameters suggest no impact to neither product quality nor production vitality. The risk of releasing H6 from quarantine is considered low and will be mitigated by QC testing for purity and specificity at the end of production, which ensures product quality and functionality. Hence, H6 can be released from quarantine. Later harvests are likewise unaffected and are not subject to this deviation since all parameters had recovered before H6 was substituted. As a consequence of this non-conformance, the production timeline is negatively impacted as more products would have been expected to be harvested if the medium supply had not been restricted, and more resources were needed for the additional monitoring and troubleshooting described in the investigation section of this non-conformance. From when the deviation occurred to all parameters had normalized took approximately 1 week. Despite this, the product will be delivered according to schedule, therefore this non-conformance has no impact on the delivery timeline.
Root cause description: The investigation concludes that a calculation error was performed when estimating tank volume and production media consumption prior to weekend. SOP0060 is vague on the process of securing medium supply and medium consumption calculation is a manual process easily subject to errors, without any traceability of the calculations performed. A similar event occurred on 26.01.2023, this is documented in NC_109. This reinforces the need for guidelines in SOP0060 and tools to aid in handling AcuSyst medium supply. SOP0060 is currently under revision for CAPAID-58 and a request is added to Proposed changes document to include more information about this process.

Non Conformance Title: Errors in CAPA module in BizzMine
Investigation summary: Several errors in the CAPA module in BizzMine, such as problems with saving/recording new records or approval of the record, were observed. The software supplier was contacted to investigate a problem. It was concluded that errors were caused mainly due to the unpeace of the software user, both in the phase of workflow implementation and daily use of the software.
Impact Assessment: The deviation doesn't have an impact on product quality or user safety. However, it impacted business as several working hours were used to investigate the root cause of occurred errors. The nonconformance also has an impact on compliance as 23, 33, 34, 35, 41, 44, and 47 were not executed correctly, and the admin has to execute steps on behalf of assigned employees.
Root cause description: The inexperienced user executes the development and validation step of the new collection workflow in BizzMine for the CAPA process. Anna Huk received Admin training for BizzMine users, which included bundling a new process workflow. However, during the creation of the new CAPA workflow, she was still not very experienced. This resulted in a new workflow containing a bug which impacted several CAPA records. A similar event did not occur after this deviation. The new collection for internal audit, supplier audit, non-coordination handling and supplier qualification was designed and implemented in 2023, and no deviation related to collection performance/errors was reported.  The BizzMine validation protocol was also updated to ensure that possible errors will be detected during the validation phase. An additional person was also trained in the validation of the BizzMine workflow to decrease the number of potential errors.

Non Conformance Title: Only one of the clamp was opened during changing the tank for maximizer
Drain pipe leakage in coldroom
Investigation summary: Pedro consulting located the leakage and applied sealant to stop the leakage. After two weeks, no further leakage had occurred. Environment monitoring uncovered a small increase in bacterial growth, but within allowed limits.
Impact Assessment: Acusyst production, harvest or media is not affected by the drain leakage. Outflow is connected to the non-sterile drain and the fluids are leaving the machine. There is no additional risk to sterility associated with the leakage. The outflow fluid could cause fungi and bacteria to accumulate in the cold-room, to mitigate this risk spor-klens was applied. This was monitored by normal weekly environment monitoring. The data shows a small increase in bacterial growth which is now declining, always within allowed limits, normal environment monitoring continues, but no risk is identified. The sealant seems to be sufficient to block the leakage.
Root cause description: Weakness in soldering of drain system.

Non Conformance Title: Yearly calibration of Sirius sensors postponed for certain equipment
Investigation summary: All calibrations were performed within deadline.
Impact Assessment: No impact, since all deadlines were met.
Root cause description: NA

Non Conformance Title: CO2 level above threshold for incubator DIA156
Investigation summary: Comparable flasks revealed no difference between affected batches in regularly functioning incubators. Previous and comparable incidents of temporarily elevated CO2 levels in incubators for similar or different reasons has not had any visual impact on production. Change 19, change action 95 has already been suggested from earlier incidents to improve the monitoring system for keeping track of CO2 levels. A CAPA is suggested to decrease chance of similar events.
Impact Assessment: No impact on production quality as seen by color indicating media and comparison to previous and comparable non-conformances.
Root cause description: Noncompliance with SOP4008 stating that the protective cap shall be removed after cleaning. Lack of checkpoint in the control form documentation (DOC4008.1) to ensure that SOP4008 is being followed.

Non Conformance Title: Chemical for 2D2 custom buffer was expired
Investigation summary: Operators did not recognize the expiration date indicated on chemical container label, when making of some buffer lots. This happened during a period of 5 weeks, 17 buffer lots were made.
Impact Assessment: Buffer lots were made with material that was expired, overdue was from 2 weeks (first buffer in table) to 7 weeks (last buffer in table). The shelf life of actual chemical is 5 years (attachment). The chemical is used for making buffers according to SOP2041 2D2 custom buffer. The expire date for this new buffer lot is 1 year. The expired chemical has no visible degradation, it looks identical to newly opened valid lot. of same cat. The purpose of the chemical Sodium dihydrogenphosphateXH2O" is to build buffer capacity into buffer. Expired material did not need more pH adjustment than previous buffer lots, which indicates that the functionality of the chemical was not compromised. There are no indication of degradation of product in the buffers containing the expired chemical. Final production step progressed as expected, no observations (visible or documented parameters) differed from products finalized a few weeks before.  Batches B8455 and B8473 were released in January 2023. Batch B8456 has not been released yet.Products delivered in this 2D2 custom buffer are considered not very stable since the buffer is very low in salt, not to be stored as products containing PBS (stable for many years). And this is well known by manufacturer and customer, so these products are not for storing up to a year. Info from sales indicates that these batches are put into production at customer site soon after delivery. Buffers used in manufacturing batch B8456: 2mMSPB230103A to 2mMSPB230104A (as listed). Impact will be the same as for other buffers listed, and other final product batches. Harvest crude for the batch B8456 was produced in ASM Batch run B8449 during a peroid logged with non conformance issues (NC_77)."
Root cause description: System for registration and use of chemicals does not facilitate awareness of expiry date.

Non Conformance Title: DIA156 outside CO2% acceptance criteria
Greasy leakage from ASM (DIA079)
Impact Other: The incident led to termination of the batch, slightly earlier (ca. 1 week earlier) than estimated. Since the batch was nearing its end, this did not have a significant impact on production timeline.
Investigation summary: Based on suggestion from the supplier/service provider, the leakage could have been from the circulation motor bracket or cold air return. The incident is a random failure on instrument, which is very rare, but such situations cannot be fully avoided.
Impact Assessment: The ASM was running for production of the batch B8421. The batch had not shown any abnormal sign druing run (like leakage from the flowpath which the supplier was concerned about) before this incident. The batch was already towards the end of the production run according to the parameters (cell count, cell viability, glucose intake, pH, EC pressure and antibody production per day) during in-process control. So, to minimize further risk to the instrument, it was decided, by the production manager together with the team, to terminate production of this batch. Most likely the bath would have been terminated on the base of its parameters the following days. Therefore this non conformance had no impact on production timeline.  Production with ASM occurs in hollow fiber bioreactor within a closed system. Cells are grown in hollow fiber bioreactors and ASM maintains the optimal environment for the cells. The status of cell culture in the bioreactor is routinely controlled during in-process control (three times per week). If the incident have had any impact on the cell culture or the product, it would have been observed as an abnormality during the in-process control. No effects of the leakage was observed on the cell culture before or after the leakage in the in-process parameters. Even with the non-conformance, the ASM was able to maintain optimal environment for the cells. Therefore the incident did not impact the product quality of the batch.
Root cause description: Instrument was moved from GMP site to ISO site in 2022 which could have extra impact on the instrument during transport. Even though the instrument was calibrated after moving to ISO site, calibration is not a comprehensive maintenance routine. The wear on the machine since the last preventive maintenance could have possibly had some effect to the non-conformance. However, the instrument is quite old and the possiblity of instrument failure which would not be detected during Preventive maintenance cannot be neglected.

Non Conformance Title: SDS647 for batch B8508 and B8507, impurity on gel recognized as band.
DIA001 used as an alternative when DIA003 malfunction
Investigation summary: DIA001 was used for one ELISA in process analysis (E6056), when awaiting repair of DIA003. The analysis is of the same technical quality as is expected and samples are repeated the previous and following week with comparable results. Non conformaty was commented in worksheet, but not documented before QA requested it.
Impact Assessment: The impact of this non conformance is minimal as the production of B8256 continued as expected, and produced the intended amount. The minor adjustments on maximizer parameters as a results of this assay poses no risk to either product quality of the production itself. These results was also substantiated by the preceding and following ELISA runs. Altough DIA001 was intended for OD meassurements of final and intermediary product. The purpose description states that: BioTek Epoch is a spectrophotometer for measuring absorbance at wavelengths 200-999nm and direct reading of concentration on samples in many different formats, including cuvettes, microplates and microspots" (SOP4012 Version 2.0). All our ELISA procedures falls under this description, but none of them includes DIA001 as a backup instrument. CAPA92 has been initiated to provide the laboratory team with training in change management to correctly document working outside our procedures if that is nessesary. CAPA120 is after this non conformance registered to change our ELISA procedures to include DIA001 as a backup instrument."
Root cause description: SOP1007 in process ELISA protocol specifies the use of DIA003. Hence, there is no safeguard for instrument failure, even though the analysis is routinely run every week. The use of DIA001 should be permitted by our procedures to have options if the primary instrument malfunctions. Since the DIA001 is capable of reading plates under the same conditions as DIA003 it can be used as a backup if the protocol allows for it (CAPA120). This non conformance was raised long after the fact because of a lack of understanding regarding when non conformance reports or change management reports should be opened (CAPA92). Finally, the procedure for handling broken instruments does not clearly outline how to report and handle instrument malfunction (CAPA59)

Non Conformance Title: Used more FBS than stated in the PS
Investigation summary: Operator proposed to add more FBS to the initial thaw medium as well as drop the centrifugal step due to poor cell growth in earlier thaw. The cells grew better under these conditions.  SOP does not include flexibility for difficult and/or poor-growing cell-lines.
Impact Assessment: It is unlikely that the added FBS at the beginning of thawing had any negative impact on product or productive ability of the cells. The effect of elevated levels of FBS is hard to quantify as this can greatly vary from cell-lines and the production lot of the FBS. The 20% FBS environment was only an initial endeavor to help the cells reinvigorate after being thawed from liquid nitrogen. The HyQ ADCF medium used in expansion of the cells is serum-free, the amount of FBS during thawing would quickly be diluted away during the very first few cell splits of expansion (see SOP0001 part 9.1.7-9.1.8).
Root cause description: Giving poorly growing cells increased amounts of FBS has been current practice for a long time according to senior production operator, despite being outside of the specific guidelines of the SOP.

Non Conformance Title: Microclave on AcuSyst sampling lines not changed in time
Incorrect dilution used during ELISA run
Dialysis under the minimum allowed duration for FPLC B8539 and B8542
Comunication error for JRI Logger DIA310 transmitting CO2 data from incubator DIA022
Investigation summary: CO2 data from DIA022 was not transmitted to the sirius cloud from 24.03.2023 11:2 to 03.04.2023 15:03. Loggers going offline is a reoccuring issue without a clear cause, that among other thing has lead to SOP4032 revision according to CMID19.
Impact Assessment: Data from before and after the data gap show the incubator performing according to acceptance criteria. Temperature data from the same incubator show that the incubator was functioning during the time of deviation when concerning electricity and temperature (Ref: Attachment 2). DIA022 did not have any internal alarms triggered during the periode, neither on CO2 threshold or other alarms. All cell cultures present in the incubator at the time of incident performed as expected concerning growth, viability and concentration of cells  where inoculation was performed within expected timelines where applicable. Cells are also observed under the microscope during entire culturing and no concernes was raised by operators performing this. The majority of involved productions use RPMI1640 as cell culture medium which has color metric as a pH indicator. A visual inspection is also performed  when culturing cells and no concernes was raised here either. If the CO2% was either to high or to low, medium would change color, if the color is ideal (Red) the hydrogen carbonate buffer within the medium maintained the pH. All of the available evidence support the claim that DIA022 performed av usual during the time periode with missing data.and therefore this non conformance has no impact on production timeline or product quality.
Root cause description: The JRI logger DIA310 malfuntioned and stopped transmitting data. Communication errors such as this are not included in SOP4032. Sirius Super users are not currently allerted by such alarms, and the procedure does not explain how to handle such incidences. Several actions are under progress according to CMID19 in an effort to minimize, monitoring and handle communication errors. Therefore no further actions will be taken as a result of this non-conformance.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage from flowpath in ASM
Investigation summary: To prevent bellow pump leakage during on-going production, the supplier suggested to run the CP at maximum speed (1000 ml/hr) for 7-10 days in pre-inoculation phase before inoculating the cells. The procedure suggested by the supplier was followed. In this batch, leakage was discovered on day 10 during pre-inoculation phase. When the bellow pump was observed after unmounting from the ASM, physical wear on the bellow pump could be visually seen (ref. IMG_1481). Thus, it was found that the leakage occurred due to wear on the bellow pump. Different tests were performed to investigate the cause of the non-conformance based on the operators' experience as well as the suggestions from the supplier.
Impact Assessment: The batch was under pre-inoculation phase when the non-conformance was discovered. The non-conformance occurred on day 10 in pre-inoculation phase. So, the non-conformance delayed the planned production timelines. The project was started with a good timeframe with regards to the product delivery date. The non-conformance increased the time pressure to deliver the product. Nevertheless, the product delivery timeline was not impacted and the product was delivered on time.  The cell culture and the cultureware prepared for inoculation had to be discarded due to the non-conformance. The non conformance led to loss of financial and human resources used for the batch and the non-conformance.  Since the non-conformance occurred before production started, no product is involved with this incidence and therefore no impact on product quality.
Root cause description: According to the supplier, the likelihood for flexing of the bellow pump is very low. Several tests were performed on ASM and the results points towards very low likelihood of failure in CP.   As mentioned above in the investigation section, there is low chance for improper mounting of a pump in the CP, or manufacturing defect of bellow pump. However, they cannot be completely ruled out.   Several tests were performed in coordination with the supplier, production operator and R&D representative to identify the cause of the non-conformance. The root cause was narrowed down to the manufacturing defect of the below pump, which will have to be inspected by the Supplier in the form of the supplier complain.

Non Conformance Title: Contamination discovered in CL1000 flask for 1A12
Investigation summary: Suspected infection in flask led to the CL1000 unit B8496 being discarded. QC analysis found the samples to be negative for bacterial or fungal growth, indicating that the contamination of outer medium was cells from the 1A12/1/4 batch, supported by visual examination of the culture.    Suspected leakage of cell-compartment membrane, possibly around the connective joints used for harvests.
Impact Assessment: As this was confirmed not to be microbial contamination, this non-conformance does not impact product quality. One CL1000 was terminated early, which constitutes a loss of crude harvest volume upto 130mL, potentially more if the production would be allowed to continue beyond H21. As 1A12 is in continuous production, produced for Abbott Dundee on forecast, the discarding of the flask had no impact on production timeline.
Root cause description: Leakage from the cell compartment to the outer chamber. Possibly around the harvest port resulting from loss of membrane structural integrity over time combined with general wear and tear.

Non Conformance Title: Monthly maintenance of pipetboys in cleanroom one day delayed
Maintenance of refrigerator was performed one day over due.
Expired 100x Sodium azide reagent used for EC samples
Investigation summary: The vial containing the reagent was not labeled correct and the operator using this reagent was not checking the marking on the vial carefully enough.
Impact Assessment: The expired sodium azide solution have exclusively been added in some EC samples from Maximizers and Xcellerator. The results from the measurements are used to determine the harvest pump rate to aim for a specific concentration of antibody in the final harvest. Before the product is released the concentration of final harvest is measured again, and the previous measurements is no longer taken into account. The sample used for the measurements of the final product does not have the relevant sodium azide solution added to it. This non conformance has therefore no impact on any product released.  Sodium Azide is added to prevent microorganisms to grow in the sample before the sample is used for antibody concentration measurement. The samples are stored in the cold room for a maximum of 2-3 months before they are discarded. No signs of contaminated EC samples have been reported.
Root cause description: Inconclusive root cause. It is not possible to determine the root cause for this non conformance after such a long time.

Non Conformance Title: Missing CO2 data from DIA156
Investigation summary: Investigation show that a broken connection between sirius logger and sensor lead to loss of CO2 data from DIA156 between 11:37 11.04.2023 -16.04.2023 00:00. during this period several CL1000 were incubated in DIA156. Manual logging was utilized until new logger was installed and calibrated to DIA156. Temperature logging, incubators internal alarms, operators which used the equipment and pH color metric all point to the equipment functioning properly during the period data is missing from.
Impact Assessment: The majority of involved productions use RPMI1640 as cell culture medium which has color metric as a pH indicator. A visual inspection is also performed each time during harvesting of CL1000 and no concerns were raised. If the CO2% was either to high or to low over longer period of time, the medium would change pH and therefore color. As no such changes of color were reported, the normal pH level was maintained in all cultures.  The available evidence support the claim that DIA156 performed as usual during the time frame where data is absent. All involved productions performs as expected, therefore this non conformance has no impact on production timeline or product quality.
Root cause description: Losened cable fasteners lead to logger-sensor connection being broken during normal use of the incubator.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA- incorrect dilutions of samples
Unusual sound from circulation motor DIA203
Investigation summary: The ASM had relatively new circulation motor and was not found to have problems. The sound was most likely due to the ASM is an old instrument. However, the ASM appears to function optimally and has no other known issues.
Impact Assessment: The sound occurred most likely due to the age of the instrument. The sound was audible even when the CP was unmounted from the circulation motor. However, this was checked and confirmed to have no impact on its performance.  Moving of flowpath out from the ASM during Pre-Inoculation phase is less likely to have impact on the production batch. The flowpath was also kept as a closed system during this period preserving the sterility of the system. The flowpath was installed back into the ASM on 13.04.2023 where it was run in Pre-Inoculation phase until inoculation on 20.04.2023. The period from the first time the sound was heard to inoculation was at least 10 days. It was suggested by the supplier to run the CP at maximum speed for 7-10 days in pre-inoculation phase to confirm the integrity of CP. However, it should be noted that this is not the absolute criteria to confirm CP integrity throughout the run. This time period is deemed enough to confirm that circulation pump is functioning as it should and the incident did not compromise the sterility of the system.   The incidence delayed the planned inoculation by 2-3 days since it is recommended to run CP at maximum speed for at least 7-10 days before inoculation.
Root cause description: The sound is most likely due to age of instrument.

Non Conformance Title: Poor cellgrowth for B8532 in CL1000
Incorrect extinction coefficient in PS for project 2023-08
Customer audit: Internal auidit of QA deartment
Impact Other: No impact on resources, validation status or compliance with customer agreement was identified.
Investigation summary: The internal audit plan is defined each year during the management review. This is performed in Q1 of each year, according to SOP5016 management responsibilities The main criteria for selecting the area to be audited are the number of deviations reported for each process in the last year. As most of the reported deviations are related to the production activities (including upstream, downstream, quality control, facility and instruments), production processes were mainly inspected last year.
Impact Assessment: The deviation doesn't have any direct or indirect impact on product quality.
Root cause description: The non-conformance results from no well-defined process for conducting an internal inspection. The procedure doesn’t provide criteria for selecting organisation areas /processes for inspection. In addition, the procedure does not state that every process/organisation area should be inspected in the defined time period. This results in some of the internal processes being overlooked during the planning internal audit program. This topic was also addressed during the Certified Bodies Inspection in 2022, therefore, change control was initiated to revise the Audit program at Curida Diatec (refer to CMID 10 Implementation Audit module in eQMS BizzMine, current change step: Execution). In addition, this same aspect was observed during other customer audit conducted in Q2 2023 (refer to NCR43 Customer Audit: SOP5029 does not define the frequency of the internal audit program). As a result of this reported deviation, CAPA 32 “Update SOP5029 Audit: provide information regarding internal audit frequency.” was initiated and is currently ongoing execution.    In addition, a limited number of employees in the QA department and a lack of other personnel trained in an internal audit role contribute that the QA process was always overlooked during internal audit planning.

Non Conformance Title: Hybridoma cells discovered in outer media of CL1000 5E M5/4/5-8
Investigation summary: Cells observed in the outer medium that was changed during harvest. Hybridoma cells could be seen with microscope, suggesting a leak from the cell compartment.  CL1000 unit for B8529 was fully harvested before the bioreactor moved out of clean room and investigated for damage.
Impact Assessment: Since the turbidity originated from cells of the same cell line that is producing our desired antibody, the product does not suffer any impact on its quality.  The CL1000 unit could not be used further as a result from the presence of cells in the outer chamber. This is because the cells in the outer chamber will eat up nutrients that the cells in the cell compartment needs. Cells in the outer medium compartment could also potentially obscure operators' ability to discern whether the flask would become infected with bacteria at a later time. A loss of 1 of 5 CL1000 flasks could impact production timeline, however the clone produced more antibody than expected despite the loss of CL1000 unit B8529. Therefore this non-conformance has no impact.
Root cause description: Weakness in cell-compartment causing a leak of cell chamber.

Non Conformance Title: Hybridoma cells discovered in outer media of CL1000 931/6D7 3B1
Investigation summary: Cells observed in the outer medium that was changed during harvest. Hybridoma cells could be seen with microscope, suggesting a leak from the cell compartment.  CL1000 unit for B8529 was fully harvested before the bioreactor moved out of clean room and investigated for damage.
Impact Assessment: Since the turbidity originated from cells of the same cell line that is producing our desired antibody, the product does not suffer any impact on its quality.  The CL1000 unit could not be used further as a result from the presence of cells in the outer chamber. This is because the cells in the outer chamber will eat up nutrients that the cells in the cell compartment needs. Cells in the outer medium compartment could also potentially obscure operators' ability to discern whether the flask would become infected with bacteria at a later time. This loss of a CL1000 unit does hamper the overall productivity of the project and could have impacted the goal of producing enough product for the deadline. Considering the project produced more antibodies than anticipated, despite the loss of CL1000 unit B8535, this non-conformance has no impact.
Root cause description: Leakage from cell compartment of CL1000 units causes the growth of hybridoma cells in the outer medium compartment. We speculate that this is due to a weakness of the membrane around the harvest port for this CL1000 lot. Further investigation will be conducted by material supplier.

Non Conformance Title: Customer complain not follow up according to the internal procedure.
Investigation summary: As this complaint is still listed as CCRxxx in the folder for customer complaints, it is fair to believe it was overlooked by QA and the process stopped.  The QA personnel working on this in 2019 has left the company and therefore it is not possible to further investigate.
Impact Assessment: The case was resolved with the customer within a few weeks.  A replacement product was shipped.
Root cause description: Lack of routines to follow up a manual system for customer complaint handling.

Non Conformance Title: Unstable pH in ASM production
Impact Other: No impact on the production timeline and requested amount of product.
Investigation summary: The flowpath was taken out of the ASM and reinstalled during pre-inoculation phase. Even though the offline pH was close to set-point before inoculation, the pH started to deviate after inoculation. During the pre-inoculation phase, the medium pH is stable due to no other factors impacting pH. But after cells inoculation, the pH starts to fluctuate from the constant medium pH due to cellular activity. But the ASM was not registering the changes in the pH. Several troubleshooting was performed and the problem was solved by reconnecting the pH cable to the ASM.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance impacted the amount of daily antibody production. The table below shows the daily production of batch B8546 before and after the corrective measures as well as results on corresponding sampling days from previous 2D2 batches:           Batch            Day no.             Daily production (mg/day)                B7077            14            36.9                21            108.0                B8385            12            38.2                23            74.9                B8421            12            38.9                26            59.8                B8546            12            28.1                22            97.2          After the corrective measure was taken on 03.05.2023 (i.e. day 13 of production), the daily production increased and the trend continued as given in the figure below.  The quality of the product was not impacted and is confirmed by the Quality Control tests performed on the final product (see attachment: QC tests.pdf).
Root cause description: Root cause is considered to be an incorrect connection of pH probe to the instrument due to a non-routine operation (NC130). Most likely there was a small adjustment needed to completely lock the pH probe to the instrument that did not initially cause any detection by the instruments. When the instrument was running for a while, the vibration from the instrument can have caused it to loosen further.  The non-conformance is categorized other" the incident occured due to improper fine adjustment of the cable which was not possible to detect for neither the operator nor the instrument.     This is the first time when this type of error occurred, all operators are well-trained and performed probe installation several times the past."

Non Conformance Title: Pipette DIA119 failed As found" before 6 month service control"
Failed ELISA analyses not stored according to protocol
Investigation summary: All the respective analyses were repeated successfully the succeeding week. Delay in delivery of detergent used for reusable laboratory equipment lead to contamination of ELISA plates and consequently analyses failing technical requirements of the procedure. Lost documentation cannot be located, and this deviation will therefore trigger a review of our document handling procedure and routines.
Impact Assessment: The impact on the three projects is deemed to be non. The failed analyses were in process analysis and were repeated successfully, and had no impact on production planning or timeline. The deviation was caught by technical revision. No product is involved in this non-conformance hence, there is no impact to product quality. Measurements were obtained in the coming week and the production timeline was also unaffected.    Although this deviation and the previous non-conformances raised concerning document handling and traceability, insist on action. They are a testament to the need for updating our procedures, to retrain employes in the changes made to clarify their responsibilities, and streamline our document path to prevent misplacement. Therefore, CAPA137 is raised to initiate the advancement of the procedures and streamline this process.
Root cause description: Investigation shows that the root cause of the failed analyses was a delay in delivery of deconex, a human error is responsible for placing the reservoir to be used without washing them according to routine.   The missing document is more difficult to establish a clear cause. The best explanation the investigation purposes is document insufficiency. That the procedure for ELISA analysis could benefit from restructuring and optimizing readability, especially concerning handling of failed documents, and registration of run numbers. The procedure for document handling is missing responsibilities concerning maintaining tracebility of documents SOP5042 and our routine for handling documents can be streamlined.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong assembly of Bio-Rad Gel Doc for SDS Page
Sample diluted to wrong concentration for BIORAD Stainfree SDS
Final product 520 Ber-Ep4 with final concentration below specification
The noncoformance NC128 was approved without list all impacted projects
ELISA Standard Sample had no dilution factor
Wrong Batch number registered in NC122
Protective cap not removed from incubator DIA022 after monthly maintenance
Cells discovered in outer medium of CL1000 unit for cell line UCHT-1
Investigation summary: Cells observed in the outer medium that was changed during harvest. Hybridoma cells could be seen with microscope, suggesting a leak from the cell compartment.  CL1000 unit for B8544 was fully harvested before the bioreactor moved out of clean room and investigated for damage, prior to discarding the flask.
Impact Assessment: Since the turbidity originated from the same cell line that is producing our desired antibody, the product does not suffer any impact on its quality.  The CL1000 unit could not be used further as a result from the presence of cells in the outer chamber. This is because the cells in the outer chamber will eat up nutrients that the cells in the cell compartment needs. Cells in the outer medium compartment could also potentially obscure operators' ability to discern whether the flask would become infected with bacteria at a later time.  This loss of a CL1000 unit does hamper the overall productivity of the project and could impact the goal of producing enough product for the deadline. Therefore new cells have been thawed and a new CL1000 unit was started to be able to fulfill the production timeline, at an additional cost to Curida Diatec.
Root cause description: Faulty product batch, as this problem has arisen in multiple CL1000 flasks the past few months.

Non Conformance Title: SDS QC analysis performed under wrong conditions
Investigation summary: The deviation originated with a transcription error in the work schedule. CAPA132 is raised to improve this issue.  It was not identified before finalizing technical review which means the error passed four control organs. A trend is observed in non conformity surrounding working according to PS and protocols. Actions will be taken to facilitate the production in working accordingly, and discussions amongst the laboratory team will be organized in the hopes of changing company culture and attitudes surrounding quality. PS is under revisement according to CAPA5.
Impact Assessment: The correct analysis was performed 12.04.2023 (Ref: Attachment 3) and fulfill all requirements of the procedure and the product specification. The deviation has no impact on product quality or timeline.
Root cause description: The deviation originated with a transcription error in the work schedule. Investigation shows that the product specification layout is less that optimal.

Non Conformance Title: Ber-Ep4 for mycoplasma testing, thawed with wrong FBS
Wrong batch used as standard for ELISA
B920 used as isotype control after expiry date
Investigation summary: B920 expiry date was discovered by a newly trained operator. Previous operators had did not know internally produced had expiry date as this is no longer the case. B920 is used as a Isotype control in QC and in-process flow cytometry analysis. Its purpose is to prevent false positive signal from unspecific binding. The expiry date calls into question the stability of the antibody. However, this is challenged by internal stability studies of similar product and B920`s continuous performance.
Impact Assessment: Since not any of the Flow cytometry protocols have established requirements for the isotype control, all technical requirements of previously run in-process or QC analyses are passed. Additionally, the results received from the isotype control is not accounted in the final results. Therefore it can not affect the approval of previous or future products. It is however a useful control for both the preparation of samples and to visualise the background signal. Contrary to the expiry date, the evidence and literature seems to support that the stability and functionality of the antibody is preserved. No impact is identified as a result of this deviation. Use of expired product should be avoided and the product should be discarded as soon as an alternative is in place.
Root cause description: Operator had no knowledge of expiry date on internal products, since this is not practised to date. In the early 2000 all product produced by Diatec monoclonals was marked with an expiry date the same way a chemical is marked, however this practice has changed throughout the years as the requirements for chemicals does not apply to biologics. As this NC occurred so long ago It was difficult to find documentation on when and how this practice was changed. None the less, the labelling of vials from previously produced products has not been changed to reflect current practises, including B920. The root cause is therefore deemed to be the lack of documentation produced with this change. A limited investigation went into the root cause, since the deviation originated so far back in time.

Non Conformance Title: Particles observed inside CELLine 1000 Bioreactors (CL1000) under inoculation
Medium number M230322C-E used twice.
DIA156 above acceptance criteria for CO2
Rift in Harvest tube from AcuSyst Maximizer for 2D2
Impact Other: Loss of less than 100mL harvest when flushing out the system to ensure no bacterial infection. This will be maximum 80 mg lost, and as Maximizer produces 2-300 mg per day this has no impact on delivery.
Investigation summary: Operator did not open clamp after harvest flask exchange. Rift emerged from harvest tubing as the clamp was still closed when the pumps were running creating a buildup of pressure. The tubing was shortened and reattached while samples were sent to Curida Diatec QC services for analysis. Analysis returned negative for microbial infection.
Impact Assessment: No infection has been observed , and no bacterial growth could be identified during analysis of samples taken. The production was never stopped for a long period of time, production time-line is not affected. Only the harvest and F3 pumps were stopped for approximately 10 minutes when shortening harvest tubing, other pumps and parameters remained operational.  Harvest 4 which is used for product batch B8609 was detached before the harvest tube suffered a leak. Harvest 6 used for product batch B8614 was attached after the system had aseptically removed rifted tubing and been flushed with EC medium. As the QC analysis returned negative results, and harvest 5 was discarded after sampling, this non-conformance has no impact on product quality.,
Root cause description: Non-compliance with SOP1064 version 4.0 pt 9.26, operator JSS forgot to open clamp when changing harvest flask before restarting harvest pump.

Non Conformance Title: Low threshold alarm on Freezer DIA363
Investigation summary: Reoccurring alarm for low temp for DIA363 product freezer. Sensor was moved in the freezer but had no effect. Set-point was adjusted, but still had reoccurring alarms so it was adjusted again.
Impact Assessment: This freezer is used as a backup for old product that we probably will not sell. Products stored in this freezer are robust enough to handle small temperature drops. Considering the short period of time below threshold (about 15-20 minutes being up to 2.5° below -33.7℃) it is very unlikely that the product has any conceivable impact from this non-conformity.  The alarm set-point is 15 minutes above or below defined threshold values, of which these alarm only barely exceed. The threshold itself is not placed based on the contents of the freezer and the capability of the product to handle low temperatures, rather it is based on SOP4034 pt 9.3.13 which calculates lower limits on the basis of the thermometer.  SOP4045 regarding freezers, refrigerator and cold room does not specify a lower temperature limit for freezers. SOP4045 version 4.0 pt 9.5.1 places the requirement for freezers to be < -18℃, so this feezer does not deviate in tempeature from what is defined in SOP4045. The upper temperature limit is more critical for frozen items than the lower limit is. The products integrity were visually unimpaired, no bottles were broken nor leaking.
Root cause description: The freezer DIA363 has regular temperature-spike cycles tied to the compressor system which results in the temperature to temporarily reach below -33.7℃.

Non Conformance Title: DIA285 material freezer outside acceptance criteria
Investigation summary: The freezer door was not properly closed and opened again due to frost buildup. Temperature in the freezer rose to about 0 degrees for to consecutive days, before it decrease to normal temperature. For most samples/reagents stored in the freezer it is recommended to avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles. Conclusion is therefore to buy new reagents where possible. Samples that cannot be tested and approved for further use will be discarded.
Impact Assessment: SDS661 and SDS662 were the two first SDS run after the incident, and the results were two invalid runs. The Precision Plus Protein Unstained standard that had been in the freezer during the temperature increasing did only show 9 bands on the gel, and the requirement for a valid run is 10 bands. SDS was run again with a new standard that showed 10 bands. This implies that the refreezing affected the standard so it was discarded. See NC159 for further information. Precision Plus Protein WesternC Standard will also be discarded.    When thawing cells for CL1000 productions, each culture must be sampled for mycoplasma testing at least 7 days after thawing according to SOP1070 version 2.0. There is no requirement for analysing the sample prior to inoculation in CL1000, and testing for mycoplasma is performed only if needed. Current procedure is that the samples are stored in the freezer DIA285 for one year for potential investigation purposes. MycoAlert Kit is intended for detection of viable mycoplasma in cell cultures by measuring activity in certain mycoplasma enzymes, but if the mycoplasma is thawed and refrozen it might lead to reduced viability and death, and this can potentially give false negative results if a test is performed. Samples taken from the ongoing productions will be kept until the productions are ended. All other mycoplasma tests will be discarded, and if a request to perform a mycoplasmatest for these batches are received in the future, information regarding this NC will be given. However it is missing a written procedure for how to store or when to discard the samples. CAPA139 has therefor been initiated to include how to store the samples and a routine to discard mycoplasma samples in SOP1070.     MycoAlert Assay Reagent and Control Set, must not be refrozen according to SOP1070, pt 8.1.3,  these samples will be discarded.     FBS is used to improve the conditions of growth for the cells, so it is not critical to use the thawed and refrozen FBS. Since we can’t guarantee that the quality of the FBS has been unaffected, the cost question regarding further use or disposal includes that using FBS with unknown quality can risk lower yield of total antibody produced. FBS will be discarded. Pen-strep is used to avoid infection in productions and it it important that the product quality is as expected. Pen- strep is not expensive, and since we can't guarantee that the quality is unaffected all Pen-Strep will be discarded.  The standards to the spectrophotometer will most likely not be affected by the thawing and refreezing, and any impact on the sample this will be detected when the sample is run the next time. These samples was tested and found unaffected 28.JUN.2023. The endotoxin samples are already analysed. Samples in the freezer contains the remaining volume of the samples originally taken, so these can be discarded. LAL Reagent Water, not recommended repeated thaw-freeze cycle, product will be discarded.  HRP Color Reagent A has expiry date 29.07.2023, product will be discarded.  Phosphatase substrate tabl. repeated thawing and freezing not recommended. Product will be discarded. Hybridoma Fusion and Cloning Supplement. Product information sheet advise against repeated thawing and freezing due to the stability of the product, product will be discarded.  Incubation samples B7763 +B7824, placed in quarantine until ØR is present.
Root cause description: Root cause is most likely that the door did not close properly due to ice built up in the freezer.

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect registration of serum cat. and lot number in medium register
Investigation summary: Incorrect serum catalogue number and lot number registered for preparation of M230407A, due to operator lack of awareness when using the medium register. The medium register is an excel list, which is easy to copy/paste the information from one column to the next. CAPA88 is ongoing to determine a new, more robust system for medium register, with audit trail.
Impact Assessment: As the correct serum was used, this does not have an impact on product quality or timeline.
Root cause description: Current system to register internal medium has no restrictions on copy/paste of previous information, no audit trail or auto save function, which makes the system prone to errors.

Non Conformance Title: Standard protein ladder had only 9 bands in SDS-PAGE
Investigation summary: SDS661 (Ref: Attachment 1)  and SDS662 (Ref: Attachment 2) was performed unsuccessfully due to non-optimal storage of the precision plus standard documented in NC157. This was corroborated by running two re-runs of the analyses, SDS664 and SDS665, using old and new standard respectively. Old standard was discarded and replaced with a new properly stored aliquot. All failed analyses were repeated successfully.
Impact Assessment: Neither product quality nor production timeline is affected by this deviation since all analyses were repeated successfully prior to batch release, and the compromised standard aliquot is discarded.
Root cause description: The protein standard used for SDS page was stored under undesirable conditions and was most likely compromised by degradation (Ref: NC157)

Non Conformance Title: Discrepancy in the cell stock status
Investigation summary: Curida Diatec was notified of a discrepancy within our cell stock register concerning clones 5G8-G2, Cl280 and Cl356. The annual cellstock report provided to Mercodia in 2022 was cross referenced with physical vial count. Some of the discrepancy could be explained by projects from 2022 to 2023, but some still remained. Historical investigation into the three clones 5G8-G2, Cl280 and Cl356 was also performed. This provided some clarity into the discrepancies. Total annual vials was trended from 2018 to 2023 to look for large errors, but none could be identified. The root cause investigation definite, however two potential causes was identified. First, a register prone to human error. Secondly, our transition to online sharepoint server (Ref: CMID17).
Impact Assessment: The investigation of this non-conformance was unable to determine the scope or impact of this deviation, considering data integrity. Although some corrections have been made, some discrepancies persist and cannot be explained. The noncoformance does not impact product quality, however, it impacts the quality of the provided service. What is more, that can be the potential impact on business reputation caused by potential undercharging or overcharging customers for cell bank service. Therefore, CAPA169 is raised to check all positions within our nitrogen storage and update the cell line register within the next 3 months. The CAPA170 is raised to improve our current register by replacing manual writing with lists for critical datapoints such as clone name, customer, position, and type.    It is, however, important to emphasize that the impact of this deviation concerns data integrity and traceability exclusively. The vials are stored in safe and suitable conditions, there is no impact on the cells stored in cell banks.
Root cause description: The root cause of this non conformance could not be identified definitely. However, it was narrowed down to the two most plausible explanations. First, the server transition documented in CMID17. Second, weaknesses in the register itself. This is supported by observations in the investigation characterizing the kind of errors that had occurred.

Non Conformance Title: FPLC instrument stopped during run
Impact Other: Extra human resource, time and reagents/buffers were needed to complete the run.
Investigation summary: Synchronisation/communication error between the software and the instrument. The problem is a known problem which is rare but unavoidable. Service has been performed on the instrument regularly every year. This error is neither detectable under service nor can be completely fixed. But spare pumps are stored in the lab in case it requires emergency changing of pumps. This will be performed by trained personnel/supplier.
Impact Assessment: The samples loaded onto the column during run FPLC3001 were re-washed during FPLC3001eluation" run. Since the process is an affinity chromatography, the binding buffer (pH 8.6) will only wash off the impurities/unbound molecules from the column but not the bound antibodies. With the buffer that is used in the process, the affinity between the antibodies and the column matrix is removed by lower pH like elution buffer (pH 3.5) or regeneration buffer (pH 2.0). None of these lower pH buffer were loaded onto the column durng the run "FPLC3001". This can be confirmed by the final results as the elution peak can be observed in the graph from run "FPLC3001eluation" only (see attachment: FPLC3001).  Therefore this non conformance does not have an impact on product quality."
Root cause description: The instrument is regularly serviced every year by approved supplier and has not had any detectable problem under service. The problem is known which occurs rarely but is not avoidable, even with service.  The new run went smoothly after the computer and FPLC instrument restart. So, It is difficult to pin-point the cause of the non-conformance.  Thus, it can be inferred that this non-conformance was a glitch in the system or technical error which is very rare and difficult to find the root cause at this point.

Non Conformance Title: Cleanroom qualification gap
Investigation summary: We inspected all documents regarding cleanroom qualification and found a gap in 2020, 2022 and 2023.
Impact Assessment: There is no impact on the production or produced product quality, as all open processes are performed in the (grade A) LAF bench. The LAF bench is serviced annually to ensure it works as it should. Cleanrooms are cleaned frequently according to SOP1072, and operators follow the class C gowning regime according to SOP1095. Cleanrooms are monitored weekly. The environment monitoring data were inspected for the last two years. There were no increased number of observed in cases of infection between 2020 and 2023 (NCID 71, 95, NCR 2009, 2031, 2039).
Root cause description: No system for cleanroom qualification to notify those responsible for this task. If such system was in place, even newly hired employees would be alerted. A contributing cause is the Insufficient training of the quality/QA manager and production team leader that lead to a gap in 2021, 2022 and 2023.

Non Conformance Title: Medium M230526A missing from internal register
Investigation summary: Unregistered medium M230526A was used on four occasions during the production 2023-20/21. Operator responsible for preparing this medium gave a statement and described her routine for medium preparation and use in production, description is according to procedure. Batch documentation was investigated for anomalies in cell count, viability and antibody concentration, no anomalies was found. A review of our medium register +/- 1 month from 25.05.2023 was performed to identify which medium specifications was in use.  Only two very different kinds of medium, RPMI and ADCF, was used within this timeframe. it was concluded that the correct medium was used but was not registered. Root cause was investigated and determined to be Equipment due to a recent change in the Ipad within our clean room suite. It was also identified a trend in recurrence of errors within our internal medium registry which would support our system for recording internal lots might be a contributing factor.
Impact Assessment: No impact on product quality nor timeline as correct medium was used. As medium was not registered/saved in internal register, traceability of materials (lot.no of medium and serum) used for this specific medium flask is not maintained.
Root cause description: The most plausible root cause for this deviation is deemed to be equipment, and system is identified as a contributing factor.

Non Conformance Title: Harvest medium in ASM went empty during production
Impact Other: New production batches had to be started in both CL1000 and ASM to achieve the delivery goal, requiring a lot of manual labor and other resources.
Investigation summary: The incidence occurred due to miscommunication between the operators. Though there was a discussion on what should be performed, it was not defined who would perform it as both were responsible for the AcuSyst according to the weekly plan. Both of the operators made their own assumptions so that neither the information was conveyed to the team-leader nor was the task performed. On the other side, the cells were deprived of harvest medium for almost 2-3 hours.
Impact Assessment: Due to the incident, the EC chamber was filled with cells on the day the incidence was discovered. Then the empty harvest flask was replaced. HP was set to 200 ml/hr for almost 2 minutes and F3 at 200 ml/hr for 5 minutes. Then both F3 and HP was set to 0 ml/hr during the weekend. The pumps were set to 7 ml/hr on 03.07.2023. After the incident, the in-process parameters were regularly followed from 03.07.2023 to 06.07.2023. The parameters were quite stable until on 06.07.2023 when the daily production, measured by ELISA, dropped to 82 mg/mL from 346 mg/mL the day before. Even though the other parameters were still within the normal range, the steep drop in daily antibody production led to the decision for batch termination. Due to this, the production batch was terminated on 07.07.2023 and the harvest collected between the period of 29.06.2023 and 07.07.2023 was kept in Quarantine". The incident can have impacted the product. The cells are present on the extracapillary space (ECS) of the bioreactor which is provided with harvest medium (cell culture medium with 10% FBS). Through the capillaries intracapillary space (ICS), medium with 0.5% FBS flows throughout the system. The medium in the ICS provides optimal cell culture microenvironment by maintaining pH and small molecules nutrient like glucose. Harvest medium provides cell culture medium with supplements like serum to the cell culture. Product is also present in the ECS which is harvested by harvest pump (HP). F3 and HP are set to same speed to replenish the volume of medium containing cells and product with fresh medium. The osmotic difference between ICS and ECS helps to remove unwanted cell culture's byproduct. Due to empty harvest flask, air was sucked into the bioreactor instead of harvest medium to the extracapillary space of bioreactor, disturbing the balance in osmotic pressure. This reduces the removal of cellular byproducts as well as the cells were sucked in to the EC chamber. The cells were deprived of medium for 2-3 hours and the nutrient starvation could result to metabolical stress, DNA damage, and activaction of several molecules for adaptation and survival pathways.  The actual impact on production from this specific cell line is not studied, however, this cellular microenvironment is not optimal for cells and antibody production, in general. Thus, the quality of the product produced during this period can not be relied upon and harvest collected in this period is to be discarded."
Root cause description: Miscommunication between the operators as both operators were equally responsible according to the weekly plan. Task was not conveyed to the production management.

Non Conformance Title: Loss of product due to clogging of filter during filtration of B8614 2D2 final product
B8614 2D2 pH measurement for final product failed
Investigation summary: pH of final product was below criteria stated in the PS. The measurments were taken 3 weeks after product was made, as the operator performing the task did not realise it during vialling of the product, as the information was not in a logical place for final product. Previous discussions with the production team has identified that it can be challenging to find correct information between SOP and PS. This is addressed in CAPA73. most likely reason for lower than expected pH is connected to the stability of the product which is known to be short, and temperature pH was measured at.
Impact Assessment: The variation in pH of the final (storage buffer) could impact the following parameters of the antibodies: solubility, stability and potency. All those aspects were evaluated during quality control testing of the final products. If the solubility of the antibodies was affected, this would be observed as an increase in final product turbidity and antibodies precipitation. The visual control of the final product was performed on 16.JUN.2023 and the operator confirmed and documented that the product is “clear and colourless”. Therefore, there was no impact on product solubility. Another parameter which could be impacted is product stability, as antibodies could aggregate. The SDS-Page analysis was performed to prove product purity. If the product would aggregate, the additional band or smears would be visible on the gel. As only one band was detected on the gel, it can be confirmed that the product did not aggregate or degrade. Product potency was also not impacted, as an identification test. This test uses ELISA plate coated with antibodies specific to the final product antibodies. This test also met its specification criteria, therefore there was no impact on product potency.    It can be concluded that minor variations in the pH of the storage buffer did not have an impact on product quality.     It should be highlighted as well that pH of the final product is measured only for the record, and it is not the final release test (according to the signed product specification).
Root cause description: The pH should be measured when the final vialing is performed. The operator who performed the activities was interviewed and confirmed that she forgot to perform this measurement. This mistake was due to the fact that the requirement to take a pH was not included in the worksheet or SOP but in the product specification (PS). The vailing is performed according to internal procedure, however sometimes additional tests or measurements are required by the customer for a specific project, and then this information is included in the PS. As this is not standard, the operator forgot to check PS for additional instructions. Because the system is currently based on PS and SOP, its prone to error.    CAPA73 was initiated to update Vialing worksheet with additional checkpoints, to ensure that all requirements from PS are followed.  This CAPA is already under execution and the updated worksheet is pending technical verification.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected infection during cell expansion of 2D2
Impact Other: Discarded newly thawed vial from master cell bank 3 (MCB3) for THAW825 as precaution of cross contamination.
Investigation summary: Infection must have originated during cell expansion 07.07.2023 beginning in a pooled cell flask from 03.07.2023 and split to the five flasks identified as infected.  Operator had worked with a large number of flasks in LAF bench which increases risk of contamination.  A small risk of contamination will always be present as long as humans are involved in the process.
Impact Assessment: The cells were not in production phase so no product batch is affected by this non-conformance.  The timeline for backup production was pushed by two weeks, being inoculated in bioreactors week 30 instead of the planned week 28.
Root cause description: The most likely root cause can be considered that operator had to handle large number of flasks at the same time as it was needed for the new 2D2 production. Operator JSS was also performing practical demonstration training for operator HPJ which could increase the chance of contamination. This combination of flasks and explanations for training may have lead to a reduced aseptic technique during the expansion of some flasks, which potentially resulted in the contamination discovered. While operator JSS has done plenty of work with cell cultures, this was the first time he operated with such a large number of flasks. When discussing it with the operator, he admits that he is aware of the principles of aseptic work which rely on few items in the LAF at a time, and that he should have split the operation into several segments of flask expansions to maintain optimal aseptic technique.  Cell culture is a very sensitive procedure and infection can occur from a very slight compromise of aseptic procedure which is very difficult to completely eliminate. Infection is rare in our production as operators go through aseptic training and cleanroom gowning validations as well as work in LAFs with open processes, however, there is always a slight risk of infection during cell handling.

Non Conformance Title: Error in non-conformance noticed after batch approval and release
Impact Other: This error was detected after release of B8614. It is related to documentation and has no impact on product quality or production. However, some extra resources were used to investigate the issue.
Investigation summary: Operator mistakenly documented harvests from a maxmizer run differently in a non conformance and in batch documentation, resulting in a discarded harvest" to be included in a final batch. This was due to the extrordinary circumstances surrounding the inciden described in NC155 and the fact that operator was not familiar with such immediate actions."
Impact Assessment: Harvest 6 (referred in batch documentation as harvest 5) used for product batch B8614 was attached after the system had aseptically removed rifted tubing and been flushed with EC medium. As the QC analysis returned negative results, and harvest 5 (the non numbered harvesting as an immediate action) was discarded after sampling, this non-conformance has no impact on product quality.,
Root cause description: human error due to no previous experience with such errors on a miaximiser and the need to act immediatly to secure the production.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong eluation pH used for purification
Investigation summary: pH below product specification has most likely been used for many years as it is needed for the product (2.86/2.9 vs 3.0). This change is not documented in our quality system or documentation of process performed. Instructions on troubleshooting are needed in SOP
Impact Assessment: The difference in pH is not considered substantial, therefore, it has no impact on final product quality. Product quality is not impacted as product batches B8619, B8643 and B8665 met its quality criteria as defined in the product specification. 100% purity was determined by SDS-PAGE with no degradation and aggregation detected. ELISA technique confirmed identity against anti-human IgE (OD > 0.1 more than negative). The products B8619 and B8665 were confirmed to have low endotoxin levels and concentration at 1.0 mg/mL as defined in the product specification.
Root cause description: Previous practice of optimizing procedures without anchoring it in the quality system is considered to be the main root cause, with contributing factors of SOPs for FPLC are not clear on how to handle trouble with elution and who should be notified, and finally possible contributing identified as the lack of understanding of basic mathematical principles (rounding of numbers). Root Cause Category:     Process - of anchoring in the QMS system,   Documentation - on troubleshooting   Lack of training - in basic mathematical principles

Non Conformance Title: ELISA Washer Malfunctioning. In-process analysis run before change was initiated for manual washing step
Classification justification: Washing manually is an approved method for use in QC analysis SOP1097 and SOP1039 which work on the same analytical principle as SOP1025. Change Action ID184 was initiated to train and instruct personnel in manual wash of ELISA plates for procedures that did not have this included. No further ELISA analysis were performed between the registration of Change Action ID184 and its completion. Using manual wash has no impact on production nor production timeline. product Specification and agreements are not affected.

Non Conformance Title: B8640 out of specification for monocytes during QC flow
Investigation summary: B8640 did not adhere to specification outlined in PS 2023-22. This is concluded to be due to sample integrity was not maintained during sample preparations due to inexperience of operators.
Impact Assessment: As the investigation has lead to the conclusion that the out of specification results were due to technical issues, this has no impact on the final product or its quality. Results obtained from the test run on 23.08.23 indicate that the product is within specification and of good quality. A new QC testing can be run on B8640 and pending on QC results, the product can be released.    This non-conformance has resulted in 4 weeks delay of product delivery to customer and demanded additional resources, both in workforce, equipment and material.
Root cause description: Root cause is determined to be that sample integrity was not maintained during sample preparations due to inexperience of operators. Factors for suboptimal separation the cell population were identified where the main contributing factors are: -       Storage temperature -        Staining procedure and the amount of time cells are without supernatant.  -        Time running samples
Classification justification: Non compliance to product specification agreed with customer

Non Conformance Title: Sirius trend review not performed within deadline
Investigation summary: Operators EVE (Admin) and SBA (Super-users) are responsible for for all Sirius tasks, including trend review. The missing trend reviews was according to the immediate action performed 15/16.08.2023 without any deviation related to the data trended. The root cause investigation considered documentation, equipment, material, training, people and system. Only system could reasonably be considered as a cause of this investigation. However, both the excel list used as a scheduling tool and the delegation of tasks within the team appears to have been contributing factors..
Impact Assessment: Sirius system are checked by operators daily for alarms or malfunctions.  The Sirius logged data were reviewed on 15.08.2023 and 16.08.2023 and no deviation related to the data trend was observed. The non-conformance did not have any impact on production timeline or product quality.
Root cause description: Investigation identified two potential root causes of this non-conformance, both can be categorized as System/Process. First, the excel spread-sheet Oversikt vasking og utstyr - Fornebu 2nd floor"  is concluded to have errors  and weaknesses in the lack of alert system, and the number of people that are responsible for following it up. Secondly, Sirius' responsibility is divided among operators as Admin and Superuser, which is not common as for other instruments used in the lab. So, there's a risk in presuming that others will handle it. Excel sheet is currently undergoing organizing and improving the excel list including defining responsibility for the follow up of the entire list. This is anchored in CAPA224. CAPA220 raised to more clearly define the responsibilities of the operators in charge of the Sirius system."
Classification justification: As trend review was performed and approved in the immediate action, this deviation has no impact on validated state of equipment.

Non Conformance Title: Ultra low temperature freezer broke down DIA261
Investigation summary: DIA261 ultrafreezer broke down on Fiday 04.AUG.2023. There was no backup freezer up and running on site but DIA409 and GMP freezer were set up and started Monday after incident. Products transferred to GMP freezer with continuous logging and alarm function connected to production managers mobile phone. GMP freezer malfunctioned on 30.aug.2023, product moved to new DIA409, raw material to DIA066. Both DIA216 and GMP freezers are old -70 freezers that most likely were due for malfunction. CAPA158 is initiated to ensure proper implementation and qualification of new freezer DIA409.
Impact Assessment: Impact on Material within the freezer at the time of non conformane: Raw material stored in the freezer does not have a requirement to be stored at -70, but should be frozen at over -20. For practical reasons FBS and pen strep have been stored at -70, as there is space for storing it there. As the material was kept at -34 at the warmest, they did not thaw and this non-conformance is not considered to have an impact on the material. Material can be used for future work. Table 1: overview of products stored in DIA261 at the time of malfunction. Total financial loss is indicated in the yellow column to the right. The last 6 products in table 1 were not registered in ferdigvarelager 2023" and therefore are not considered to be a part of the financial loss as this product would not have been sold. As the final products were kept at -34 degrees for almost a week when its specification is stated as -70/-80, its quality can not be confirmed to be intact and all final product will be removed from stock (CAPA157 and CAPA159) Other samples stored in DIA261 impacted by this non-conformance: -NGAL antigen for Western QC of NGAL antibody products:  Ngal #9278 (cat),    according to CoA material should not be stored at higher temperature than -65. Vials are to be discarded and new vial will be secured prior to next Ngal production. -Samples lacking info about contents: M34350017, 16vials, and 14-HB-D-002, 1vial. MIB, VFA, ØHR, JFL and AHU were contacted regarding what these vials are, without any better understanding of what they are. These vials are to be placed together in a bag, clearly labelled with quarantine, date and reference to NC.  Impact on future products while freezer is under implementation/qualification: As DIA409 freezer is continuously monitored to be within the threshold by a sirius logger calibrated to this temperature, it does not have an impact on any future material and products stored in this freezer. This includes B8642 Sirolimus project 2023-24 Sirolimus and freezing of cellbanks for projects 2023-33 and 2023-34, products 6450 280 and 6460 356. As long as the freezer is not qualified, any product should be stored as close to the sirius sensor as possible for most accurate reading. If another issue occurs with temperature of -70 below threshold, a new NC needs to be opened and the impact of all product located in the freezer at that time will be assessed there."
Root cause description: Equipment malfunction, DIA261. When incident was discovered no backup ultrafreezer was available. Operators did not inform Management, misunderstood the time aspect (waited until management was on site).
Classification justification: Risk to product quality as stability of product has not been established at a temperature under -70.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA analysis failed due to not comparable duplicates after manual wash steps performed.
Classification justification: In process analysis were repeated and approved. No impact on process or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA analysis failed due to not comparable duplicates
Investigation summary: It is a lot of steps in the procedure where the accuracy is very important. Even if the operators are well trained in the process, there are many steps where a small inaccuracy can lead to errors. The process is very manual and not very robust.
Impact Assessment: This NC has no impact on the product quality as the samples was analysed only for information and the information was received when the samples were re-analysed.
Root cause description: The process is very manually performed with many steps that require high accuracy. Root cause is considered to be the fact that the process itself is not very robust leading to accuracy of the operator has not been good enough.
Classification justification: In process analysis does not impact quality of the product, employee safety or customer agreements. Results are only for information regarding amount produced, and analysis was repeated successfully.

Non Conformance Title: Filling volume of bulk vial HE1/4b/3 B8665 product not according to PS
Investigation summary: Product Specification information was not followed during vialling of product batch B8665 due to a wrong understanding of specification.
Impact Assessment: Product was revialled prior to batch approval, where one of the extra 1 ml vial was included in the bulk vial, making the total bulk at  21 mL. As there was an excess of 1,0 mL vials (16x 1,0 mL vials more) this had no impact. There is no impact identified on product quality or production timeline.
Root cause description: misunderstanding of specification in PS for product 7050. Vialling of bulk has been performed according to vialling SOP since before this requirement was included in PS.
Classification justification: As this NC is in non-compliance with customers product specification, this is classified as a high risk NC according to SOP5034 v3.0

Non Conformance Title: High threshold alarm for CO2 in incubator DIA025
Classification justification: The incident occured for relatively short period of time and the %CO2 was marginally higher than alarm limits. As the buffer system in the cell culture medium manages to stabilize the minor fluctuation in the pH, this non-conformance is not considered to have an impact on production or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Production in ASM without implementation of the instrument
Investigation summary: The ASMs were serviced and calibrated by qualified supplier and were ready for use in production. instrument binders were started and equipment already had a equipment number. However, instruments were not properly implemented in the QMS system.
