Employees responsible for equipment implementation and planning, had left the company and it was not clear to other employees that the instruments were not ready for production.
Impact Assessment: This has no impact to the product quality or production timelines. The instrument was calibrated and serviced by the service personnel and declared fit-for use". As the production facility is already using ASMs for production, this instrument was used to replace a similar instrument. All the basic requirements, procedures were already in-use and the operators are trained to use the instruments.  Instruments were not properly implemented and were not included in instrument list, and maintenance list but as this was discovered before maintenance was due it had no impact to the instruments."
Root cause description: Procedure for labelling of equipment not ready for use was not followed and proper implementation of the equipment was not performed. it is not possible to determine the true root cause as employees responsible at that time have left the organisation.
Classification justification: The instruments should be properly implemented before use to ensure control, maintenance schedule and that it is fit-for-purpose . As this is a like-for-like change and instrument was serviced prior to use, it has no effect on qualification status, product quality, HSE or customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: CO2 supply control not performed week 27, weekly check of CO2 cylinders in outside shedule
Classification justification: CO2 levels were recently filled, and as this non-conformance did not result in any issues with CO2 supply it has no impact.

Non Conformance Title: Location in Nitrogen tank deleted in cellstock
Investigation summary: The location in cellstock was most likely deleted by mistake by operator GS when the information regarding the vial should have been removed.
Impact Assessment: This NC does not impact the quality of any product or customer agreement. This is only an isolated incident, and all other locations containing cell vials were still present in cell stock.
Root cause description: The root cause is identified to be gaps in restricted control of the database at the time of the incident. As the investigation also shows there are no other similar recurrences of similar instances, it is likely this change in restrictions was introduced during the move of the F disk to its current sharepoint location, rather than a long standing problem (previously the database was one set up on Microsoft Access). During the investigation of this NC the gap related to insufficient control of the  access restrictions was identified and corrected as an immediate action. Although the NC has been classified as high impact as this issue had the potential for significant quality issues with control of the cell bank, the investigation shows the extend of impact is low as it has been identified early. No further corrective actions are therefore considered necessary to prevent recurrence.
Classification justification: This NC does not impact product quality, employee safety or customers agreements. However it is classified as high risk due the potential impact it could have for the customer that store their cellbanks at CD if this incident and potential similar incidents had not been detected, as this is our only database for LN2 storage. Back up of cell stock register is performed and stored by IT supplier Braathe therefore information regarding deleted location can be recovered.

Non Conformance Title: UV-VIS standard solution not ordered in time for quarterly check of Spectrophotometer and Elisa reader
Investigation summary: Standard solution products were not ordered when stock was empty as it is dependent on operators memory to inform logisitics. By having an automated notification system within the ERP system this will be handled, see CAPA164
Impact Assessment: As products may not be released without performed and approved QC tests, which in turn can not be performed until after quarterly check of the instrument. Therefore this non-conformance had the potential to cause a delay in timeline for multiple projects, B8667 and B8668 were pending due to QC elisa (Specificity control). B8677 and B8678 could not be vialled to final product as concentration is measured by the spectrophotometer (QC OD). There is no direct impact on quality as despite the delayed quarterly check of equipment, on 18.SEP.2023 it was confirmed that equipment is within specification.  As all pending tasks were completed on 19.SEP.2023, this non-conformance had little effect on timelines, and all products should be able to be released according to schedule.
Root cause description: No system in place for automatic reminder at stock status zero, and ordering more is dependent on operators memory
Classification justification: As all ELISA QC analysis and product vialling was postponed until UV-vis was available again, this has no impact to product quality. No impact to validated state of equipment or compliance to customer agreements. No risk to employee safety. However, as it has a potential serious impact on timelines for multiple projects, this NC will be investigated further.

Non Conformance Title: Insufficient date marking on label for 1A12/1/4 PCB7 not documented i DOC 0001_0003.1 for thaw 819.
Classification justification: This non conformance has no impact on process or product. The insufficient date on the label was already discovered and the correct date for freezing was added to the locations for all remaining vials in cell stock.

Non Conformance Title: Pipette DIA118 and DIA307 failed As found""
Investigation summary: Pipettes have been discarded after two failed as found events. The criteria for discarding a pipette is too strict for the accuracy needed at Curida Diatec. All pipettes pass after service and maintenance so far. To reduce resources used on non-critical incidents, and avoid the frequent need to buy new pipettes, requirements for acceptance need to be re-defined.
Impact Assessment: Some minor economic impact as pipettes have been discarded.  No impact on product as Curida Diatec testing does not require such accuracy in their pipettes.
Root cause description: The criteria for calibration failing and subsequent discarding of pipettes is too strict.
Classification justification: No impact on product or analysis as the analysis at Curida Diatec using pipettes do not require so high accuracy. The pipette DIA307 passed after service and maintenance and the pipette DIA118 will be discarded according to SOP4014. DIA118 is used for dilution steps for processes such as ELISA and SDS, but was one of four pipettes of the size 20-200µL. The loss of one is not considered to have an impact to production.  As this is a recurrent NC it will be classified as High risk.

Non Conformance Title: Missing temperature and CO2 sirius data from incubators DIA156 and DIA169
Investigation summary: The sirius logger alerted for low battery and the alert went unseen for the operators within several other system alarms due to lack of procedure to handle technical alarms. Thus, the logger went out of battery and data on temperature and CO2 was lost.
Impact Assessment: Incubators have built-in temperature/CO2 sensors that allows them to keep the parameters within the set limits. However, these data are only accessible through the display in CO2. To make these data accessible online, additional Sirius logging system is also connected to the incubators. The measurement from incubator and Sirius is independent of each other. This incident doesn't impact the content within the incubator because this is an extra cloud-based monitoring system of the parameters in the incubator. Any deviations out of the specified threshold in the incubator triggers a sound alarm in the incubator itself, easily noticable. Sirius system makes it easier to detect and record data when outside of the cleanroom compared to that of the incubator itself and lack of data from sirius could result in a problem with detection of critical parameters in time. As the incubator gives an alarm independence from the sirius system, and no issues were reported in this time by the operators, temperature and CO2 appear to have been within their thresholds and therefore this non conformance does not appear to have had an impact on production or product quality.    This is further supported by the in-process quality control of production by ELISA. The result is given in the table below:           Production Batch            Clone            Harvest no.             Concentration (mg/mL)            Sampling                B8599-8601            HE1/4b/3            H10            0,4            Before NC                B8599-8601            HE1/4b/3            H15            0,4            After NC                B8575-77            1A12/1/4            H10            1,33            Before NC                B8575-77            1A12/1/4            H15            1,45            During NC                B8575-77            1A12/1/4            H20            1,62            After NC                B8612            UCHT-1            H5            0,53            Before NC                B8612            UCHT-1            H10            0,8            During NC                B8612            UCHT-1            H15            0,78            After NC           Any impact on cells from temperature (either high or low) would most likely have resulted in a  drop in concentration (mg/mL) as the period would have stressed the cells. The result shows no impact during the period of NC and all concentrations are similar to previous results from the clones.
Root cause description: Battery was not replaced when it was alerted by the system.  The alert from the system went unnoticed since the system has a lot of system alarm and it is not specified on the SOP to handle these type of alarms. (Ref. SOP4032 version 3.0, pt. 6.4)
Classification justification: The temperature and CO2 in the incubator are regulated based on the reading by incubator's in-built sensors. Sirius facilitates easier monitoring and recording of parameters but has no impact/influence on the parameter in incubator itself. However, in case of malfunctioning of incubator, the chance of detecting such error is lower without Sirius logger.  Since the incident impact recording of the parameters but not the parameter itself, the non-conformance is classified as low risk.

Non Conformance Title: pH standard reference buffer not received in time
Classification justification: Standard is used for In process monitoring of AcuSyst production, which is in its monitoring phase. This is a relatively stable period where the pH measured varies little day by day. It is considered low risk of using expired standard, if it is needed, as it is newly expired and will not affect the reliability of pH measurements. No final buffers will be made before receiving the new lot of reference. therefore No impact on product quality and low impact on process/equipment.

Non Conformance Title: EC error rate exceeded for ASM production B8622
Investigation summary: Alarm for exceeded EC volume triggered before working hours Monday 11.09.2023 due to buildup of cells. Cell removal procedure done to reduce buildup.
Impact Assessment: The product quality of harvest 6 and onward does not suffer any impact due to this NC as can be seen in in-process parameters being within expected values.  If the EC volume had reached the filter and entered the instrument, it would have severe consequences for the machinery. This could not have happened as the filter was confirmed untouched at visual inspection, the medium has a distinct pink colour and would be easily visible.
Root cause description: Cell debris buildup in EC chamber approaching critical levels. The cell debris buildup in EC chamber is unavoidable, as cells grow exponentially. Operators have seen the EC chamber containing a substantial amount of cells, but cell removal was put off as the procedure often comes with a significant reduction in cells and antibody production.
Classification justification: The triggered alarm indicated that the cycling process of the bioreactor is no longer optimal and it struggles to empty the EC chamber. Lack of efficient cycling can negatively impact cells over time and therefore delivery plan. Cell removal is a risky procedure which removes a lot of living cells in the process of removing cell debris. The production will suffer a reduction in antibody output until the cells manage to recover.

Non Conformance Title: Loss of data from DIA066 and DIA245
Investigation summary: According to correspondance with JRI technical support, the 1 minute recording frequency settings limited the data storage capacity of the loggers DIA343 and DIA357. Therefore all data was not recovered. Internal procedures does not inform of this correlation between recording frequency and memory.
Impact Assessment: Two loggers could not recover data after internet malfunction. During this period no internal alarms on the equipment was triggered. The loggers was also checked manually within the time frame and both DIA066 and DIA245 was within acceptance criteria. Therefore no impact could be identified from this deviation.
Root cause description: Data was lost due to information regarding logging frequency and data recovery was lacking in process description, as this was not previously known.
Classification justification: As all Sirius loggers have capacity for storing data, almost all data was recovered with re-connection of the internet. The loggers that lost data was checked manually within the time frame of the lost data. No deviation from acceptance criteria was noted. Hence this non-conformance can be categorized as low risk.

Non Conformance Title: Cell removal initiated from BRX instead of EC
Classification justification: IPC measurments after cell removal indicate no difference from standard cell removal procedure. No cells were removed from the BRX.  This NC has no known or potential quality/safety effect. Product from harvest 6 and on going are unimpaired.

Non Conformance Title: Number of CFU on settle plate used during D validation exceeds limit.
Classification justification: This non conformance has no impact on customer agreements, employee safety or the validated state of the equipment. It has been three validations in DIA223 after the NGR`s validation, all with 0 CFU for LAF settle plate, so there is no reason to suspect something is wrong with the bench. No product was made during this validation, so this non conformance has no impact on product quality either.

Non Conformance Title: Contamination discovered in CL1000  flasks for 2D2
Investigation summary: Suspected infection in CL1000 unit B8626 and B8629 lead to them being discarded. QC analysis found the samples to be positive for bacteria or fungi.B8626 had infected cells in both inner and outer compartment, which also indicates a leakage has taken place between the two compartments.B8629 only had infected cells in the inner cell chamber, which lowered outer media pH and turned it yellow.A third infected unit was disovered the next working day where CL1000 units stemming from DIA156 were inspected.
Impact Assessment: It was confirmed to be bacteria or fungal contamination but both flasks was removed and never opened inside the cleanroom. The termination of the CL1000 flasks constitutes a loss of crude harvest volume up to 420mL, but since 2D2 was produced both in CL1000 and Maximiser, the discarding of the flasks had no impact on the production timeline.Impact assessment for B8650 is performed in NC205. The flasks were not harvested at the day of discovery. B8626 and B8629 harvests 1-14 have no impact as they tested negative for infection. Infection in CL1000 using RPMI media is quickly detected as it changes the colour from red to yellow. It is not likely that this infection started at a much earlier time point. Furthermore, even if there was a beginning of infection the elution at a low pH would destroy any growth in the product. Finally, the 0,2 µm filtration would eliminate any possibility of microorganisms growing in the final product. Therefore, infection of the final product is considered very unlikely, therefore product quality is unaffected.
Root cause description: It is not possible to identify a definite root cause. Contamination from operator to and around the harvest port using unsterile materials and/or equipment is likely the root cause. Operators are operating as in class C room in an unclassified room. Although operator are taking great precautions, there is always a risk for contamination. The medium was of a turbid, yellowish hue typically resulting from Staphylococcus or Streptococcus contaminations, strains typically found on human skin.
Classification justification: The flasks were removed at harvest 15 and the production was intended to be ended at harvest 25. The amount of crude lost was about ~400 mL total.  The flasks were not harvested at the day of discovery. B8626 and B8629 harvests 1-14 have no impact as they tested negative for infection.  The ongoing 2D2 production was big enough so that the loss in about 400 ml of expected crude did not make an impact on amount product needed or delivery timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage within CL1000 unit cell chamber
Investigation summary: A total of 12 flasks were discovered to either have or develop leakage. 5 of these flasks were discovered before harvest 10 which is within the time-frame (30 days) ensured safe by the supplier.
Impact Assessment: MY11 lost 4 of 8 production units, causing a delay in production and an extended period of harvesting in order to reach necessary quantity of antibodies.  1A12/1/4 were produced on forecast with no exact date of delivery. The loss of production units for project 2022-25 does not impact production schedule.  581 lost 1 of 3 production units, however the two remaining units produced significant amount of antibodies leading to no change in production timeline.  2D2 were produced in CL1000 as a backup production working parallel with the AcuSyst Maximizer production unit batch B8622. B8622 produced very well and the loss of one CL1000 at a very late stage of production (leak discovered at H24, production was intended to end at H25) had no impact on production timeline As all CL1000 were confirmed not to have an infection and were terminated upon discovery, Only H11 for B8647-49 clone 1A12, and H2 for B8683, clone 581 were discarded as a consequence of this deviation as they were considered a risk for bacterial contamination. All other harvests from the impacted batches were evaluated to be acceptable and free from infection, and could be used for further processing towards final product. This non-conformance has no impact on product quality.
Root cause description: CL1000 flasks from batch 0000043464 are defect. Supplier complaint has been filed.
Classification justification: Some of the leaking production units were discovered very early in production, this could impact production timeline. More than 3 previous events have been recorded the last year.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA analysis failed due to not comparable duplicates
Investigation summary: It is a lot of steps in the procedure where the accuracy is very important. Even if the operators are well trained in the process, there are many steps where a small inaccuracy can lead to errors. The process is very manual and not very robust.
Impact Assessment: This NC has no impact on the product quality as the samples was analysed only for information and the information was received when relevant samples were re-analysed.
Root cause description: The process is very manually performed with many steps that require high accuracy. Root cause is considered to be the fact that the process itself is not very robust leading to accuracy of the operator has not been good enough.
Classification justification: In process analysis does not impact quality of the product. Results are only for information regarding amount produced, This non conformance has no impact on customer agreements, employee safety or the validated state of the equipment

Non Conformance Title: Extra band in control sample during SDS-PAGE
Investigation summary: B6777 was produced in 2018 and has degraded since. Based on the SOP, even though the control and test sample are not comparable, the sample fulfills the technical requirements of SOP1066 and product specification. The SOP fails to explain the role of control sample in SDS-PAGE run. therefore use of old batches as reference batches should be avoided and the role of control sample for approval should be defined.
Impact Assessment: Based on the current version of SOP1066 and the criteria in PS, the non-conformance has no impact on the product quality based on the following consideration:  a. SDS677 meets the technical requirement for the approval of gel run (Ref. SOP1066 version 3.0, Pt. 10.1) and is considered a valid SDS-PAGE run. b. According to product specification (PS), two bands (one weak band additional to the main band) is an acceptable criteria for the test sample, which the test sample B8678 fulfills.   c. Comparison of migration pattern of B6777 in SDS420 and SDS677 shows that the control sample is degraded over time, which can be expected during storage. Nevertheless, test sample (B8678) in SDS677 has similar migration pattern as the control sample has when first approved (B6777) in SDS420.  QC SDS-PAGE was re-run and confirms that product is of sufficient quality as according to specification.
Root cause description: The control sample, B6777, was produced in 2018 and has since then degraded due to age of product with age. A control sample must be run parallel with the test sample, and should be a previously approved batch if possible or at least a batch of the same isotype. As some products are produced with a few years between them, it increases the likelihood of degraded control sample. To mitigate this, control samples should not be more than 12 months old.
Classification justification: Since the test sample (B8678) is according to specification, this does not impact product quality. Method is not validated and product is produced in compliance with customer agreement this is considered a low risk NC.

Non Conformance Title: Possible infection discovered in CL1000 flask 1A12/1/4
Investigation summary: Suspected infection in flask led to the CL1000 unit B8650 being discarded. QC analysis found the samples to be positive for bacteria or fungi. Infection is linked to two other infected units from earlier that week. Infected CL1000 was not harvested on day of discovery.
Impact Assessment: The infection was discovered before harvest no 10. That means a possible loss of harvest crude approx. 300 mL. The flask was not harvested at the day of discovery. B8650 harvests 1-9 are likely to have no impact as there is often a strong smell from infected flasks, as well as change in colour and turbidity, and we expect to discover infections at an early time point.  If the cells were producing for a siginificant time in an infected container, they can become stressed and either produce low amounts of product or worst case produce something else. As cells were removed on 18.SEP.2023, and the infection was determined to be newly originated, this is not considered a risk to the product. Furthermore, even if there was a beginning of infection the elution step during FPLC is performed with at a low pH buffer which would destroy any growth in the product. Finally, the 0,2 µm filtration would eliminate any possibility of microorganisms growing in the final product. Therefore infection of the final product is considered unlikely, and product quality is not affected.
Root cause description: Infection is likely to come from infected B8626 and B8629 which were also housed in incubator DIA025. In NC201 for infected B8626 and B8629 it was concluded a probable root cause to be contamination from operator to and around the harvest port using unsterile materials and/or equipment because the medium was of a turbid, yellowish hue typically resulting from Staphylococcus or Streptococcus contaminations, strains typically found on human skin. Operators are operating as in class C room in an unclassified room. Although operator are taking great precautions, there is always a risk for contamination.
Classification justification: The results from QC services was positive for bacterial or fungal infection.

Non Conformance Title: EC volume above alarm threshold in ASM
Investigation summary: The EC chamber was filled over the limit and the possible reasons could be clogging of bioreactors with cells. EC cell removal was performed a day before the incident so the likelihood for clogging EC chamber is low.  Leakage from the DO IN LOW tubing was discussed with the service provider. It was suggested that the leakage could occur due to constriction in the IC circuit tubing between the bellows pump out port and bioreactor bottom end ports, which is less likely for large these tubings.  Also it was discovered that the outflow pump was not functioning in the ASM (NC210). This could have led to increased volume in the EC chamber (in addition to the IC chamber) and increased pressure within the system which resulted to leakage of medium. Failure of pump/motor can occur during normal use due to wear and tear in the instrument.
Impact Assessment: The incident led to termination of production batch. The wet EC vent filter as well as leakage from the tubing will compromise the sterility of the system, so the production was terminated earlier than originally thought. The overflooding of medium could have caused entering of liquid from IC or EC chamber to the electronics of the ASM which could have damaged the ASM. Since the incident was discovered before the liquid level reached the electronics, this electronics were not affected.  No impact on the product because the harvest flask was replaced right after the incident and also the non-conformance affected the instrument itself rather than the cells present.
Root cause description: As discussed in the Investigation" section, the most likely reason for the non-conformance is in Outflow pump. Failure of outflow pump increase system volume in bioreactor leading to increase volume in IC and EC chamber as well as pressure within the tubings. The pump status is checked during preventive maintenance, which was overdue according to the supplier's recommendation. SOP0060 recommends preventive maintenance every 5 year but it is not a requirement.  On the other hand, failure in the outflow pump was not checked when the non-conformance occured but was discovered during start of a new batch. So, the other possibilities for the non-conformance that are relevant based on the condition of the ASM can not be neglected. EC error rate alarm can also occur due to clogging of the fibers in bioreactor. Based on the in-process parameters, EC fall pressure and cycling time measured the day before the incident occured, it is less likely that the non-conformance occurred due to clogged bioreactor. The possible reason for leakage from DO IN LOW tubing can be due to constriction in the IC circuit tubing between bellows pump out port and bioreactor bottom end ports. This is also less likely due to the type of tubing present in this section of flowpath. This is further supported by the picture (Attachment 3) taken just after the incident occurred, where no any signs of kink in these tubing can be seen.  Cells adhering in the fibers of bioreactor is a common process. The amount of cells adhering is dependent upon cells characteristics. Some clones are well-known for adhering to fibers leading to clogged bioreactors earlier than the other. 2D2 is a relatively new clone which we culture in ASM and we don't have much information regarding its growth in bioreactor. Once cells clog the bioreactor, there is not much that can be done to continue production. So no corrective action will be performed for possible clogging of bioreactor.  So the root cause for the non-conformance is likely to be failure in the outflow pump/motor, with a contributing cause of accumulated cells in the system. "
Classification justification: The incident led to termination of production batch. if not reacted upon, It could have led to overflooding of medium from EC chamber to electronics in ASM, which will damage the ASM.

Non Conformance Title: Skipped a column during serial dilution- ELISA
Investigation summary: The deviation occurred due to lost of focus during pipetting/ serial dilution.
Impact Assessment: According to SOP1007 version 5.0, pt. 10, ELISA run must fulfil the following technical specification to be approved:  - The standard giving a linear area  - Standard curve and sample titration curve must have similar shape.  - Duplicates must be comparable  The ELISA run, E6366, meets all the criteria for approval of ELISA run (See attachments: Attachment-1", "Attachment- 2 NC207.png"). Thus, the results are considered to be valid and not impacted by the deviation.   In-process ELISA is performed so that production can be planned by knowing daily production and the amount produced, to be able to adjust accordingly. This can cause the production to be terminated early if sufficient yield is reached, to save on material and resources. Other than this, the result will not lead to alteration of production parameters or product quality. MY-11 was not adjusted based on this ELISA and analysis fulfilled the technical specification. Therefore this incident had no impact on the production timeline or quality."
Root cause description: Mistake during pipetting resulted in a skipped column due to lack of focus. ELISA method requires both good technique and focus of pipetting.
Classification justification: This is an in-process control and the results are used by production management to plan production. Analysis is considered valid according to technical requirements in the ELISA procedure, and no adjustments were made based on these results. No impact on product quality or timeline.

Non Conformance Title: SDS-Page QC Analysis for B8678 failed the criteria of the product specification.
Investigation summary: Investigation cleared training, reagents, samples and equipment as potential root causes. The image created from the development appears smeared or blury, most likely due to movement of DIA066 during development or lack of cleaning after use. Additionally, the analysis yielded comparatively low intensity of the bands detected in samples of B8678. Due to these anomalies system suitability failure for SDS680 is concluded and the analysis will be retested.
Impact Assessment: As the investigation has led to the conclusion that the system suitability failure was due to technical issues, this has no impact on the final product or its quality. The product quality will be evaluated by the re-test of B8678 QC samples. If this analysis adheres to the technical requirements of SOP1066 and the product specification of project 2023-29, the product can be released. If retest is successful, the delivery timeline can be met.
Root cause description: Anomalies in the analysis SDS680 can not be established by this investigation. Image from analysis is blurry most likely due to movement of DIA066 while developing, or lack of cleaning the the stain free tray after use. A most likely contributing factor is the difference in sample intensity within the same analysis, causing lower intensity bands to be obscured from software detection.
Classification justification: QC analysis for final product prohibit its release hereby affecting the delivery timeline and is a direct reflection of product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Quarterly trend review not performed for DIA024
Investigation summary: Investigation uncovered that trend review had not been performed since 02.08.2022. Instrument had been turned off due to inactivety and deactivated in the maintenance list. when the instrument was turned back on, this list was not updated as is according to routine. Root cause investigation explored training, equipment, system, procedure and other potential root causes.
Impact Assessment: Trend review is a preventative measure to detect long term issues like recurrent errors or drift in data for sirius monitored instruments. Daily monitoring and routine assures that instruments are within designated acceptance criteria and calibration. Trend review was performed for the missing period, and DIA024 was re-activated in the document used for alerting upcoming maintenance of instruments. The trend review performed uncovered no anomalies in the data, and instrument performed according to acceptance criteria. Maintenance list is updated with the date of this trend review. Therefore, no impact of this deviation could be identified.
Root cause description: Root cause investigation uncovered that SOP4032 has significant weaknesses when explaining start and shut-down procedure, this is considered to be the main root cause of this deviation. In addition,  supporting tools like the equipment maintenance list has outdated information both concerning procedures for equipment and responsibillities. This is considered to be a contributing root cause.
Classification justification: Trend review is a preventative measure to detect long term issues like recurrent errors or drift in data for sirius monitored instruments. Daily monitoring and routine assures that instruments are within designated acceptance criteria and calibration.

Non Conformance Title: Outflow pump motor not rotating
Investigation summary: The pump motor for DIA030 was malfunctioning, discovered during pre-inoculation phase of startup. It is believed to be caused by mechanical wear and tear over time. Service supplier was contacted and a procedure of switching the motor for a functioning was performed using instructions from service supplier. CAPA191 is proposed to add a section for pump testing at the beginning of the starting of maximizer procedure.
Impact Assessment: The pump failed before cells were inoculated. The cycling or pumping pattern has not been abnormal after cells were introduced in inoculation phase. The new pump operated within all parameters for the tests proposed by service supplier. This non-conformance does not impact the product quality nor the production timeline.  DIA079 is currently not qualified for use and as impact from this NC is also missing a functioning outflow pump.
Root cause description: Most likely mechanical failure due to wear and tear of the outflow pump motor.
Classification justification: Potential effect on the validated state of equipment.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage in drain connected to ASM
Classification justification: The leakage leading to spillage of medium on the floor in cold room had little to no impact on ASM and the environment of the cold room as ASM was in pre-inoculation phase, outflow is unidirectional and small in amount, and environmental montitoring results came back with results within limit.

Non Conformance Title: Customer not followed after test production.
Classification justification: No deviation from protocol could established.

Non Conformance Title: Customer vist was not followed up according to internal procedure
Investigation summary: A costumer visits to Axis Shield in Dundee Scotland took place without a summary report being produced. The impact of deviation was established to be low as there is no loss of information. The root cause investigation concluded that process is considered the main root cause, as the difficult landscape of customer visits are not accounted for. A contributing root cause was also identified to be the departments lack of resources at the time of deviation.
Impact Assessment: The absence of meeting reports can lead to loss of customer information which eventually can affect customer satisfaction. However, the impact is considered low as the same information is stored in other formats than a summary report (presentations/agendas/email correspondence). There is no risk to product quality nor production timeline as no product is affected by this deviation.
Root cause description: The investigation identified that process is considered the main root cause of this deviation. The difficult landscape of customer visits are not accounted for and additional tools are necessary to support the users of the costumer management procedure. CAPA178 is raised to create a template for the summary report, facilitating the process. CAPA177 is raised to cross-reference SOP5003 with the new document. In addition, manpower was identified to be a contributing root cause due to business development departments lack of resources, which is no longer the case.
Classification justification: No potential impact on the product quality, customer satisfaction, product quality/safety effect, validated state of equipment/process/system, negative impact on employee safety, or incompliance issue with customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: Customer complaint definition is not clearly explained in the internal procedure
Classification justification: There is no risk for business with customer as we always ensure to follow up with customers. It has not been a reoccuring problem, but we open CAPA for it and update SOP5035 with definition on what should be registered as a customer complaint (ref. CAPA184)

Non Conformance Title: Centrifuge fail under run
Classification justification: No potential effect on product quality (flow run): it is very unlikely that the extra spinning time would have an impact to the cells' characteristic. In addition, the cells were not used since the procedure was aborted due to problem with flow cytometer No potential effect on processes: service was performed on centrifuge very shortly after incident No impact to thawed cells THAW864 and THAW865. These were used for cloning for productivity test which succeeded without issue.  No impact to CL1000 units B8738-40. There was a heightened risk of infection after moving cells out of clean room, but no infection was observed during run.

Non Conformance Title: Harvest not performed for Clone 581
Classification justification: As crude impacted was discarded this has no impact to product quality.  Final product according to customer request was already processed, therefore no impact ot timelines or customer agreement.

Non Conformance Title: FACS Calibur Intrument unable to perform beads Calibration before flow cytometry analysis
Investigation summary: Instrument needs a full wash before analysis. The full wash was not performed acurately by Nextera AS before analysis.
Impact Assessment: The instrument serves as an in-process control (IPC) tool for selecting fractions to be pooled in the preparation of the product. Additionally, it plays a crucial role in the quality control (QC) release of the final product. Analysis was repeated after correction. The values for the beads met the criteria, proving that the root cause was identified. However, we discovered a new issue that is addressed in NC221 and NC222.    The deviation had small impact on internal timelines and no impact on timelines for delivery (planned delivery: end of January).   The deviation has a financial impact since more resources and additional material were needed to carry out an investigation.   There is no impact for product quality.
Root cause description: The beads could not be detected by the instrument due to the instrument not being properly washed. It was agreed with the supplier that a full wash should be performed although this is not covered by service agreement. Super user was absent the day instrument should be washed. The task was delegated to one person of his team who did not perform the washing correctly. The service supplier does not provide good enough service to us.
Classification justification: Results from flow is required to perform selection of fractions to prepare the product. Analysis is also used for QC released

Non Conformance Title: Context of the organisation not sufficiently described
Investigation summary: The QA representative did not check for compliance to ISO9001:2015 during the last document updates. It was assumed this had already been performed during the previous document update. What is more, the document was audited several times during the certification audits, and inspectors reported no compliance issues.
Impact Assessment: No impact on bussiness, customers, or quality of provided services.
Root cause description: The compliance level with the ISO9001 standard was not addressed in the previous document revision, as it was wrongly assumed that this had already been performed during the last document revision (Personel). The CAPA93 for the updating period revision process is currently pending execution. Therefore, no additional CAPA will be initiated for that aspect. What is more, the document was never internally audited, as previously, the pre-inspection process was mainly focused on the production and logistic processes (Process).  The CAPA32 for updating the Internal audit process was recently executed and is pending effectiveness evaluation. Therefore, no additional CAPA will be initiated for that aspect.
Classification justification: No impact on product quality, safety or validation status.

Non Conformance Title: Document retention time not justified
Classification justification: No impact on product quality, safety or validation status

Non Conformance Title: Customer impact and business risk not evaluated for non-conformance
Investigation summary: The impact on business / organisations is not part of the initial NC classification but is investigated in the impact assessment step.
Impact Assessment: No impact on business/organisation as this aspect is addressed during the investigation of the NC, where applicable. Refer to SOP5034 Impact assessment” section 5.4.3."
Root cause description: NA, as business and organisation impact is addressed in the Impact Assessment section, not in the initial classification. The low-risk NC will unlikely impact customer satisfaction, so no further impact assessment is performed for them.
Classification justification: The original non-conformance had a high negative impact on the business.

Non Conformance Title: Positive control do not fulfill Technical requirements (flow cytometry).
Investigation summary: Unidentified elements having the same size as lymphocytes and monocytes are detected negative for CD45. It could be attributed to abnormal population of white blood cells that are negative for CD45 (Ex: blasts), the presence of unlysed cells, or the existence of cell debris. It is unlikely that a blood giver present a high amount of CD45 negative cells as health conditions related to this is rare or would make the blood giver inelligeable for blood giving. The FSC/SSC scatter from all samples from 21.11.2023 showed an unexpectedly high amount of events. We suspect these events to be caused by unlysed RBCs, cell debris, or other types of debris, since they can have the same size as lymphocytes and monocytes. Precise gating becomes challenging when populations are not distinctly separated, particularly in the presence of significant cell debris. Large debris would contribute to counting false negatives in the CD45 positive control sample.  After performing many tests and thorough investigations, we determined that the debris origin was not due to suboptimal cell labelling. In particular, we ruled out suboptimal RBCs lysis, problems with reagents, equipment (pipettes), operator-to-operator variations, and environment. In NC222 it was highly suspected that there was a problem with sheath fluid (reported in NC222). We suspect it is likely that the sheath fluid was contaminated and that the samples were contaminated due to sheath fluid backflow into the tube sample. The reason for sheath fluid backflow is not entirely clear. The main suspected reasons for sheath fluid backflow are wear-out of the rubber ring, pressure irregularities and contaminated sheath fluid. The last of the three is the most likely. Under normal conditions and in our setup, sheath fluid should not impact the results unless sheath fluid is contaminated. Contaminated sheath fluid is probably due to improper cleaning routine of the tank where the sheath fluid is stored (See NC222 where this issue is addressed). Positive control for re-analysis of fractions performed on the 16.01.2024 was within acceptance criteria.
Impact Assessment: The results obtained from this flow cytometry analysis (21.11.2023) are dismissed as a consequence of this deviation. Fractions weree re-analysed after identifying the likely root cause. Fractions were selected based on the re-analysis and the final product was prepared. QC was performed and passed. Therefore this deviation do not have impact on product quality. Internal timelines were delayed by several weeks. External timelines remain unaffected, as the product is still scheduled for delivery within the agreed-upon timeframe with the customer (end of january).
Root cause description: Main root cause: Samples contaminated by sheath fluid Secondary root cause: drift in FSC/SSC settings due to unclean sheath fluid
Classification justification: Results from flow is requireed to perform selections of fractions to prepare the product. analysis is also used for QC released

Non Conformance Title: Instrument FACS Calibur failed during analysis of samples
Investigation summary: Instrument failed during IPC flow cytometer analysis due to drift in the FSC/SSC settings that is likely due to sheath fluid contaminated sheath fluid. Contaminants in the sheath fluid can impact the FSCSSC results and drift would be observed if the sheath fluid contaminants are not homogenously resuspended. The las bit of sheath fluid was used from a tank/reservoir that was not washed regularly. A new flow analysis with Calibur using water as sheath fluid showed no drift in FSC/SSC (performed as part of investigation) confirming the hpothesis.   Re-analysis of the fraction was performed on 16.01.2024. No drift in FSC and SSC observed.
Impact Assessment: The instrument serves as an in-process control (IPC) tool for selecting fractions to be pooled in the preparation of the product. Additionally, it plays a crucial role in the quality control (QC) release of the final product. The results obtained from this flow cytometry analysis performed on the 21.11.2023 are dismissed as a consequence of this deviation.Fractions were re-analysed on the 16.01.2024 after identifying the likely root cause. Fractions were selected based on the re-analysis, and the final product was prepared. QC was performed and passed all acceptance criteria. Therefore, this deviation does not have an impact on product quality.  External timelines remain unaffected, as the product is still scheduled for delivery within the agreed-upon timeframe with the customer (end of January).
Root cause description: The observed drift in FSC/SSC scatter, affecting accurate cell population gating, is likely attributed to potential contaminants inside the sheath fluid. These contaminants may originate from factors like prolonged storage of the FACS flow solution, posing a risk of microbial growth, and the potential accumulation of salt on the faucet's tip.
Classification justification: Results from flow is required to perform selection of fractions to prepare the product. Analysis is also used for QC released

Non Conformance Title: Reference batch used in SDS684 not suitable for quality control
Classification justification: Product performs according to the requirements of product specification and SOP1066 Version 3.0, this deviation does not reflect product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Samples analysed in singles rather than duplicates in MYCO176
Classification justification: Results from MYCO176 (Ref: Attachment 1) are negative and the clone has since been tested negative again as MYCO184 (Ref: Attachment 2) in connection with cellbanking.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong batch was reported in NC134
Classification justification: The correct batch number was used in NC134, and the correct NC number was referenced in the batch B8529 documentation.  The NC134 was fully investigated before the batch was released r (no impact on product quality).

Non Conformance Title: Technical requirements not fulfilled in isotyping of clones 581, 72-5A4 and 356
Investigation summary: All technical aspects of the worksheets were reviewed and approved. Training, maintenance, and calibration were verified in this investigation. Re-testing was performed for all the deviating clones, with the same results. Contamination was deemed improbable due to results of re-test and procedure for handling sample. Three potential causes remained including host serum immunoglobulins, test cross-reactivity or non-monoclonal hybridomas. Neither of which could be verified. The Pierce kit was determined to be less fit or use and its method will be replaced. Supplier complaint will be initated.
Impact Assessment: The production timeline of cellbank project 2023-34 has seen a delay due to the investigation of this non-conformance. There is no impact on product quality, as the reported out-of-specification (OOS) results were not confirmed and were found to be due to nonspecific binding with the Thermo Fisher assay used. All cell banks in question were tested using the Roche IsoStrip and the Bio-Rad Mouse Monoclonal Antibody Isotyping Test Kit. Specifications were met when two different assays were used, and no secondary nonspecific bands were present in these tests. Refer to the full investigation in DEV166 Repor.
Root cause description: The recent rise in multiple band occurrences across several cell banks/products suggests that the current isotyping method may not be robust enough for the samples tested. Tesing with 2 differnt comercial kits, and several comercial and in house clones, demostrated that Thermo Scientific™ Pierce Rapid Antibody Isotyping Kit is not suitable and can produce unriable results. Refer to report from feasibility study DEV 166.
Classification justification: This non-conformance is directly correlated to the product quality. All for the cell banks produced (2023-34, 2023-38, 2023-41) and for future production with these clones is affected by these isotope results.

Non Conformance Title: Incomplete information on MCB3 vials label of 581
Investigation summary: A root cause analysis was performed evaluating people, equipment, documentation, materials and process. It was concluded that non conformance could be considered a consequence of insufficient training of operators and a suboptimal process.
Impact Assessment: Re-labeling of frozen cryovials will put the cells at risk, since it requires thawing. Therefore no re-labeling will be executed.  To mitigate the risk of the missing information was commented in internal cell stock registry.  Apart from the lack of information there is no impact on product quality nor production timeline.
Root cause description: As established in the investigation the main root cause of this non-conformance is considered to be System/Process. This is supported by recurrence with similar deviation. More specifically, the lack of reinforcement under a critical step of the process. CAPA168 has already been initiated to prevent errors on product vials. CAPA181 will therefore be raised to extend this preventative measure to include cellbank vials.  In addition, the operators who executed the procedure lack experience and seem to have received suboptimal training. Therefore, People/Human error is considered i potential contributing cause. The current subject matter expert will review the training module used before operators YNE and JSS  will receive additional training in this procedure in accordance with CAPA197.
Classification justification: Non-compliance with procedure SOP0002 Version 2.0, Section 9.4 for project 2023-38 and 2023-44 cellbanks. A significant incompliance issue with customer agreements (Quality Agreement, Product Specification etc.)

Non Conformance Title: Monthly cleaning of CO2 incubator DIA169 not performed on time
Investigation summary: Monthly maintenance of CO2 incubator DIA169 was not performed due to inexperience of operator with cleanroom routines.
Impact Assessment: No impact on production as the chance for infection is low, bioreactors and cell flasks are equipped with filters, and both flasks and shelves are frequently wiped with ethanol. Incubator is located in cleanroom. No incidence of infection in any of the CL1000s has been detected.
Root cause description: Root cause is determined to be inexperience of operator with the current routines
Classification justification: Low likelihood of impact on production as the chance for infection is low, bioreactors and cell flasks are equipped with filters, and both flasks and shelfs are frequently wiped with ethanol. Incubator is located in cleanroom. No incidence of infection in the CL1000s has been detected to the date of 09.01.2024.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong QC samples used for mycoplasmatesting of clones 86-11 and 86-14
Classification justification: The QC samples used for MYCO186 were handled aseptically and was not compromised. The same samples could therefore still be used for QC ELISA. The Mycoplasma test was repeated successfully in MYCO187 with the correct samples stored in -20 °C. Therefore, this non-conformance has no impact on product quality or production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA analysis failed due to lack of reference curve
Investigation summary: ELISA run E6397 did not have a measurable standard reference curve. This could be the result of a mixup of the reference standard sample, or lack of pipetting the standard reference sample. The results from this ELISA were not critical information thus no repeat analysis was performed.
Impact Assessment: The production batch tested, B8725-27, had already been analysed with ELISA during previous harvests (H2 and H5) giving valid results. The information gained from testing H5 and H10 would not change production timeline as the production units had already been ended at the time of this analysis.  This is just an in process control test, not producing any final information regarding the product from these production batches.  There is no impact to product quality.
Root cause description: Probably a small distraction during sample titration or sample dilution process.
Classification justification: The production was already finished and earlier ELISA result was available from H2 and H5 for estimation of antibody yield during purification (E6378). This NC does not impact product quality as it was an in process control, and it does not impact production timeline as the production was already terminated as scheduled. ELISA E6397 was run for information only.

Non Conformance Title: Insufficient volume aliquot for QC analysis.
Classification justification: No QC tests have been impacted by lack of sample. The smaller volume does not change nor impact the quality of the samples for any QC tests.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected infection in CL1000 unit for 1A12/1/4 batch B8715
Classification justification: The flask was removed at harvest 14 and the production was intended to be ended at harvest 17. The amount of crude lost would be about ~100 mL. As we are contiuously producing a lot of 1A12/1/4, this loss has no impact on the production timeline and expectations. The results from QC services were negative for any bacterial or fungal growth, there are thus no risk of impact to it or any other production units.

Non Conformance Title: Reference batch used for ELISA analysis had different subtype of Isotype from sample tested
Classification justification: The analysis is a in-process control and its results is used to calculate the expected yield of each production. Since production was a test production and ended as planned, the deviation has no impact on production timeline or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: EC error rate exceeded for ASM production B8731
Investigation summary: Alarm for exceeded EC volume triggered before working hours 19.12.2023 due to buildup of cells. Cell removal procedure performed at 20.12.2023 to reduce buildup.
Impact Assessment: The product quality of harvest 4 and onward does not suffer any impact due to this NC as can be seen in in-process parameters being within expected values.  If the EC volume had reached the filter and entered the instrument, it could have severe consequences for the machinery. This could not have happened as the filter was confirmed untouched by visual inspection, the medium has a distinct pink colour and would be easily visible.
Root cause description: Cell debris buildup inside the inner pipe in EC chamber triggered this alarm, as UCUS99.05 clone is known to grow quickly and clog the EC chamber and/or bioreactors. The cell debris buildup in EC chamber is unavoidable, as cells grow exponentially. Therefore the root cause is considered to be material (cell bank).
Classification justification: Impacts production rate and cell proliferation. possible impact to product quality needs to be investigated.

Non Conformance Title: Cell membrane leakage from CL1000 production units
Investigation summary: Leakage of cell chamber within CL1000 units B8743 for NGAL-1-2322-455 and B8746-7 for NGAL-1-903-430 discovered after and during outer medium exchange of harvests 3 and 5 respectively. The remaining bioreactors for NGAL-1-2322-955 will be sufficient for the production output expected by project 2023-31, while new NGAL-1-903-430 cells was thawed and inoculated for production.
Impact Assessment: No impact to product quality. Harvest 4 and 5 from the identified leaking CL1000 units will be stored for use in realization of product. Production timeline for project 2023-32 may become slightly delayed due to the needed restart of production.
Root cause description: Issues with the CL1000 bioreactors have been seen to occur several times the last year.
Classification justification: B8746-47 were the only two CL1000 units in production for NGAL 1-903-430, forcing the immediate startup of new cells for new production units to meet production timeline. Re-occurrence.

Non Conformance Title: AcuSyst Maximizer for production B8731 had medium temperature under set point.
Classification justification: The hybridoma cells do not die at 33.5°C. The cells may proliferate slower, but based on ELISA results before and after the event, the productivity of the bioreactor did not change. Product quality is not affected, nor is the production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: AcuSyst Maximizer Stopped cycling
Investigation summary: Cycling process had halted during EC fall". The lack of cycling could have a negative effect on the cells' living conditions. A cell debris removal procedure was immediately initiated to relive the instrument and resume the cycling. ELISA analysis show a significant drop in antibody production following this incident. "
Impact Assessment: Cells under stressed conditions could potentially produce unwanted survival compounds or suffer unwanted mutations. However it is very unlikely that the product is affected from this non-conformance as the ASM had halted cycling for only a short period, within a working day. The cells were still receiving nutrients from medium as the EC and IC medium pumps were not stopped. Harvest 6  was immediately determined not to be impacted as the connected harvest had about 1200 mL crude, and at maximum 4 hours at a pump speed of 7 mL/h would only yield about 28 mL. Also, there is a certain delay in effect on the cells, therefore any impact would not have been applicable to harvest 6. Harvest 7 and 8 were initially placed under quarantine to. As it is concluded that there is no plausible impact to the product, quarantine of these harvests can be lifted and used for downstream processing.  Enough products have been produced for the first delivery without any change in the production plan. The second and third delivery will need to have a new production started. This has an economical impact as a new production flowpath unit would be required.
Root cause description: Buildup of cellular debris within the central pipe used for volumetric measurements within the EC.  5 Whys Why did the cellular debris build up within the central pipe?       UCUS 99.05 is known to be a cell line with expansive growth. The EC chamber has been observed to have a considerable amount of cells multiple times through the production run leading to multiple cell debris removal procedures.  Was one of these removal procedures done before this NC?     A cell removal procedure was planned to be performed following work day 05.01.2024 due to the increase in cellular debris.  Why was this removal procedure not done sooner?                 The cell removal procedure will remove both living and dead cells from the system, as well as stopping the cycling for the entire process. It is a stressing process in itself and usually lead to reduced productivity until the cells recover. It is hard to establish an exact time cell removal procedures should be executed as the effect of cell debris buildup can vary from cell lines and how the debris is building up. The primary issue in this case was the cell debris inside the central pipe which is difficult to assess.  Why is the central pipe more difficult?                                         The central pipe has a small opening at the bottom floor of the EC chamber. The EC removal procedure described in SOP0060 v. 3.0 section 9.27 is unable to fully clear the contents of the central pipe due to its position and enclosed construction.  Why does the central pipe have such a high impact?                  Cellular debris inside the central pipe will prevent the Maximizer from accurately assess how much volume is inside the EC chamber, which in turn will stop or hinder effective cycling as the cycling pattern is purely based on the volume of the EC chamber.  Operations Manual for AcuSyst Maximizer propose that the default transfer volume to the EC chamber should gradually be increased to 140 mL for productions with two BRX, unless metabolic activity is low. The standard for productions at Curida Diatec is two BRX, yet this optimization has not been followed. This optimisation is anchored in CAPA219.
Classification justification: Lack of efficient medium cycling can cause a buildup of dead cells, debris, and cellular waste in the BRX. This could hamper growth and productivity, which in turn could impact production timeline and possible product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Yearly calibration not performed for cold room DIA283 within deadline
Investigation summary: Annual service of cold room was delayed by a few weeks as responsibilities were unclear and overview excel sheet was incorrectly updated.
Impact Assessment: The temperature in the cold room DIA283, is continuously monitored by two Sirius temperature sensors in our monitoring system Sirius. Two sensors measure the area with high and low temperature in the cold room. The temperature recordings from Sirius monitoring system shows that the temperature has been within the acceptance limit (Ref. Attachment 2- Sirius), even though the maintenance was not performed within the deadline. There have been no incidents relating to the abnormal temperature since installation. In addition, environment monitoring of cold room is performed every week according to SOP1076 version 3.0. The environmnet in the cold room was within the required microbial limit during this period showing no issues with the cold room's environment either (Ref: Attachment 3- Environment monitoring). Therefore this non-conformance did not impact the cold room environment or product quality.
Root cause description: True root cause was determined to be unclear responsibility due to organisational changes. A contributing factor was that the service date on excel sheet with overview of instrument was accidently changed to NA instead of service date. This moves the instrument on the bottom of the list together with unused instruments and operators do not get notified for deadline.
Classification justification: The yearly service is performed to ensure the instrument is working optimally. Temperature monitored by Sirius monitoring system showed that the cold room maintained temperature within the specified threshold during the period. Therefore this had no impact on product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Documentation for maintenance of refrigerators (minibar) connected to ASM units not performed
Investigation summary: The operators working with ASM have been performing the cleaning procedure but did not think about the documentation of the cleaning in the proper control form. The control form for the refrigerator is also together with analysis lab's instrument. It was not quite obvious and easy to remember for the operators to notice it in the folder.
Impact Assessment: The incident impact the documentation system of refrigerators. The refrigerators were cleaned after every batches with ethanol and is documented in DOC0060.4. But documentation for cleaning routine for refrigerators before starting of ASM batch is not documented. Even though the documentation was missing for the refrigerators, the operators working with ASM has confirmed following the SOP and performing the cleaning procedure. The operators follow the usual routine for wash of refrigerators for the refrigerators connected to ASM as well. Since the documentation is not availabe, there remains a possibility that the cleaning routine was not followed all of the time. However, if the refrigerators had not been cleaned for more than a year even though in use, there is a risk for some incident related to possible infection. No NCs related to possible infection has been registered for the refrigerators connected to ASM. So, it can be justified that the impact of this NC on production batches is low.
Root cause description: SOP0060 does not refer to the proper documentation of cleaning of refrigerator. The DOC that should be followed to register cleaning of minibar are not directly related to ASM. Other instrument worksheet like ASM (DOC0060.6), and pH probe (DOC0060.5) have document number similar to ASM SOP (SOP0060). Only Doc4045.3 is not placed together with other worksheet related to ASM. DOC4043.3 is placed in Analysis lab in the same folder as the other documents for instruments in Analysis lab.  The operators involved in the process for starting of ASM are also trained in cleaning of refrigerators. Starting of ASM is a relatively long process and requires SOP0060 and user manual for ASM to perform this process. Though SOP0060 mentioned to follow SOP4045 for cleaning of refrigerators, the operators performed the cleaning based upon their experience on cleaning of refrigerator instead of actively using SOP4045 while performing cleaning. So, the operators only followed the worksheets that were directly linked to SOP0060 and missed registration of minibars in DOC4045.3 as the opertors did not actively follow the SOP but performed the task based on experience.
Classification justification: The non-conformance does not have impact on the product or the instrument itself. The maintenance procedure were followed but not documented, thus will have low risk. The fridges keep harvest medium and harvested crude, and we have seen no infection in either of these during these productions.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA QC test for mAb12 batch B8749 failed
Classification justification: As both reference batch and sample to be tested failed, this was identified as a technical issue. Analysis was repeated successfully with another vial, containing a proven functioning lot of coat. No impact to product quality or timelines.

Non Conformance Title: CL1000 harvest froze inside refrigerator
Classification justification: Lower temperature works as a preservative for antibodies. There is no impact to the product quality from freezing the crude harvest.

Non Conformance Title: Concentration of vialled product below the required specification
Investigation summary: During OD adjustment, priming of cuvette is not performed after addition of small volume of buffer when the mesurement is performed several times before the final measurement. In addition, the blank set on spectrophotometer for measurement of bulk product and aliquotated product was different.
Impact Assessment: Due to the incident described in this incident, the product had to be kept on Quarantine". To release the product, customers approval for upconcentrating the product is required. The incident was consulted with customers and it was decided to upconcentrate the product. Thus, the concentration of the product was adjusted as required according to SOP0070 (Ref. Attachment- 3). This impacted the timeline for the delivery of product.  The OD range for the product is quite low and a small inaccuracy can lead to out of specification of the product. So, the sales team in Curida Diatec is discussing with the customer to broaden the OD window or to allow up-concentration of product without approval whenever the concentration becomes too low as we see this as a recurrent problem to a sensitive procedure. "
Root cause description: Inadequate mixing: The acceptable OD range for the product is quite small. When the operator adjusted the OD for bulk it was found to be within range but not after aliquotation. This could be due to inadequate mixing of the bulk solution. The operator performing the vialing process has a good experience working with the volume of bulk prepared in this vialing i.e. almost 5 liters, but the chances of inadequate mixing still remains.  Blank measurement: Measurement of blank can have small impact on setting the baseline for OD measurement. The OD criteria for this product has a narrow range and a small impact on the baseline could impact the final result. Setting of blank was performed a few times in addition to repeat the OD measurement but the result was below the specification regardless. Since the OD range for the product is quite small, a small change in blank can invalidate the results for product. This could be a major cause as similar non-conformance is reported previously as well (Ref. OOSR021 in attachment 2). Inadequate priming: After addition of small volume of buffer, priming is not always performed. This is because the minor residue left over in the cuvette will affect the new reading of very little after very little additional buffer is added. This is, however, a possible root cause and the operaor will be more careful during this step in the future to always prime well before the last readings. This has since been brought up and discussed between all operators performing this procedure.
Classification justification: Non-compliance of final product with product specification

Non Conformance Title: Error in collection in non-conformance flow
Investigation summary: The NC record step 7 of workflow 9 was programmed so that all employees assigned to the QA team" function execute the step. This was discovered when an additional employee was assigned to that function group. The collection setting was corrected for workflow 9."
Impact Assessment: There was no impact on product quality, all NC reports (batch disposition) were written and approved correctly, and no information was lost or changed.  The NC workflow 9 was replaced with workflow 10 in 2023, which was also inspected, and no similar error was detected in the workflow setting.
Root cause description: The incorrect setting of the NC collection workflow was discovered. The collection was validated according to the protocol VP2021-224 BizzMine Software Validation testing, but those parts of the collection setting were outside the validation scope.  The error in the collection setting was probably due to a lack of operator experience in programming and testing eQMS software. All personnel involved in the first phase of QMS implementation received training. However, an error was overlooked due to a lack of experience from the personnel who executed testing. Several collections in BizzMine were implemented in 2022-2023 according to the established validation protocol template DOC5048.1, and a similar error was not observed.  This collection workflow is no longer in use, and all NCs are currently recorded in workflow 10, which does not have a similar error.
Classification justification: There was no impact on product quality, all NC reports (batch disposition) were written and approved correctly, and no information was lost or changed.  Potential impact on the validation status of the BizzMine Nonconformance collection. There was no impact on compliance with ISO9001 standards or customer agreements, and all NC approvals were executed correctly.

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect gowning by external service provider (Pingvin klima AS) and lack of training by FEU
Classification justification: This non-conformance is low risk, as it does not affect the validation state of equipment/process/system, product or documents. No impact on product quality or business.

Non Conformance Title: Flushing of drain system not performed in time
Classification justification: No production connected to drain was running at the time of non-conformance. This NC has no impact to any product, nor any production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Failed QC ELISA- OD below acceptance criteria
Investigation summary: The operator made an unusual observation from the ELISA washer which seemed that the washing step stopped before it was complete.
Impact Assessment: The deviation led to invalid result. The ELISA test had to be repeated and was successful (Ref. Attachment- 2).
Root cause description: While performing the test for batch B8782, the O.D. values observed was below the acceptance criteria for both the sample (product) and positive control (standard) (ref. Attachment-1). Since O.D. for both the sample and positive control was below the acceptance criteria, the result indicates the failure of experiment performed. During ELISA, there are several steps that can lead to failure in the process. These steps include, use of wrong reagents, calculation failure, insufficient incubation time during steps or improper pipeting. - Calculation failure: The calculation performed to dilute the samples (test and control) were checked and confirmed to be correct. So the chances of calculation error can be opted out. - Procedure: The procedure was performed according to the valid version of SOP. The operator (SBA) is trained in this procedure and has performed ELISA several times.  - Reagents: The reagents used are registered in the worksheet and were rechecked. It was confirmed that correct reagents were used.  - Pipeting error: Duplicates of both test and control samples in the experiment were not precicely comparable, but ok enough. The operator (SBA) who performed this test has a good experience with pipeting and ELISA. Nevertheless, the possibility for pipeting error can not be neglected.  - Unsual observation: The operator observed that the washing step after the blocking step was not complete or unusual as described in the investigation step. As seen in the result, the blocking step had most likely led to false negative signal due to masking of antigen-antibody reaction.  Thus, the possiblity of the result obtained from this experiment seems due to the wash step although there is a little possibility of pipeting error from the operator as well.   Since it was a random event that occurred with the ELISA washer once, the incident is considered as a one-time event and no corrective action is performed for now.
Classification justification: The non-conformance is classified as low risk as the analysis was repeated without irregularities and passed the QC test. This does not have an impact on the product quality or timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Introduction program was executed incorrectly for interns
Investigation summary: The interns who executed their internship at Diatec during Q1-Q2 2023 had not completed the Introduction Training Program. 4 interns missed 3-4 obligatory modules, mostly due to lack of time to execute.
Impact Assessment: No impact on product quality, students were not involved directly in any production activities, which could impact the finished product quality.  No impact on validating the status of equipment. There is no impact on compliance with ISO9001:2015 standard or any customer agreement. No impact on employee safety, as all interns received HSE training. The intern mainly missed the Introduction to Production/sales/Logistics/company module, which is just a presentation of different departments and department operations.
Root cause description: The introduction program is too comprehensive and unsuitable for students doing internships at the Curida Diatec. The internship is usually not longer than 12 weeks and approx 10 hours per week.  What is more, the direct manager of interns did not follow up on training or provide feedback on difficulties in completing this program in the defined time period.
Classification justification: No impact on product quality, students were not involved directly in any production activities, which could impact the finished product quality. No impact on employee safety, as all interns received HSE training. No impact on validating the status of equipment. There is no impact on compliance with ISO9001:2015 standard or any agreement with the customers.

Non Conformance Title: Performed non-reduced SDS analysis of NGAL 1-903-430 (B8783-85) and NGAL 1-2322-455 (B8786-88)
Classification justification: No impact on product quality or production timelines as the analysis was immediately repeated under correct conditions, and analysis was successful.

Non Conformance Title: Particle observed inside B8698 CL1000
Classification justification: No infection observed throughout the production. Therefore the particle can be considered sterile. No structural deterioration to the CL1000 could be identified. Therefore, production proceeded as expected. further processing of crude ensures that particle is not carried into the final product. this non-conformance is not considered to have an impact on production timeline or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected contamination in production for CL1000 due to leakage
Investigation summary: Infected prodution unit was identified to be noticably leaking from the outer medium chamber. Samples sent to QC Services confirmed microbial contamination.
Impact Assessment: 1A12/1/4 are produced on forecast to stock storage. This non conformance does therefore not have any impact on production timeline for this product.  The previous harvest of the affected unit was tested for contamination and confirmed free thereof. The unit was not harvested the day contamination was discovered, therefore there is no impact to any product.
Root cause description: Leakage from the outer chamber of the CL1000 could be seen when the unit was taken out of cleanroom for inspection. No antibiotics are used in this production, so if the medium of the bioreactor is exposed to the outside through a leak it will increase chances of contamination considerably. This is believed to be the most probable cause of this contamination.
