Operator SBA, who harvested the unit during harvest 2 (29.01.2024), explains that he did not note anything different during the handling of the flask that day.
Classification justification: This would in one sense be the fourth reoccurrence within 1 year, however, NC205 and NC201 are tied to the same time and event.  CL1000 unit had to be removed from production and discarded early in the production run (after H3), which lead to a big loss in crude.

Non Conformance Title: Responsibility for communication of change notification missing
Classification justification: In practice, the process of communication with the customer is defined. However, the responsibilities are not stated in the SOP

Non Conformance Title: Secondary ladder spread out during Western Blot Analysis
Classification justification: Only one ladder is needed to see that the gel electrophoresis was successful. The secondary ladder is primarily used as a practical tool to separate the gel in two sections (one side with known positive control and one for sample being tested). The analysis is based on antigen reactivity, of which the ladder is not. The spread of the secondary ladder does thus not impact the samples, nor the validity of the QC analysis.

Non Conformance Title: Extra band detected during SDS-PAGE analysis of IgM test sample
Investigation summary: Extra bands were detected for the test sample for B8808. However, extra bands were present in the control sample (B8082) and also test samples B8807. B8807 is an IgG molecule and usually no extra band in observed in the lanes. Since the extra bands were present on several samples, the contamination is not limited only to B8808.
Impact Assessment: This is a QC test for release of final product. This NC prevents release of product and impacts product delivery timeline.  Based on the investigation, the samples (B8807 and B8808) were rerun on SDS-PAGE and no extra bands were observed on the gel (Ref. SDS700 and SDS701). Both of the batches passed the QC requirements (technical requirement and QC approval requirement) of the product. Thus, the results indicates no impact on product or product quality.
Root cause description: There are several reasons that could result extra band during SDS-PAGE analysis. Some of the possible reasons are discussed below: 1) Sample preparation:  Some common factors that can degrade the sample leading to extra band on SDS-PAGE are temperature dependent denaturation, pH dependent fragmentation. It was discussed with the operator (YNE) who performed this test and the operator did not have any abnormal observation/incident during the test that could have impacted the result. The procedure for sample preparation for SDS-PAGE is specified in SOP1066 version 3.0, pt.9.3. According to the SOP, sample preparation steps includes, dilution of antibody sample to 0.1 mg/mL concentration with 1x PBS, mixing with sample buffer at protein-to sample buffer ratio of 1:1 and heating the sample/protein-buffer mixture to 95 degrees for 3 minutes. pH during sample preparation can be affected by 1xPBS buffer used for dilution. The batch of 1x PBS used for the analysis is P1x231123A and is registered in the worksheet. Same batch of 1x PBS was used for several other approved SDS-PAGE as well (eg. SDS699) with no problem. SDS-PAGE was performed again on the samples following the SOP and no similar bands were present on the re-run which indicates that the error could have occurred during sample perpartion in SDS697.  2) Sample degradation during storage: B8808 is an IgM molecule and degradation of IgM has been observed several times during storage. However, both B8808 and B8807 are relatively new product and the SDS-PAGE was performed within a week of production. Furthermore, B8807 is an IgG which is relatively stable from previous production experiences. Both of the product were produced at different time by different operators and the stored in cold room like other final products and control samples. The SDS-PAGE performed on other samples/final product after this run did not have similar problem showing the storage condition did not affect the other sample (eg. SDS699). There is less chance that only these two samples were not stored properly, though it can not be completely neglected. Thus, the possibility for degradation of these samples during storage is low.  3) Impurity in sample: Multiple band in SDS-PAGE can occur due to contamination of sample. Contamination of sample can occur at any stage of production from thawing of cells to final product perapartion as well as during sample preparation for SDS-PAGE. It can be confirmed that the bands on the gel did not arise from the sample buffer used to dilute the samples but from the sample itself as the empty lanes (lane 8-10) loaded with just sample buffer did not have any visible bands. In SDS697, weak bands can be observed on samples in lane 2-7, except lane 5. Inadequate purification during the purification step can lead to impurities in the sample. The purification of B8807 was performed on 29.01.2024 by operator ØHR and B8808 was purified on 30.01.2024 by operator SBA. The purification material used for purification of antibodies depends on the antibody isotype and was different for these two batches. As faint band was present on both the batches, chances of contamination of both of the sample produced at different time and by different operator is less likely, although possible.  As described above, the chances that the band arise from sample buffer is quite unlikely and degradation of product during storage in such a short period of time is quite low. Also the possibility for impurity in sample due to inadequte purification is also low, as the impurity was present on two samples that were purified by two operators at different time. Since extra band was present in both of the IgG (which is generally stable) together with IgM samples and were of different size, there is a chance that the extra bands could be degraded product of the sample itself. Since the bands were not present on the re-run (SDS700 and SDS701), the likelihood for degradation during storage and impurites is even lower. As described above, the most likely step for sample degradation is during its preparation. There are several factors that can degrade samples during its preparation like heating, pH, it remains as a most possible reason for the extra bands observed for samples in SDS697.        So the root cause for the NC is most likely during sample preparation that could have degraded the samples.
Classification justification: The test failed the QC test and prevents release of the product.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected contamination of CL1000 unit for 1A12/1/4
Investigation summary: Outer medium was thoroughly investigated under microscope by multiple operators. Sample sent to QC services returned negative for any microbial growth.
Impact Assessment: Loss of CL1000 production unit at an early harvests, H3 and H5.
Root cause description: Weakness in CL1000 construction. This has been a recurring issue which has lead to the creation of supplier complaints SCR070 and SCR071.
Classification justification: Multiple re-occurrences within 1 year. CL1000 units had to be removed from production early (H5 for B8770 and H3 for B8798).

Non Conformance Title: Duplicates not comparable for B8787 Quality Control
Classification justification: Since system suitability is assumed when the technical requirements of the procedure is failed. Therefore it does not reflect product quality.

Non Conformance Title: pH not meassured in final product B8782 as stated in product specification
Classification justification: The test was performed as per the immediate action and results was in accordance with product specification (Ref: Attachment 1).

Non Conformance Title: Particle observed inside CL1000 flasks during production
Classification justification: Similar particles have been previously observed, however, it did not have any impact on the product of the CL1000 flasks.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong version number and missing valdity date for DOC1076.4
Classification justification: The users can access all documents, such as procedures and production worksheets, only via the Document Management module in eQMS. When the operator prints a new production worksheet, the document is automatically labelled with a copy date. Therefore, it would be possible to trace the correct document version if needed. In addition, there is no risk to product quality or operating safety, as only a valid version of the document can be accessed by an operator via eQMS.

Non Conformance Title: OD not meassured before filtration for B8782
Classification justification: In-process measurements are tools for production management in planning. Product is finalized and  the desired yield and concentration is met,.  This non conformance has no impact on neither product quality nor production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Registered yearly cleaning of locked cabinet DIA293 in cold room not found for 2023
Classification justification: There has been no impact on the product or file samples kept in DIA293. Cleaning was most likely performed in 2023, but has not been documented correctly. Yearly cleaning was performed for DIA293 on 20.FEB.2024.

Non Conformance Title: Incubation time exceeded protocol in Quality control of B8787
Classification justification: This analysis (Ref: Attachment 1) failed the technical requirements and does not reflect the product quality. Therefore, this analysis is of no consequence to Western063, as it have already been failed on system suitability. The re-test, Western064, passed the requirements of the procedure and product specification (Ref: Attachment 2).

Non Conformance Title: Standard diluted to wrong concentration during Elisa
Classification justification: The analysis is an in-process control and its results are used to calculate the expected yield during a production. The in-process ELISA analysis was repeated and is considered to give a good indication of the antibody level during production, and there is no impact to product quality nor production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect information entered on several instances of the Medium registry.
Investigation summary: The medium with incorrectly registered information were all of standard design (1% FBS for outer medium, 10% for cell side medium in RPMI1640). Operators are trained in the medium creation process and it is believed with high certainty that the medium produced is correct. No hamper in proliferation or production could be noticed for the relevant cells and batches.
Impact Assessment: No hamper in proliferation or production could be noticed for the relevant cells and batches, thus there is not believed to be any impact to the product.  This does shine light on a weak link in our data registration for medium production. The medium registry and current electronic devices pose a threat to the data integrity and traceability of the system. CAPA88 has already been opened to address this issue.
Root cause description: Issues with electronic devices currently implemented inside the clean room.  A somewhat old-fashioned way of registering medium through excel as this does not always clearly show where your cursor is and allow for changing data in multiple instances through a simple missclick.
Classification justification: These errors concern minor typing error when entering information to medium registry.

Non Conformance Title: Labels for CoA not printed for PSS and MCB
Classification justification: All product has been labelled correctly, an additional label to be attached to CoA will be printed for each respective project.  No impact on product quality or timeline, HSE or customer agreement.

Non Conformance Title: Productivity test (re-cloning assay) for PSS3 cell bank of clone A8B5 faild
Investigation summary: Only 8 of total 26 re-cloning tests pass for growth of >30 wells total, <30% to confirm single cells and >90% productivity to pass the test for a productive cell line. This mean the test passes in less than in 31% of the performances. The SOP0013 version 2.0, Cloning of cell lines is the method used for testing of productivity of cells from a PSS bank. The test is based on growth of cells in 96 well plates. The growth has to be less than 30% to confirm that there is only one cell in each well. Some cell lines is hard to grow single and in small volumes in wells, and to reduce bad growth we usually add HFCS (Hybridoma Fusion and Culture Supplement) to the growth medium. It is also difficult to suspend the cells to low concentrations. Either the cell grow too bad or too good.
Impact Assessment: Poor growth of cells have been contradicted by how we've seen these cells grow before and after. Amount producing cells is acceptable according to SOP0013 version 2.0 Pt. 9.15 and Pt. 10.1.2.  We know the cells produce well from ELISA results for in process CL1000 production. This is an repeatedly observed issue but has never been reported.  This has no impact on product or timeline. A8B5 grew poorly last time a cellbank was made (15.JUL.2022) and Curida Diatec started this project to make a better PSS for use at a later time point.
Root cause description: The method is not representative as most cells that failed the method criteria performed well (produced well) during production in CL1000 bioreactors. What is more, the method is not well described in vague language with not well defined criteria, which makes it challenging to interpret results and allows for rerun analysis after satisfactory results are obtained.
Classification justification: Has no impact on product or timeline. A8B5 grew poorly last time a cellbank was made (15.JUL.2022) and Curida Diatec started this project to make a better PSS and MCB for later use. The cells look to grow good and produce well, but this has not been represented in our analysis. See impact assessment.

Non Conformance Title: Wrong final buffer used for Dialysis of 2023-44 5350 ND-5G4
Investigation summary: Information in PS was insufficient for buffer composition, and operators performing the activity did not enquire about it before the last dialysis into final buffer. Product was therefore dialysed in additional 2 rounds into correct final buffer.
Impact Assessment: As all products at Diatec are dialysed in standard PBS for the first round, the standard and the final buffer are similar buffers with the same amount of salt, and the final product was immediately continued for two rounds of custom buffer, this NC is not considered to have any impact on product quality or production timelines.
Root cause description: Root cause identified is system is not robust enough to ensure information flow when key personnel quit. CAPA raised to improve system.
Classification justification: This is considered a high risk Non conformance as it does not comply with a customer agreement (product specification for 2023-44).

Non Conformance Title: Suspected Infection in CL1000
Investigation summary: CL1000 unit B8763 were taken out when the outer medium appeared turbid (02.FEB.2024). Sampling revealed that the unit was contaminated with microbial growth and also H11 harvest which was last harvested from this unit, but upon repeat testing as the sample was clear it was found to be negative (therefore previous test is considered a false positive) and harvest 11 is confirmed to not be infected. CL1000 unit B8765 were taken out when harvest 14 appeared cloudy in storage (12.MAR.2024). Sampling revealed that the unit was contaminated with microbial growth. Harvests 11,12,13 from this unit were shown to not be infected, however H14 harvest appeared cloudy and is believed to be contaminated. During the QC testing for the investigation harvests 11-13 from the two batches have been sampled twice, returning positive the first time when they were sampled along with a contaminated CL1000 unit, and returning negative when sampled alone. However this has no impact on final outcome of investigation.
Impact Assessment: Loss of two production units from harvest 11 (B8763) and harvest 14(B8756).  Harvests 11-13 from the two batches have been sampled twice, returning positive the first time when they were sampled along with a contaminated CL1000 unit, and returning negative when sampled alone. Harvest 14 from B8765 is discarded.  Harvest 13 and below from B8763-8765 is considered accepted.
Root cause description: Difficult to determine, most likely handling of the CL1000, possibly connected to material issues.
Classification justification: Infection confirmed in both production unit and harvest 11 from samples sent to Curida Diatec QC services.

Non Conformance Title: B8807 kept outside storage specification before final approval
Investigation summary: ND-5G4 was likely picked up by mistake from cold room shelf of unapproved finished product when ADS from BD was moving multiple NGAL product vials from that same self to -70 degree freezer on Friday afternoon in preparation of a shipment of frozen NGAL-product the following Monday.
Impact Assessment: Customer was informed and asked if they wanted to receive the product for testing, and if ok they would only have to pay half price. As of 22.MAR.2024 it looks like customer will not use this product and it will be scrapped.
Root cause description: BD performed a task which wass not their task to perform and a mistake was made. QA alone should move product according to SOP1098. If certain people from QA are working remote, other QA personell should assist. There also needs to be better procedures or at least agreed upon guidlines in advance when we perform work for customers that do not follow our standard realisation of product. SOP 5051 for “Batch revision and approval” is being implemeted shortly (ref. change control CMID46). In this procedure is clearly marked who is moving the product from shelf to green area. We will add to this procedure, that any product, even this product which does not require QA approval can only be moved from that shelf by QA team. When new procedure will be approved in coming day, QA will give training for everyone in diagnostic and BD. The shelf in cold room will be marked Product is only to be moved off shelf by QA team"."
Classification justification: This non conformance can be categorized as  A significant non compliance issue with customer agreements". As product has been stored outside what is specified in the signed product specification for an unknown period of time."

Non Conformance Title: Visual check for aggregation of final product was not performed after vialling
Classification justification: The QC test must be performed to get the product approved for release from QA. As the missing test was spotted during technical review, and then the test was performed, and it is not reoccurent, the risk associated with the NC is low.

Non Conformance Title: Installation of flow path aborted due to sounds from exhaust fan of DIA030
Classification justification: As instrument was not used in production this is of no consequence to production or product quality. Since DIA030 is quarantined there is no danger to the equipment's validated state. New maximizer was started and used for project 2023-46.

Non Conformance Title: Precipitation observed before release of product B8780 and B8812. Product vials not stored appropriately with parafilm seal.
Investigation summary: During final approval of products B8780 and B8812 by QA, it was discovered that vials lacked sealing with parafilm (B8780) and observed precipitation in both products,  Product 5130 is known to precipitate in final buffer, this is well documented and communicated to customer. The product was re-processed and re-tested. Vials were ensured properly closed at discovery. Dilution of elute pre-dialysis was done intentionally, to not fall below the final concentration of antibody. This is deemed to be of no consequence to precipitation in final product.
Impact Assessment: 1. Vialling without parafilm: The purpose of sealing the vials with parafilm is done to protect the finished product and ensure that the vials stays closed at all times. The vials were confirmed closed upon discovery and sealing with parafilm was immediately performed. Therefore this has no impact or risk to product quality or timeline. 2. Precipitation of product: Re-processing of product can affect the concentration of final product which is a standard Quality control defined in product specification for product. UCUS 99.05 is known to precipitate in final buffer, this is well documented and communicated to customer also in this instance. B8780 and B8812 were reprocessed into B8825 and B8826 and quality control analysis were conducted for the new batches. Final re-processed batches pass both purity and concentration control in accordance with product specification. Hence, no impact to product quality can be identified. This impacts delivery time line, as due to re-processing, release and easter, product will not be shipped before week 15 (agreed with customer to avoid additional risk during shipping).
Root cause description: 1. Vialling without parafilm: The lack of sealing of final product B8780 with parafilm is due to a human error as trainer did not observe that this was not performed, despite being well familiar with the procedure and executing it correctly for the next batch.   2. Precipitation of product: Product 5130 is known to precipitate in final buffer, this is well documented and communicated to customer. This is the nature of the antibody and without changing the formulation of final buffer or final concentration of antibody this is expected to happen from time to time during storage of final product. Therefore, Material is considered a main root cause of this deviation.
Classification justification: Potential effect on product quality due to precipitation. Product needs re-processing and new QC testing prior to release.

Non Conformance Title: Duplicate not comparable during in process ELISA
Investigation summary: ELISA is a manual process and requires several pipetting steps. The operator used both reverse pipetting and forward pipetting, this lead to confusion in the serial dilution step. No check of the attachment of the pipette tips to the multi-channel pipette lead to incorrect dilution during the dilution step. Operator was quite new to the procedure and to working at Curida Diatec's diagnostics lab.
Impact Assessment: In process ELISA is performed to check antibody production during production phase. This incident lead to the ELISA analysis of clone 1A12/1/4 B8760-62 not being valid (E6452). B8760-62 is produced in CL1000. Even though the result was delayed, the production still continues with no change in production parameters. ELISA is performed to check the antibody concentration which tells us how the production is going. There were several 1A12/1/4 batches already produced and we have knowledge of how long production usually lasts. The analysis was repeated after a week and the result was approved (E6458). The result is attached as Attachment 1. Therefore, there is no impact on production timeline.
Root cause description: ELISA is a manual process and requires many steps with high accuracy. The operator accidently used reverse pipetting during serial dilution step that lead to higher volume in one of the columns. The operator failed to check proper attachment of pipette tips. JGR is a relatively new operator for this process, but has successfully performed other ELISA on the same day (E6453, E6454). The results of these other successful ELISA are attached in Attachment 2.
Classification justification: Since this is reoccuring NC, it is categorized as high risk.

Non Conformance Title: Monthly cleaning of lab area according to SOP1099 version 7.0 not performed on time.
Classification justification: The floors have been cleaned three times a week with other detergent, and we do environmental monitoring weekly. No risk for products to be infected. No impact.

Non Conformance Title: Freezer outside of threshold values due to sensor slipping
Classification justification: The sensor was moved to its appropriate position. The temperature showed correct values after it was moved.this indicates that the correct temperature was within the freezing box at the time of this non-conformance and has therefore no impact on product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Particle observed in CL1000 unit
Classification justification: Particles like this have been observed on multiple occasions before and is believed to originate from light shedding of the CL1000 production unit. The medium was clear which indicates that there was no infection present.  The CL1000 therefore remained in production and no other problems appeared as a concequence for at least the following month.

Non Conformance Title: The change actions were not approved for execution together with change control
Classification justification: The deviation does not have an impact on product quality, business, or production timeline. No impact on compliance with quality standards or agreement with customers.

Non Conformance Title: Only 9 bands detected for standard protein ladder in SDS-PAGE
Investigation summary: Possible reasons for detection of 9 bands could be due to error in loading standard protein, sub-optimal standard protein loaded, or background noise. The gel image from SDS709 shows increased background noise towards the bottom of the gel where the 10th band remained undetected. For SDS710, sub-optimal precision plus standard ladder added to the gel can be a reason why only 9/10 bands were detected.
Impact Assessment: The deviation has no impact on the product or its quality. The deviation fails the analysis SDS709 and requires the sample to be reanalysed by SDS-PAGE. The samples were reanalysed by SDS-PAGE (SDS711 and SDS713) using the same standard and the analysis were approved (Ref. Attachment- 5 and Attachment- 6). Ten bands were obtained for standard in addition to expected bands for the samples. The deviation does not have a crucial impact on product quality. Similar deviation can occur when the method is performed differently from what is described in the SOP (on the customer request), so no corrective action is performed for this instance.  SDS-PAGE is a known method in need of improvement in the lab, and CAPA119 has already been opened with the aim of revising the process for performing SDS-Page analyses. For future similar requests from customers, any alteration to the method protocol  will be executed for R&D purposes only (failure will not be investigated). The samples were re-run in SDS-PAGE, and the QC test for SDS-PAGE (reduced) was passed after the re-run. The NC did not impact the product, its quality or the production timeline.
Root cause description: Root cause analysis: -SDS709: Based on the analysis result, the most possible reason for not detecting the 10th band is due to background noise for SDS709. The analysis was performed following the standard procedure. Since several gels were run on the same time, proper handling of the gel could have compromised in this gel. However, the chance of improper pipeting or sub-optimal loading of standard cannot be completely disregarded.  -SDS710: The cause of fewer bands for standard in SDS710 could be due to lower amount of standard loaded. Only 6 µL of standard was loaded to the well during this SDS analysis. The settings and the volume specified in the SOP are optimized accordingly, and most likely requires change in these settings if the volume/amount of samples and standard is changed.
Classification justification: Both runs were not accepted due to system suitability failure. These are not out of specification results.Therefore, this NC is classified as low risk.

Non Conformance Title: Buffer registered with incorrect ratio for SDS Page
Classification justification: SDS708 - method met technical requirements (system suitability) and criteria defined in PS. Therefore, there is no impact on product quality. SDS709 and SDS710 - method did not meet its technical requirements. However, it was provided in NC 277 that failures were not caused by running buffers. Both analyses were rested, and both technical requirements and acceptance criteria in PS were met. Therefore, there is no impact on product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Missing lot number for 1xPBS in Doc1066.1 dated 22.MAR.2024
Classification justification: It is not possible to get the samples shaped for running without dilution in 1XPBS buffer to correct concentration. Samples loaded to this gel SDS708 were in correct concentration. 1XPBS is used but not registered.

Non Conformance Title: Cell count not performed on Harvest 3 and 5 for 1A12/1/4 B8644-61
Classification justification: Cell count performed on H4 and H6 instead of H3 and H5 have no impact on product quality nor production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Incubation period with substrate during ELISA was not according to SOP

Non Conformance Title: Second purification of BRA-4F1 failed, IgM captocore
Investigation summary: A second purification run for IgM antibodies did not work using captocore 700 (SOP0083).
Impact Assessment: Product B8830 was not fully recovered due to failure of second purification process carried out in agreement with customer. Majority of the product was lost in this process.
Root cause description: Due to the nature of CaptoCore 700 og IgM product, the product BRA-4F1 mouse IgM stuck to the column material when it was loaded in an alreaady purified state.
Classification justification: Only a small part of product B8830 was recovered in B8862. Rest of product was destroyed on column.

Non Conformance Title: Missing NC ID numbers (279-292 and 332-335)
Investigation summary: * When the error message was generated, the form did not look like anything was saved. The form was still in the first registration step with no generated NC ID number.  * The missing NC ID numbers is not found in the system garbage bin or in the collection list.  * The supplier of the software informed us that when an error occurs in the first step of a registration, the ID number will be generated without a record.  * The supplier looked into this particular error and concluded that the error occurred due to a missing field in one of the linked documents in the form. This is now corrected.  * Update 12.08.2024: New NC IDs missing. BizzMine support service can not find any source for this error, thus we must expect this to happen.
Impact Assessment: The NC doesn't have an impact on product quality or user safety.  It has an impact on business efficiency and QMS integrity, as it takes up time when errors occur and to investigate, as all missing ID numbers in the QMS should be accounted for and be documented.
Root cause description: Root cause analysis by Fishbone method: Man:  The operator tried again and again because he did not know that each error would generate a new ID number. When he clicked cancel" and tried again from scratch, it worked on first try.   Second time the issue happened, it was done by a different operator.  Machine(Software):  The NC process collection in BizzMine is validated. BizzMine informed of an update that could affect the work flow and cause the error.  The update was implemented in march 2023, and the workflow was validated in august 2023. BizzMine support claimed the error was due to the update, even though this had been used for a long time with no problem. They made a correction of what they thought was the problem (ref. attachment 2).  Even after the first error was corrected, this happened again with no found cause of the problem. Errors may be expected to happen in the registration step, and from the answers from supplier, it is a known problem that ID numbers is generated with no record of them when this happen.  Materials:  NA Method:  SOP describes how to perform the registration. The Operator performed task according to SOP.  Environment: An NC should be registered the same day as it occurs. The fact that the operator wanted to perform the task before he could go home,  affected the decision to try several times and became stressed.  The most likely root cause: 1) Weakness in BizzMine software causing dead ID numbers when errors occurs in the registration step. Errors can happen with no apparent cause. (System/process) 2) Factor leading to so many NCIDs were generated at the same time, was the stressed operator wanting to register the NC before going home for the day. The first step in the registration of a new NC consists of a form requiring a lot of information, links and attachments to be filled in. This makes it time consuming to make the registration, and leaves many rooms for errors to occur. (System/process)"
Classification justification: Missing ID numbers with no trace of existence or documentation related to them will affect the integrity of the QMS. Potential impact on the the validation status of the NC process collection in BizzMine. It is crucial to have control over quality documents and records for securing compliance and to make sure no records or information are lost.  The software used to manage documents and records is validated to be robust with no possibility for users to delete information permanently.  That NC ID numbers are missing with no record of them is thus considered to be a high risk.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected infection in CL1000 flasks
Investigation summary: The results from QC department showed contamination in inner medium of all three production batches B789, B8793 and B8794 (Ref. Attachment-1 NC302). Previous harvests for the batches were tested for infection on 23.04.2024 to find when the infection could have occurred and the harvests affected.  B8794. Infection discovered before H22, but not harvested that day. Three lasts harvest were tested. H19-20 were clean, while last harvest H21 was infected. B8789. Infection discovered before H22, but not harvested that day. Three lasts harvest were tested. H20-21 were clean, while last harvest H22 was infected. B8793. Infection discovered before H24, but not harvested that day. Three lasts harvest were tested. H21 was infected due to being pooled with B8794, H22 was clean, while last harvest H23 was infected.
Impact Assessment: Infection in CL1000 flasks lead to termination of production batch affecting the production timeline and, if undetected, leads to contamination of product. However, the flasks were in the late-production phase and were planned to be in production until H25 as productivity decreases. Infection in the flasks all occurred after H20 so, the impact on the amount that was lost is relatively small. Earlier harvests were also tested for contamination to prevent contamination of final product.  B8792-94 H21, B8789-91 H22, and B8792-93 H23 have been discarded. B8789-94 H1-20, B8789-91 H21, B8792-93 H22, B8790-91 H23-25, and B8792 H24-25 can be used to make final product.
Root cause description: Contamination from operator to and around the harvest port using unsterile materials and/or equipment is likely the root cause. However, NC267 will perform a more detail analysis encompassing root cause for infection for the several 1A12/1/4 infections that have occured in CL1000 flasks lately.
Classification justification: The flasks were removed all later than harvest 20 and the production was intended to be ended at harvest 25. The amount of crude lost is acceptable. Harvest tested positive for infection have been discarded. Earlier harvests for infected flasks are tested negatvie for infection and there will be no impact on any future final product.

Non Conformance Title: Very weak signal observed during ELISA
Investigation summary: This investigation found the following deviations during ELISA on 18.04.2024: 1) Incubation over the time period specified in SOP (E6471 and E6472)  2) Incorrect coating of ELISA plates (E6471 and E6473) Law 268:29 is known in our lab to give slow OD signal and all other criterias were met by the results obtained. This is a known based on previous experiences that the incubation period for some clones can be longer than specified in the SOP though the tests are valid.   The signal from E6471 and E6473 (HAM6) were to low to calculate antibody production due to the fact that the reagents used for the test were incorrect with relation to the species and isotype of the sample.   Both of the samples were successfully tested again (Ref. attachment: E6474 and E6475).
Impact Assessment: Due to the non-conformance, ELISA had to be performed again for the samples. This requires use of extra resources.    In-process ELISA is performed to check if the production is going as expected. Checking antibody concentration from different harvests during production serves as a check-point in the production process. The results will therefore not lead to alteration of production parameters or product quality.  The test were performed again successfully. Therefore this incident had no impact on the production timeline.
Root cause description: The SOP does not specify specific requirement of how much the signal should be and only mentions the time frame. Since the production team work with a wide variety of clones and the reagents used is general for antibody species and isotype, it is difficult to determine how an individual clone will react with the reagents. The time frame mentioned in the SOP applies for several clones, provided correct isotype and species is used, but some exceptions cannot be excluded.  The operator performing the coating step checked the species and isotype, but mistakenly checked the product name" instead for the "isotype and species". It is not common that the product name will be confused with isotype and species. These information in Product specification are always present in the same place/section and the operators use PS quite often for different production related information. This is therefore considered a one-time occurrence. The operator performing the test (E6471) did not check the PS and relied on the operator performing the coating step. On the next run (E6473), coating was performed by the same operator as for E6471 and the same operator performed the analysis. But the operator did not recheck the PS for information.  So, the root cause of this NC can be summarized as stringent time frame in SOP and mistake during use of PS, resulting in wrong coating used."
Classification justification: The process is an in-process control to check antibody production in CL1000 and does not have impact on product or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Weekly cleaning of cold room DIA283 not preformed
Classification justification: No impact on products. Low activity in cold room.

Non Conformance Title: Additonal band on SDS analysis
Investigation summary: SDS718 was rerun as SDS720, but yielded similar results. The gels are accepted to be correctly run, B8867 (Law 268:29) does not meet PS requirements, B8866 (280) passes.
Impact Assessment: Product B8867 does not meet level of purity stated by CoA and PS. Customer must be notified.  Product B8866 from 280 has passed technical and product specification (PS) when run in both SDS718 and SDS720. There is no impact on this product.
Root cause description: Sample could contain contaminants. Law 268:29 have occasionally been observed to produce additional bands before (attachment (3)) ref SDS610.
Classification justification: The SDS run for Law268:29 product B8867 does not meet product specification.

Non Conformance Title: Technical requirements not fulfilled in isotyping of clones A8B5 and LAW 268:29
Investigation summary: Additional bands could be seen on multiple analysis and/or wrong isotype determined. All reagent used were confirmed to be within expiry, and samples had been stored according to procedure. Incubation time exceeded manufactor's protocol but were within internal SOPs.  Re-tests of Law 268:69 and A8B5 yielded same results. DEV166 was initatied to test the integrity of the isotyping method employed.  The Pierce kit was determined to be less fit or use and its method will be replaced. Supplier complaint will be initated.
Impact Assessment: Law 268:69 batch B8867 did not result in earlier confirmed isotype (analysis performed on customers request). Customer has been contacted regarding B8867 and after performing internal testing have accepted the product. Purity analysis was slightly lower, and therefore functionality, but customer confirmed that its still within their QC specification. As isotype is now confirmed to be IgG3, and the kit at fault for wrong results, the lower purity and wrong istoype are not considered related issues and these isotyping results have therefore no impact on the quality of the product. Cell banks for Law268:29, and A8B5, FREEZE078 and FREEZE074, failed QC testing of the cellbanks as the requirement is to have only one band to be considered monoclonal. Law268:29 is confirmed to work as expected by customer and after more testing during Dev166, it is confirmed that it has only one isotype, IgG3, and the TFS isotyping kit is not working for this clone. This has therefore no impact on the quality of the cellbank.  A8B5 is a Diatec owned clone, and these results are accepted on a risk based evaluation, where the risk is consdiered very low that the functionality of the product is impacted, and that these results are most likely an artifact of the testing itself. After DEV166 it is further concluded that the additional bands are an effect of the Pierce testing kit itself and not a reflection on the quality of the cellbank. Cell bank for ND-5G4, FREEE076, failed the recloning QC analysis and was therefore not retested. A new cell bank has since been created, FREEZE084 which were confirmed to be IgG2a. The IgG1 specification in the PS was discovered to be incorrect when going through old documentation and testing during Dev166. The cell bank passes criteria as the clone should be producing IgG2a.
Root cause description: The recent rise in multiple band occurrences across several cell banks/products suggests that the current isotyping method may not be robust enough for the samples tested. Testing from DEV166 support this notion and a new method for determining isotype will be implemented and validated based on he results from Dev166
Classification justification: This non-conformance is directly correlated to the product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Findings during internal audit: Missing monthly maintenance of FPLC and expired solutions stored in the lab
Classification justification: 20% ethanol is an additional rinse of the instrument perfomed monthly, but instrument is rinsed before and after each use with RO-water. this has no impact on product quality or production,

As 1A12 is produced on forecast and this leakage was at H23, it had no impact on timelines. SPV-T3b is produced in 3 flasks on the forecast, this has therefore no impact on the delivery timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage between cell compartment and outer medium of CL1000
Classification justification: CL1000 leakage between cell side and outer medium has been seen to occur at regular intervals. The leakage of same-cell units within the bioreactor does not impact the quality of the product (albeit may reduce the concentration harvested from the affected units).   All crude was harvest from the CL1000 units to be used for product purification. The production line for IIE2 (unit B8927) was the only production unit for project 2024-40 thus requiring the startup of a new production unit (B8953). This did not hamper the production timeline as backup cells were kept alive from the inoculation of B8927, reducing the necessary time to proliferate sufficient cells for production.  No corrections were done for 1A12/1/4 as there are 18 CL1000 continually being produced year round on forecast, and therefore the loss of one unit does not significantly impact the delivery.

Non Conformance Title: Autoclave DIA086 broke down during an autoclaving cycle
Classification justification: Classified as low risk as this malfunction does not impact product quality, HSE or customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: Precipitation observed in final product
Investigation summary: Product B8867 was ready for shipping when precipitation was observed by QA through final checking of product vials. It was reprocessed into B9005 and vialled, followed by new QC tests.  Product B8901 was also purified from the same production units which made B8867 (from later harvests), but was observed to have a cloudy precipitate after dialysis. The product was reprocessed into B8905, but was not vialled as turbidity was still present. A sample (100mL) was reprocessed into B9006, still having a cloudy solution.
Impact Assessment: The presence of precipitation in an antibody solution can theoretically affect the quality of the finished product. The product specification does not include a step for visually checking the product. The customer has informed that it is not uncommon to see turbidity in the vials they receive with product.  B8905 and B8906 are currently placed under quarentine from having turbid solutions.  Depending on the customers response to the product, some of the batch(es) may be scrapped.  A sample from B8867 was sent to the customer and passed their internal QC tests. Product B9005, reprocessed from B8867, passed new QC tests (SDS738 and SDS739) and is thereby accepted by the customer (see email chain in attachment 1). Since the product passed the customers internal testing for their intended use, the visual precipitation and turbidity might have minimal impact to the actual quality and effectiveness of the product. It could simply be an issue of solubility in the buffer which is inconsequential to the customer's use.
Root cause description: The exact cause of precipitation is not clear. The product from Law 268:29 has been known to produce precipitate over time in the past, to the point where the customer is expecting to see it at delivery.  The precipitation could be caused by some part of the production process, either upstream or downstream, or the finalizing buffers used for vialling. It is also possible that the product aggregates over time, or the storage temperature is not optimal.
Classification justification: The presence of precipitation can have a direct impact on product quality as product might be precipitating or final formulation might be wrong.  The product specification for this product does not require any visual check of the product.

Non Conformance Title: Duplicates not comparable for standard during in process ELISA
Investigation summary: E6500 had standard OD values not considered to be comparable, even though it is not specified in the SOP what amounts to being incomparable.
Impact Assessment: This is an in process analysis and does not have any impact to the product quality nor the production timeline. Analysis was repeated and results were considered satisfactory.
Root cause description: It is difficult to establish an exact root cause, one possibility is the pipette drawing air during the dilution step, or having a slightly loose tip, causing the difference between duplicates. What is more method description is not detailed enough to distinguish significant imprecision in dilution replicates. The procedure does not provide any value limited for different dilution levels, which causes operators are not consistent in rejecting ELISA runs The method requires several manual dilutions and it is sensitive to laboratory/human error.
Classification justification: The information from the in process control was not immediately needed and the analysis was rerun later to have information about the early stages of the production. This does not impact product quality, nor production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: pH alarm in ASX production B8863
Investigation summary: No pinched tubing was discovered, but since the system stabilized immediately once the medium pump rate was increased and the operator observed the air in the tubing disappearing, this indicates that there must have been a partially pinched tube somewhere.
Impact Assessment: All parameters such as pH, cell count, viability and concentration were carefully followed for several weeks afterwards, and system appeard to quickly stabilize again.  1. pH and gas:after the incident was resolved, pH stabilised slightly higher than before (see graph below) which indicates some cell death. However, pH did not rise to exreme levels (pH 7.4 and above) and the fact that within 24 hours it was back to similar levels as before the incident, indicates that most of the cells were able to survive and continue to prolifirate. Gas number (mixture of CO2 and oxygen) continued being stable at 1 the entire time and was not impacted by the incident.. 2. cell count and viability: cell count and viability reflect higher values right after the incident. that is according to expectancy when medium flow has been obstructed, where some of the cells have loosened from the bioreactors and travelled to the EC chamber where the sample line is connected. cell count and viability stabilised to pre-incident values the very next day indicating that cells impacted were removed from the bioreactor cartridges. 3. glucose: Glucose levels increased only on the day of incident and the fact that glucose level became much lower the next day indicates that the cells were still consuming glucose, something that supports the reasining that there was no major cell death or significant stress over an extended period of time. 4. concentration: on 26.06.2024, an ELISA analysis was performed by HPJ for EC samples taken from 03.06.2024- 24.06.2024.The results shown in the table below indicate that the concentration remained reasonably stable even after this incident.The slightly lower result 17.06.2024 is due to a cell removal performed 13.06.2024, which is a standard procedure for UCUS 99.05 production in Maximizer/Xcellerator. Date for EC sample   Concentration   03.06.2024   2,08 mg/mL   06.06.2024   2,3 / 2,07 mg/mL   10.06.2024   2,19 mg/mL   17.06.2024   0,96 mg/mL   24.06.2024   1,73 mg/mL   More cell debris was observed in harvest bag 2 than in harvest bag 1, as well as larger amount of cells within the EC chamber. This is quite normal for UCUS as the UCUS cell line is known to grow quickly until they clog the flowpath and therefore end the run. This incident might have caused the celldebris buildup to increase more rapidly, but as cell removal is performed when needed, this is not considered to have an impact to the run. In conclusion the results of the tests conducted after this incident indicate that the restricted medium was limited to a short time and has minimal impact on production or product quality. The measurements taken on the samples collected while bag 2 and bag 3 were connected indicates that the production recovered within the next 24 hours and proceeded normally. Therefore this non-conformance does not impact any of the harvests from production run B8863 and harvest 2 and 3 can also be used for further processing for final product.
Root cause description: There is no clear root cause, but the most likely explanation is that an unknown obstruction or an air pocket moved from the tank and within the medium tube restricting medium flow.
Classification justification: This NC is classified as a high risk due to the potential consequences to the production if the bioreactor received insufficient medium supply over an extended period of time

Non Conformance Title: Performed monthly cleaning procedure using peroxide instead of acid based detergent.
Classification justification: Peroxide is a strong antiseptic and one of the main components of the acid based cleaning detergent used at Curida Diatec (Spor Klenz). The hydrogen peroxide used is not as effective against fungal spores as the Spor Klenz detergent regularly used for monthly maintenance, but combined with the general aseptic and sterilization techniques employed it is considered to be effective enough as a replacement.

Classification justification: No potential impact on product quality, customer satisfaction, product quality/safety effect, validated state of equipment/process/system, negative impact on employee safety, or incompliance issue with customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: Internal audit 6: Missing instruction for how/when to create supplier agreements
Classification justification: No potential impact on product quality, customer satisfaction, product quality/safety effect, validated state of equipment/process/system, negative impact on employee safety, or incompliance issue with customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: Product removed from DIA409 and left in room temperature
Investigation summary: The product was discovered at room temperature on 24.07.2024. This freezer also stores other materials like FBS, Pen Strep, and antigens for QC tests. The product was leftover from validation runs in 2023 and was not intended for use or sale. Oversight during handling, led to the product being misplaced. To prevent recurrence, the freezer has been locked, limiting access to the Facility Manager and QA.
Impact Assessment: The product quality could be directly affected by this non-conformance, however this particular product was not intended to be used for further processing or sale. No quality testing has been performed on the misplaced product after it was found. Product was discarded.
Root cause description: Two root causes can be identified:  - Human error: Accidental misplacing during handling of material in the freezer. - unrestricted access to -70 freezer. As access was restricted as an immediate action, this is considered sufficient mitigation and requires no further actions.
Classification justification: This non-conformance in considered high risk due to two main reasons: 1) The product has been stored in undesirable conditions for a unknown period of time, which can directly impact the product quality. 2) The storage of product in room temperature is in direct noncompliance with product specification.

Classification justification: Classified low as this is just a validation process and has not infected the product.

Non Conformance Title: CL1000 leakage between cell compartment and outer medium compartment
Classification justification: CL1000 leakage between cell side and outer medium has been seen to occur at regular intervals. The leakage of same-cell units within the bioreactor does not impact the quality of the product (albeit may reduce the concentration harvested from the affected units).   All crude was harvested from the CL1000 units to be used for product purification. Loss of one flask from M21C12 does not impact the production timeline.  No corrections were done for 1A12/1/4 as there are 18 CL1000 continually being produced year round on forecast, and therefore the loss of one unit does not significantly impact the delivery.  Loss of one flask from EO-1 project 2024-52 does also not impact any production timeline as this project was primarily started for testing CL1000 membranes in DEV164.

Non Conformance Title: Product eluted with regeneration buffer during FPLC
Investigation summary: - Operator SBA is trained in purification process, but was unaware of the procedure for test production. Usually operator ØHR performs such project.  - The operator followed pt. 9.1.2 in SOP and decided to collect peaks in different containers instead of reading further in the SOP. The program continues automatically and by the time it was known about pt. 9.2.9, the product had already began to elute with regeneration buffer.  - Neutralizing with 1M Tris was not possible. Therefore, elute was added with 1x PBS as a dilution process which also helps to neutralize pH to some extent.
Impact Assessment: Exposure to acidic condition/low pH increases the possibility for protein unfolding, conformational changes and irreversible loss of native antibody structure. This conformational disturbance can lead to loss of potency/complementarity with antigen and formation of antibody aggregates. With the test available in the production facility, it is neither possible to confirm the exact nature and intensity of impact, nor it can be confirmed that the antibody is stable and functional. But, based on the precipitation and loss of product observed before and after dialysis, as well as relevant literature and theoretical knowledge, it is considered high likelyhood that exposure to such acidic pH negatively impacted the product quality. Therefore the product will be discarded. The non-conformance has a negative impact on production timeline og cost. Production of a test production takes ca 7 weeks, and costs approximately 50.000 NOK in material and resources. No impact on customer relationships as this was an internal project meant for marketing purposes. Business development department is informed and will notify production if the product is to be replaced.
Root cause description: SOP is not very clear or optimal for information flow regarding handling of test productions, as sections are divided throughout the SOP. Therefore, insufficient and confusing procedure is considered the primary root cause. Operator SBA has performed purification multiple times in the last couple of years, and has completed training. But the operator is not well-experienced with test productions. There is no specific training for test productions, only general training in protein A purification.  Therefore, insufficient training in test production purification is considered a contributing root cause.
Classification justification: Low elution pH (of regeneration buffer) can have negative impact on product quality.

Non Conformance Title: CL1000 units harvest period exceeding 5 days
Investigation summary: Eight CL1000 units B8985-90 + B8991-92 from cell line M21C12 were not harvested within protocol frequency causing a drastic decrease in cell count and viability. The cells were still able to proliferate back to normal cell mass, albeit with a reduction in production output. A QC test of purity on the finished product revealed an impurity which potentially could stem from the period of stressful conditions on the cells. There is not a great supportive system to ensure that all units are harvested on time.
Impact Assessment: The reduced number of cells for some harvest caused a lessened output of antibodies compared to other bioreactors of the same cell line. The production was still above required amount of product for project 2024-46 therefore there is not considered to have been any impact to the production timeline.  The cells recovered rapidly indicating that there were still some living cells, that were able to produce. The improvement in viability shows that the cells, while initially stressed, were not exposed to conditions so severe that they reached a critical degradation point. This indicates that they were still metabolically active and capable of recovery, which limits the risk of significant protein misfolding or degradation. However, as an impurity was visible on the SDS, it cannot be ruled out that this didn't come from the stressing conditions the cells were subjected to and therefore has an impact on product quality.
Root cause description: Root case is identified to be a combination of:  -  lack of communication between operators to inform which series have been harvested or not. -  lack of sufficient training in communication between operators for which units have been harvested.  -  lack of an efficient system to double check and ensure series of production have been harvested on time.  -  lack of awareness as operator failed to notice that a series of M21C12 had not been documented harvested. -  larger than usual workload contributing to create an environment where actions that are not effectively checked can be missed.
Classification justification: The extended time between harvests potentially caused a dip in production output from harvest 3-5. While this does impact the quantity of antibodies produced, the overall production timeline is unaffected. The total output was still well past required amounts for project 2024-46. The cells recovered rapidly however lower purity was observed for the final product, therefore impact on product quality is a possibility.

Non Conformance Title: Band detected on SDS-PAGE analysis of IgM test samples
Investigation summary: Non-reducing SDS-PAGE analysis of IgM batch B8977 (SDS733) showed detectable bands in duplicate test lanes (50–75 kDa) and in the control (~37 kDa), resulting in failure against SOP1066 and Product Specification requirements (“No visual bands”). Repeat analysis (SDS735) confirmed failure of B8977, with duplicate lanes showing bands at 50–75 kDa (faint) and ~25 kDa, while the control passed.   Duplicate test lanes loaded from the same preparation showed differing molecular weight bands, and the control band observed in the first run was not reproduced in the repeat, indicating lack of reproducibility. The method includes denaturing conditions (SDS and 95°C heating) and is not validated for IgM structural assessment.   Although the banding pattern suggests analytical variability rather than a consistent intrinsic degradation pattern, the batch does not meet the defined release specification and is therefore non-compliant under SOP1066. In the absence of verifiable orthogonal testing related to product function (e.g. ELISA) or degradant verification (Reduced SDS), it is not possible release the batch for use.
Impact Assessment: Impact on Product Quality: Under the defined release method (SOP1066), batch B8977 does not meet the Product Specification requirement of “No impurities visible on gel.” Although scientific assessment suggests the possibility that observed bands may reflect structural behaviour under denaturing conditions, the method remains the approved release test.  No additional orthogonal or functional confirmatory testing was conducted.  Based on specification criteria alone, the batch is considered non-compliant. Impact on previously released batches: Same SOP and preparation conditions historically applied.      No documented increase in complaint trends.      No reported functional performance issues.     The present non-conformance reflects failure under the defined release method rather than confirmed evidence of historical product degradation.    No retrospective action on previously released batches is required at this time.
Root cause description: Primary Root Cause: Use of a non-validated non-reducing SDS-PAGE method for IgM release testing, resulting in variable band detection under denaturing preparation conditions. Contributing factors identified:     Method not validated for IgM conformational integrity, robustness, or reproducibility.      Denaturing preparation conditions that may induce structural dissociation of IgM.      Observed inconsistent migration behaviour between duplicate lanes and between runs.      Prior similar deviations indicating recurring analytical challenges with IgM SDS-PAGE.      Acceptance criterion does not account for method variability or structural sensitivity of IgM.      While the banding pattern may reflect stress-induced structural behaviour under denaturing conditions, no confirmatory testing was performed outside the defined specification method. As the method remains the approved release test, the batch cannot be justified for release.
Classification justification: This NC is classified as high risk as it reports a final product's significant incompliance issue with Product Specification and SOP1066.

Non Conformance Title: Lower antibody concentration than expected during 2D2 production.
Investigation summary: Reduced productivity of 2d2 was observed. medium/serum, infection and leakage were investigated as potential sources of the issue, but were all negative. No root cause could be determined.
Impact Assessment: If the cells stopped producing antibodies due to increased stress then the quality of antibodies produced might also be compromised. This will affect all harvests from H8. Therefore, all harvests from H8 and until termination are discarded. The stark reduction in productivity poses a direct threat to hamper the production timeline for this project. As this impacted all 30 CL1000 and they were run until H18-H22 with extra concentration analysis for investigation, the economical impact of these iis estimated to be over 600.000 NOK. A maximizer has been initiated to ensure delivery on-time, so no impact on deliery timeline or customer relationship.
Root cause description: As far as we are aware through the documentation and investigation, everything has been done and prepared according to our procedures and all materials have been used within the time limits of our procedures.  No definitive cause could be established after this investigation.
Classification justification: The deviation is classified as high risk due to the economic impact it will have if we do not produce enough product on time, as we have an agreed delivery with a customer.

Non Conformance Title: Infection in CL1000 flask
Investigation summary: Bacterial infection was confirmed in all three production units. Since all of them share the same production series it is believed that there might have been a spread during handling of the infected units.
Impact Assessment: All harvests from the 2D2 series from harvest 8 was discarded at a later time due to issues with production output ref NC331. None of the harvests from the infected production units were used for any product, therefore there is no impact to the quality of any product.
Root cause description: Momentary slip in aseptic technique or potential leak in one or more of the infected units.
Classification justification: All production units from project 2024-36 had significantly dropped in productivity (investigated in NC331) prior to the discovery of infection in units B8931, B8933, and B8935. Because of this, the remaining CL1000 units were also discarded shortly after (H18-H20). The loss of the infected units does not impact any product nor production timeline as the production output for the units at late harvests was remarkably low. This NC meets the criteria in the SOP5034 for recurrences to assign a high risk classification to it. However given that handling production CL1000 flasks is a daily activity that has a high frequency, and the number of recurrences of infections as a proportion of the total number of CL1000 flasks processed is low, it is being assigned a low risk classification. The material from the batches impacted in this NC were discarded from HArvest 8 onwards. This further lowers any risk to quality of product as the infections were observed in harvest 15, much later on in the production run.

Non Conformance Title: Test production not produced in compliance with the product specification
Classification justification: This NC is considered to be low risk as no impact has been identified to product quality and production time line. No other impacted areas identified.

Non Conformance Title: Elisa failed due to blank plates after Substrate step
Investigation summary: Human error during on or more steps
Impact Assessment: Due to the non-conformance, ELISA had to be performed again for the samples. This requires use of extra resources.    In-process ELISA is performed to check if the production is going as expected. Checking antibody concentration from different harvests during production serves as a check-point in the production process. The results will therefore not lead to alteration of production parameters or product quality.  The test were performed again successfully. Therefore this incident had no impact on the production timeline.
Root cause description: ELISA is a manual process and requires many steps with high accuracy. Mix up of Coating is the most likely reason for blank plates. There is no specific system in place to ensure that the samples and solution used in ELISA process are kept separated. This is not considered necessary, as this has not been an issue in the past.
Classification justification: The process is an in-process control to check antibody production in CL1000 and does not have impact on product or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: CO2 level over threshold in incubator DIA156
Investigation summary: Based on the investigation, it seems that overloading of incubator could have led to failure of incubator to maintain CO2 within limit. Since the incubator has the capacity to contain 24 CL1000 flasks, failure of the incubator to maintain %CO2 within the setpoint needs to be addressed.
Impact Assessment: Due to the NC, the cells in CL1000 were exposed to higher %CO2 than setpoint on several occassions. The maximum %CO2 measured during the period discussed in this non-conformance was 6.7% for 9 hours. The maximum period with %CO2 above upper threshold was 13 hours 25 minutes before any action was taken to maintain the CO2 level. CO2 impacts pH of the cell culture and higher %CO2 can be toxic to cell culture. The possible impact of this non-conformance is assessed below:    - CO2 toxicity: Though it is clone-dependent, 6.7% CO2 is generally not considered toxic for cell culture. So the chances of toxicity is low.  - Duration of exposure: The sirius trend shows that the CO2 level did not rise abruptly, so the duration with %CO2 far from setpoint was not for a long period of time. Thus the impact should be low.  - pH of cell culture: Change in pH during cell culture is inevitable. So, cell culture medium are prepared with buffer system which can prevent pH fluctuation in cell culture. Thus, the gradual CO2 increase in this incident should not have impacted the pH drastically in the culture.  - Physical apperance: The shift in pH of medium in CL1000 flask can be observed by color change of RPMI medium (except Cal 1-4H1/2/2 which uses ADCF medium). Since, the operators has not noticed any unusual color change in RPMI1640 medium, it can be confirmed that the pH was not drastically affected by this incident.  - Antibody production: The impact of the non-conformance on production can be checked by ELISA. ELISA result for HE1/4b/3 shows similar concentration of antibody (i.e. ca. 0.4 mg/mL) in harvest before (H10) and after (H11) the incident. The concentration of antibody in clone M21C12 kept in DIA156 was also comparable to the CL1000 series from same clone kept in a different incubator. The concentration of antibody in H5 (before non-conformance) and H10 (after non-conformance) has increased, and lies in range as expected from our previous experience. These results shows that the non-conformance did not impact antibody production. The antibody concentration of 2D2 is not relevant for comparision as the clone was already having trouble with antibody production so that the concentration in the harvest was falling with each harvest before this incident occured.    Thus, it can be implied that the incident is not considered to impact production or the product quality.
Root cause description: Fully loading of incubator may interfere with air circulation within the incubator which could have led to failure of incubator to regulate the CO2 level. However, it can not be concluded as a root cause and requires further testing before it can be confirmed.  CAPA239 is started to perform further test for confirmation/determination of root cause.
Classification justification: Considering gradual rise in %CO2 to maximum of 6.7% as well as the buffer system of cell culture medium, the NC is not believed to impact product quality is therefore regarded as low risk.

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect buffer used for dialysis
Investigation summary: The two variant of PBS buffer appears similar to each other, with some minor adjustments. PBS prepared according to SOP2019 is frequently used buffer for dialysis and is standard buffer at production facility of Curida Diatec. Due to assumptions on standard buffer composition correlating with description in product specification, the operator did not realize that the buffer mentioned in the PS was custom buffer (PBS azide without potassium) and used standard buffer (PBS without azide) instead.
Impact Assessment: PBS is commonly used as an isotonic buffer solution typically consisting of sodium, potassium and phosphate salt. These salts together maintains a stable pH, ionic strength and osmotic balance to create optimal physiological environment for biological systems. PBS can be prepared as different variant by altering the differen components depending upon need. PBS is used as buffer for antibody solution due to its ability to maintain stable pH and physiological ionic strength.  Some PBS buffers for antibodies make changes in the concentration of salt used in PBS to prevent potential interference with antibody stability, enzyme activity, or other assay components that might be sensitive to salt level. Potassium ions can sometimes cause non-specific binding or disrupt specific experimental conditions when the product is in use. Removing potassium helps maintain antibody specificity and ensures better compatibility with sensitive assays or cell-based systems. Additionally, potassium-free buffers can offer better stability for long-term antibody storage. Overall, adjustment of salt concentration in PBS helps optimize performance in diverse applications. The impact of the non-conformance on the product is assessed below:  - SDS-PAGE: Aggregation and purity of final product was tested by SDS-PAGE on 19.07.2024, and the results show bands as expected for IgG molecule (Ref.: QC_SDS-PAGE.pdf).  - Physical appearance of the product: Incorrect buffer can impact stability of antibody solution leading to precipitation/aggregation. Physical appearance of the product was checked on 15.08.2024 and no sign for precipitation/aggregation was observed (Ref.: Final product.jpg). Together with the results from SDS-PAGE and physical appearance test (standard QC tests for product in Curida Diatec), it is concluded that the stability of the product is not impoacted.  - SOP2019 and PBS mentioned in PS are two different variant of PBS buffers which appears similar. The PBS used for dialysis has the same pH as that of PBS mentioned in PS, so pH effect to antibody can be omitted. There is a small difference in salt concentration between these buffers and the final pH of both of the buffers are same. As mentioned above, salts present in the buffer can have impact on the stability, enzyme activity and other assay components when product is in use, but does not impact the antibody itself. Salts and their concentration in buffer is specified by customer according to their need.  Based on little difference between the two buffers and results from internal QC tests, the impact of the buffer on the product is considered low. However, potassium can affect further processes/enzyme activity further downstream in the process at customer's end. therefore, product was pooled and re-dialysed into correct final buffer to remove potassium from the final product (final batch B9022). As the first round of dialysis is always in standard PBS, product was dialysed for two rounds with correct potassium free PBS buffer as specified in the PS. The two rounds involved dialysis of product with 100 times volume of PBS buffer (SOP2034) compared to the volume of eluate and minimum of 8 hours between each round. This exchanged the buffer from the product with correct buffer and removed unnecessary salts (excess phosphate and potassium in this case) present in the product.    As no impact on the product is observed through QC test and the change in buffer can be performed according to SOP, the impact of this non-conformance is deemed Low". "
Root cause description: The root cause for the incident in this case is identified as human error, however it is also noted there are contributory factors from gaps in the documentation related to the process. There are already existing ongoing CAPAs (5,76,86,206) related to improving the product specification documentation and process which intend to close these gaps that can contribute to errors
Classification justification: The NC is classified as high risk as the buffer used does not comply with Product Specification.

Non Conformance Title: Customer audit: Temperature Probe Discrepancy and Missing Calibration Certificate
Investigation summary: Case 1:    Curida Diatec failed to demonstrate during the audit the thermometer calibration certification used for logger qualification. The loggers belonged to Catapult Life Science Company which was acquired by Diatec. Catapult Life science performed equipment qualification. The loggers were implemented in Curida QMS, however, their qualification documents were not well incorporated at this time and were kept in the storage box. Due to the lack of the system to provide a clear status overview of the qualification/calibration status of the equipment and its corresponding documents, no one noticed a gap in the equipment documentation.     Case 2:    The inspector interpreted that sensors used in tank qualification were qualified/calibrated using reference method/standard, therefore documentation of these activities should be demonstrated. During the investigation, it was found that the sensor was verified internally by dipping into liquid nitrogen. This was executed and documented in that same report which was inspected. However, person who executed and authorized the study was not present at the company to explain the used qualification approach. What is more, the used qualification approach was not very clearly described in the study protocol/report, leading to wrong conclusion.
Impact Assessment: Case 1:    The certification for used reference thermometer was found. Therefore, the loggers used during qualification activities are considered reliable. Therefore, this deviation has no impact on validation / qualification status of DIA283 instrument.There is no impact on stored-in cold room product quality or status of instrument qualification.    Case 2:    It was provided that the logger used in tank qualification was reliable. Therefore qualification status of tanks remains valid. The deviation does not impact cell bank stored in tank and does not have any impact on product quality.
Root cause description: Case 1    Curida Diatec failed to demonstrate, during the audit, the thermometer calibration certification used for logger qualification. The loggers belonged to Catapult Life Science which was acquired by Curida Diatec. The loggers were implemented in Curida QMS, however, their certification documents were not well incorporated at this time and were kept in the storage box. Due to the lack of the system to provide a clear status overview of the qualification/calibration status of the equipment and its corresponding documents, Curida Diatec failed to present the requested certificate to auditors. The qualification was done correctly by the previous equipment owner but it was not well incorporated during implementation into Curida Diatec QMS after the Catapult Life Science acquisitionwas requiredacquired by Curida Diatec. (Category 5 System/process)    Case 2    The protocol/report did not provide clear description of how used sensor should be qualified. Due to this gap, the report was wrongly interpreted. (Category 3 documentation)
