Classification justification: No compliance with ISO9001:2015, section 7.1.5.2 When traceability is a requirement, the organization must ensure that measuring equipment is calibrated or verified at specified intervals, or prior to use, against standards traceable to international or national measurement standards. The organization must retain evidence of the fitness of the measuring equipment for its intended purpose. Records of calibration and verification activities must be maintained""

Non Conformance Title: Customer audit: Expired Materials and Quarantine Storage in Approved Areas
Investigation summary: Case a)  The logistics department failed to consistently perform and document routine checks for expired materials due to vague procedural guidelines and the absence of formal tracking tools. This issue arose after organizational changes, when the task of checking expiration dates was not properly assigned to newly trained employees, despite their completion of required training. Case b) Quarantined materials were found stored in clearly marked and segregated areas within the Ready for Use" warehouse and cold room, despite the rooms being labeled for approved materials only. Although no incidents of unauthorized material use have been reported since the storage facility's design in 2020, the current setup is misleading and can lead to using authorised material in production activities."
Impact Assessment: case a) None of the expired materials found in the warehouse were used in production activities. All materials used are always double-checked by the operator, and the expiry dates of most products, raw materials, and consumables are stated and controlled in the production worksheets. case b) no impact on product quality, there was no single event of using unauthorised or under quarantine material" in production activities."
Root cause description: Case a) Unclear instruction in SOP5010 regarding time regime and documentation of activities related to warehouse check (category 3 and 5) Case b) The original design of the storage facility, implemented in 2020 under change control CMR 200, may not have fully accounted for the need to clearly differentiate between approved and quarantine materials within the same space. (Category: 6)
Classification justification: No complaince with standard ISO9001:2015 §8.5.1/8.5.2

Non Conformance Title: Customer audit: Missing Approval Signatures on Validation Report
Investigation summary: Case 1: QMS SW BizzMine Validation documents were checked (VP2021-224 of 25/01/2022).  The report was prepared and signed by Anna Huk and Mira Børstad on January 25, 2022. The document was then scanned and sent to Turid Høysæter, who was working from home at the time, for final approval. Both the first page, containing the original signatures of the author and verifier (Attachment 1, Page 1), and the page with all three signatures (Attachment 1, Page 2) were retained. However, since the first page was also the cover page of the document, it was misinterpreted as indicating that the report had not been signed.  Case 2: Qualification of ColdRoom DIA283 (PQ Report 2020_198 of 17/12/2020). In the investigation of Case 2, it was found that during the 2020 relocation of Curida Diatec’s Diagnostic Production, over 20 qualification activities were conducted. Kent Ghost, the QA responsible at the time, lacked documented training in the authorization of such documents, though this was attributed to unclear training procedures at that time. The investigation identified a missing signature on one report, likely due to an attention lapse caused by resource constraints amid high qualification activity, but the exact root cause remains unconfirmed due to the time elapsed and Ghost's departure.
Impact Assessment: Case 1: QMS SW BizzMine Validation documents were checked (VP2021-224 of 25/01/2022).  The report in question was correctly approved by QA in JAN.2022. No additional step is needed. The qualification of software remains valid. Case 2: Qualification of ColdRoom DIA283 (PQ Report 2020_198 of 17/12/2020). QA revived the report in question, and no concern or unauthorised departure from protocol was inspected. All data entries were correctly signed. All used equipment was traceable. The report was approved on 22.08.2024
Root cause description: The main root cause of this non-conformance can not be identified due to the fact that none of the personnel involved in the qualification activities is employed at the organisation any more and the fact that both qualification studies were conducted a few years ago.  the most probable root cause is identified to be: -	Insufficient QA resources were allocated to oversee qualification and validation activities during the production site relocation in 2020 (category: other) -	Lack of robust tracker which would provide a clear status of qualification/validation activities (category: system/process)
Classification justification: No compliance with ISO9001:2015, section §7.5.2 Documented information""

Non Conformance Title: QC HRP ELISA OOS
Investigation summary: QC analysis did not meet criteria from PS. The product was suspected to have failed based on how it behaved during conjugation prior to QC.
Impact Assessment: Product does not appear to have conjugated properly, therfore its specifcation of the product was not confirmed. The quality issue was reported directly to customer. Despite confirmed OOQ result, customer still want to test the HRP product created for their uses and have accepted that the QC ELISA test failed (attachment (1)).
Root cause description: There was an issue during the conjugation method. It was observed that the antibody did not bind properly to the conjugate, but it was not possible to determine whether this was due to a problem with the conjugate itself or the antibodies (e.g., age or precipitation).
Classification justification: An analysis out of spec directly represent the quality of the product.

Non Conformance Title: Environmental monitoring plates incubation period exceeding SOP
Classification justification: The 20-25℃ incubation is done to see the formation of sporulating colonies by fungi. A 2 day increase in incubation at 20-25℃ will not affect the formation of colonies by the following 30-35℃ incubation step . There is no effect on product quality, and the production timeline remains unaffected.

Non Conformance Title: Additional band during SDS-PAGE analysis
Investigation summary: Two production batches (B9026 and B9210) failed to meet the required purity specification due to the presence of free kappa light chains, confirmed by SDS-PAGE and Western blot. Initial hypotheses related to analytical error and upstream process deviations were ruled out. The most plausible root cause was identified as a change in Protein A column material to Eshmuno A, which may affect purification selectivity for this specific clone (M21C12). A polishing attempt using SEC successfully removed the impurity, but full-scale implementation is not feasible with current equipment.
Impact Assessment: The non-conformance affects clone M21C12, with two consecutive batches (B9026 and B9210) failing to meet purity specifications due to the presence of free kappa light chains. The issue is limited to this product and has not been observed in other batches despite extensive use of the Eshmuno A column, indicating a narrow scope.  From an operational perspective, the impurity prevents product release and has led to significant delays in delivery. Although SEC polishing successfully removes the impurity, it is not scalable with current equipment, requiring approximately one month of dedicated purification time, which would significantly impact production capacity.  The financial impact is moderate, as M21C12 is a low-margin product. Additional purification steps or deeper investigations would not be cost-effective given the product’s commercial profile.  From a customer perspective, the issue has required additional communication, testing, and coordination, and may affect customer relations if not resolved. A small SEC-polished sample (B9216) was sent to the customer for evaluation, which met specification, and suggestions on how to move forward, including timelines and cost.  There are no safety or regulatory risks associated with the impurity, as it consists of free light chains and does not pose a hazard. However, the product did not meet agreed specifications.
Root cause description: The most plausible root cause of the failed purity in batches B9026 and B9210 is the change in Protein A column material from Prosep vA to Eshmuno A. Western blot analysis confirmed the presence of free kappa light chains, which may either be more efficiently captured by the new column or result from antibody fragmentation during purification. This issue appears specific to clone M21C12, as no similar deviations have been observed in other products using the same column.
Classification justification: B9026 and B9210 failed the purity specification of the product specification.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected infection in outflow tubing from ASM
Classification justification: The infection does not appear to have spread to the flowpath itself based on visual inspection. The outflow is under continuous flow to the drain which makes it difficult for any infection to reach the flowpath from the outflow system. The sample from the IC chamber returned negative for growth, therefore it is not considered to be any impact to the production nor the product acquired.

Non Conformance Title: PSS below 90% productivity from ELISA test
Classification justification: The cell bank was tested in production and produced much more than expected. Customer have agreed to accept the cell bank as is without further recloning to raise the productivity. This non-conformance does not impact any product quality.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA Reader malfunctioning during reading of QC analysis
Classification justification: No impact to production timeline or product quality. Spectrophotometer DIA001 is already a qualified instrument in use for measuring of concentration (SOP0026) and is planned to be included as a backup instrument for all ELISA procedures (CAPA120)

Non Conformance Title: VP2024-241 validation - accuracy results for one of the sample did not met acceptence criteria.
Classification justification: The error does not have impact on product quality, or instrument qualfication status. The error was isolated to one operator, which suggests it may not be a process-wide issue.

Non Conformance Title: Power outage for ASM unit
Investigation summary: Since the power outage was for a relatively short period of time, all the cell culture parameters were within the threshold during the period. In-process control performed the following day shows normal results. The ASM was not connected to the power outlet with backup power supply as it was not labelled on the outlet if it was connected to UPS.
Impact Assessment: The power outage stops all the functions of ASM during the period it lasts. If power outage occurs over longer time, it impacts the production and is visible by drop in pH when cells use nutrients in cell culture medium and produce enough byproduct (lactate) that lowers pH. pH is controlled by ASM using gas exchange functionality as well as pumping new medium while continuously removing used medium. Similarly, temperature in ASM incubator drops below the setpoint (37C). If the ASM had been without power for a longer period, the pH and temperature would fall below the lower limit triggering alarm on the ASM after power is restored. Based on the alarm registered by the ASM and the following in process control, it is confirmed that the parameters for cell culture were within the limit. Even though the ASM stops functioning, these parameters does not fall below the threshold at once. It takes time for the temperature to drop from ASM incubator as well as cells to use up medium and accumulate byproduct. Harvesting is also stopped thus the cells do not run out of medium. As the power outage lasted for 5-10 minutes, the impact on parameters (pH, temperature, gas) are determined to be low to non-existing. Production performed as expected immediately after power outage, thus, there was no impact on cells, production and product quality.
Root cause description: The power outage occured due to problem in power supply from the municipality. The wall power outlet are not labelled if it is connected to backup power supply and the ASM was connected to the outlet with no backup power supply.
Classification justification: A power outage to the ASM can be critical to production and product quality, as all critical operations stop during this time, negatively impacting the cells.

Non Conformance Title: Water purification Elix3 stopped producing RO-water after error.
Investigation summary: Error was identified to be inside core instrument. Instrument was serviced/repaired by external technician. A broken pump was replaced.
Impact Assessment: Al time during the instrument downtime there were sufficient quantity of water for productions set up that week. About 80L in tank was available at start of error. If we did not have sufficient water for the actual ongoing or near future productions, our ability to meet delivery time would be challenged. How much depends on down period. 80L of Type 2 water was available for use in purification, but the water demand varies greatly at any given time. Water requirements can fluctuate significantly, and during this event, the demand was particularly high. This situation represents a business risk due to delivery delays and does not necessitate follow-up in the quality system as a CAPA. The process to obtain a quote for a new, higher-capacity water system has already begun and will be monitored by FEU and management moving forward. There is no impact on product quality, though this does impact our ability to meet delivery times and our reputation to the customer. But it is still considered a business-risk, not a quality-risk.
Root cause description: Broken instrument, the instrument had a broken pump that needed to be exchanged. A possible cause is that the instrument is near expected life time (15years). This is a reminder that we might have to expect that there are these kinds of failures in the future as well. However this is still a business-risk and have been agreed by head of production, that it will be handled either by evaluating the need for a new instrument with increased capacity (put in the budget for next year) or exchange new failing parts etc.
Classification justification: RO-water availability is absolutely necessary in production.

Non Conformance Title: HE1/4b/3 batches B9025 and B9028 released without complete documentation
Classification justification: The missing document was placed in wrong folder due to multiple productions of the same product, it was not lost. The document belongs to a in-process analysis, of which several have been performed. If it  was a critical document, such as a QC analysis that were missing from the batch documentation it would have been detected earlier during the technical review. The document that was missing does not affect the production or the product quality in any way.

Non Conformance Title: Secondary ladder spread out during SDS analysis 20.sep.2024
Classification justification: Analysis was disrupted and decided to be done later. No impact on product quality. New analysis was executed (SDS765) and technical requirements were met for that SDS run.

Non Conformance Title: VP2024-241 validation - documentation missplaced
Classification justification: The misplaced Protocol Version 2 was used to execute the linearity study. The linearity study was conducted correctly, and therefore, there is no impact on the execution of the study. All other tests were performed according to the signed Protocol Version 1.

Non Conformance Title: ELISA analysis failed due to not comparable duplicates
Investigation summary: It is a lot of steps in the procedure where the accuracy is very important. Even if the operators are well trained in the process, there are many steps where a small inaccuracy can lead to errors. The process is very manual and not very robust.
Impact Assessment: This is an in process analysis and as no parameters were adjusted based on this,it does not have any impact to the product quality nor the production timeline. Analysis was repeated and results were considered satisfactory.
Root cause description: Most likely root cause is the process it self, which is sensitive to small inacuracies in volume during dilution. The process is very manual with many pipetting steps which are dependent on each other.
Classification justification: In process analysis does not impact quality of the product, but have potential to impact quantity of product. This non conformance has no impact on customer agreements, employee safety or the validated state of the equipment

Non Conformance Title: Centrifuge DIA259 stopped during spin resulting in error
Investigation summary: DIA259 experienced a power cutoff. Consultation with the service supplier and equipment manual pointed to a likely failure in the motor or related circuit board, requiring costly circuitry replacement. Due to the expense and the availability of a similar centrifuge, repairing it was deemed economically unfeasible. Change action 460 started within CMID076 to replace centrifuge.
Impact Assessment: The harvests inside were not implicated by this non conformance as the centrifugation tubes were perfectly intact. Spinning for 15 minutes at 1900G is done when there is a large amount of cell debris, but 10 minutes will still be sufficient for the purpose of removing large cell mass particles prior to the purification process. The harvests are being run though a large filter before being applied to the chromatography separation column (SOP0005), which was done without any issues. As immediate actions were performed to get the harvest out of the centrifuge and the rest of the crude was sedimented inside the cleanroom, no significant delay to the process was experienced. Therefore there is not considered to be any impact to product quality.
Root cause description: Malfunctioning circuitry between motor and circuitboard.
Classification justification: The centrifuge is used to sediment cell debris from crude. As crude is then run though a large filter before being applied to the chromatography separation column (SOP0005), which was done without any issues, there is not considered to be any impact to product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage between cell chamber and outer medium in CL1000 flask
Classification justification: As the cloudyness of the medium was due to leakage observed within the flasks, this has no impact on product quality since there was no contamination or infection. As 1A12 is produced on forecast and this has therefore no impact on the delivery timeline.

Non Conformance Title: QC ELISA run with different OD measuring instrument.
Classification justification: Spectrophotometer DIA001 is an approved and implemented instrument for OD reading and has a program for reading OD at the same wavelengths as DIA003. There is no impact to using the instrument for OD reading of ELISA plates.

Non Conformance Title: Black particle discovered in CL1000 flask
Classification justification: No infection observed throughout the production. Therefore the particle can be considered sterile. No structural deterioration to the CL1000 could be identified. Therefore, production proceeded as expected. this non-conformance is not considered to have an impact on production timeline or product quality. This is the fifth occurrence of seeing black particles in CL1000 the past 12 months which would make this a high risk NC according to SOP5034. However, given the harmless nature of the particle and the observations made both before and considering its sterility, this non conformance is classified as low risk with no additional investigation.

Non Conformance Title: Autoclave not functioning
Classification justification: Classified as low risk as this malfunction does not impact product quality, HSE or customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage between cell chamber and outer medium for CL1000 flask
Classification justification: As the cloudyness of the medium was due to leakage observed within the flasks, this has no impact on product quality since there was no contamination or infection. The production units had already produced enough antibodies for project 2024-59 and anything further would be made on forecast. This has therefore no impact on the delivery timeline.

Non Conformance Title: UCUS 99.05 B8970 released without complete documentation
Classification justification: The document was placed in wrong folder as there was several batches of the same product. As there were several ELISA analysis already included in the batch documentation, it was not detected that this analysis was missing. If it was a critical document such as a QC analysis this would have been detected during technical review. It does not affect the production or the product quality in any way that this ELISA analysis documentation was missing from the batch documentation when the product was released.

Non Conformance Title: QC thawing not kept for sufficient time
Investigation summary: Test for infection according to SOP0002 was not done over long enough time as cells were discarded too early. New vial was thawed to do a new test. QC for Freeze087 and passed all specifications.
Impact Assessment: No infection could be observed in the new cells thawed from the cell bank.  An additional vial had to be thawed to check for potential infection, but none of the vials requested by customer had to be used. There is therefore no impact to the quality of the cell bank or the delivery timeline.
Root cause description: The root cause of this non-conformance is determined to be oversight of the length of the incubation period by a mistake. The operator is aware of the specification to check for infection (using microscope to evaluate cells' condition as this is highly affected during infection) but did not recall the specific required incubation time in SOP. This is therfore considered as human error which occasionally can happen when people are involved in the processes.
Classification justification: This non-conformance will be classified as a high risk as it impacted one of the internal tests classified as QC in SOP for creating cell banks. There is a low risk that any infection would be missed by the shorter time as three days cultivation at ideal conditions would give clear indication that there was an infection. This would also have impacted any growth of the cells which were observed to proliferate well.  Two ampules were frozen for QC testing, none of the ampules reserved for customer's MCB were used for the second thawing. There is no impact to any product or to the rest of the MCB. Production timeline is not affected.

Non Conformance Title: CO2 level above threshold on incubator DIA156
Classification justification: Considering the gradual rise in %CO2 to maximum of 0.2% above the threshold as well as the buffer system of cell culture medium, the NC is not belived to impact product quality. However, as it is a reoccurrent deviation, it must be classified as a high risk. No additional investigation will be performed as this is a known issue and well investigated in NC339, and is not considered to impact the culture or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Cell line vials not found when destroying cell lines
Investigation summary: The nitrogen tanks had accuracy issues addressed in CAPAs 169 and 170, with CAPA 169 being most relevant for this NC. It involved a full manual inventory check (6,000–10,000 vials) to update the cell stock database. Two empty vial positions were missed during this process, but the oversight did not affect invoicing, as the customer’s total vials remained above the relevant threshold. The problem originated from the non-optimalized method for cataloging cell vials in the currently used Sharepoint system.
Impact Assessment: No impact on production quality or timelines. No impact to customer as these vials were to be destroyed and the price matrix for cell line storage was the same as before without these vials.
Root cause description: Sharepoint was not meant for such a large scale, complex database- not letting you expand to see all positions within a specific tank or rack. This made the data entry difficult and most likely led to the missing of the two empty positions.
Classification justification: The customer had 92 registered vials in the cell stock, which is well above the threshold for a change in invoice bracket (49 vials). Consequently, the incorrectly registered vials did not affect the invoiced amount, as the customer remains above the threshold for a change in invoice bracket. However, this is not according to customer agreement regarding storage of cell vials. Therefore this is classified as a high risk NC.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage within CL1000 unit
Classification justification: This is type of deterioration of production unit has been seen before and has been investigated to not cause any issues to the product itself. The cell line BPD002 was observed to have a considerably lower antibody output after harvest 10 thus the loss in yield is also not going to impact production timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Internal audit: lack of documentation of job-specific training of HR advisor
Classification justification: The non-conformance pertains to a gap in the documentation for job specific training for the HR Advisor role. The issue is limited to documentation rather than the absence of actual training or competency of the individual in the role.  The HR role is a supporting function, not directly involved in product or service realization processes. Any gaps in training documentation for this role have no direct impact on the quality of the company's products or services provided to customers.The company already has a system in place to document job specific training, where the main focus has been to document training connected to processes with direct involvement and impact on product realization.Even in the absence of specific documentation, the HR Advisor's competence can be demonstrated in other means, such as feedback from managers and evidence of successful task completion. ISO9001:2015 emphasizes competence, which can be proven beyond written records.  The focus on ISO9001:2015 is on risk-based thinking and continuous improvement. This non-conformance demonstrates the organization's commitment to identifying and resolving gaps. The gap is procedural/documentary and not operational, which positions it as a low-priority risk in terms of ISO compliance. This identified gap will be included in the evaluation and plan for further development of the handling of training for roles that are not directly involved in the product realization.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage in CL1000 pre-harvest 10
Classification justification: The leakage occurred at Harvest 5, within the supplier-assured safe timeframe for CL1000 units. This non-conformance is classified as low risk, as the leakage was between compartments and did not impact product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Incorrect standard concentration written for QC ELISA
Investigation summary: Concentration of positive control was written incorrectly, 1 mg/mL instead of 2.96 mg/mL. The analysis is qualitative, not quantitative thus the exact concentration does not affect the result of the QC (positive vs negative). A concentration of dilution is not specified in SOP. The event is considered to be result of human oversight.
Impact Assessment: No defined dilution is required for the positive control. Since the positive control and the tested samples are clearly positive, the error is considered to have no effect on the QC results or product quality.
Root cause description: Human oversight when writing down concentration of positive control.
Classification justification: This analysis was a QC test which would typically classify the non-conformance as high-risk. However, the error does not impact the QC assessment because this test serves as a qualitative measure of antibody specificity. As a result, no defined dilution is required for the positive control. Since the positive control and the tested samples are clearly positive, the error is considered to have no effect on the QC results or product quality. Therefore, this non-conformance is categorized as low-risk.

Non Conformance Title: Suspected infection in CL1000 production batch
Investigation summary: Production using clone EO-1 was conducted in three CL1000 flasks (batches B9102-B9104) as part of the DEV164 study, with harvests collected individually. During the final harvest (H20), flask B9104's outer chamber appeared yellowish, prompting testing, which revealed infection in the inner chamber but not the outer chamber. Further investigation confirmed that H19 from B9104 was infected, while H20 from B9102 was clean, consistent with the natural yellowing of later harvests.
Impact Assessment: - The non-conformance resulted in loss of crude harvests that were suspected for infection of approximate 66 mL from B9104 and 50 mL from B9102. The harvests from each of the flasks were collected individually, so the harvests from batch B9102-03 can still be used. Since the infection was observed on the final harvest (B9104-H19 and H20), it had a little impact on the expected volume of crude from the batch.  Furthermore, Dev164 is an internal production and therefore does not impact any customer agreements. - Infection possess a risk of cross-contamination. The incubator where the infected CL1000 was stored was tested for infection and performed monthly cleaning. The result showed that the incubator was free from infection. The infection testing showed that B9104 was infected during H19. However, there were no signs of cross-contamination to other CL1000 flasks that were handled on the same time as shown by test result from B9102 and physical appearance.  So, the impact of this non-conformance is considered low.
Root cause description: Same harvest medium was used for B9102-04. So, it can be confirmed that the medium was infection-free.  It is difficult to point out a specific root cause. It was one of the several CL1000 flasks harvested by the operator on this day, which have no supsicion of infection.  The amount of CL1000 handled every week without being infected points to this being a a random compromise in sterile operation during handling of CL1000 flasks, something that is always a small risk for during handling of cellculture. As this was an isolated incident, training and aseptic technique are not considered a likely root cause.
Classification justification: The CL1000 flasks and their harvests are handled separately as well as the incident occurred on the last harvest from the batch, the chances of cross-contamination to other flasks in quite low. This non-conformace will impact neither the production timeline nor the quality of product/harvest that were harvested earlier. However as this is a reoccuring NC and it is classified as high risk.

Non Conformance Title: %CO2 below lower threshold limit in incubator DIA024
Investigation summary: The %CO2 in incubator DIA024 dropped below the Sirius monitoring system’s threshold (3.8%) between December 24th and 26th, 2024 after use on December 24th. The inspection on the incubator was performed on December 26th and no visible issues were discovered. The Sirius CO2 sensor was partially obstructed by CL1000 flasks, which were rearranged to improve airflow. The incubator’s set point was adjusted from 5.0% to 5.2% to stabilize CO2 levels within acceptable thresholds. The drop in the CO2 was almost 0.2-0.4% below the threshold for most of the time recorded by sirius monitoring system. The incubator adjust CO2 level based on its sensor's reading and the measurement performed on 26.12.2024 with reference CO2 measuring device shows that the incubator's sensor is functions well and maintained correct CO2 levels.
Impact Assessment: during 24th-26th of december the %CO2 fluctuated and remained mostly around 3.4-3.6% and the cells were exposed to approximately 0.2-0.4% lower CO2 than setpoint for most of the deviation period. Deviation from lower limits of CO2 can change the pH of the cell culture, which in turn can affect the growth of cells. However, RPMI1640 Cell culture medium has buffer system which can prevent dramatic changes in the pH due to fluctuations in CO2 levels. There would have been change in the color of RPMI cell culture medium in CL1000 flasks if the change in pH was large. No unusal observation was made when the incubator was inspected on 26.12.2024.  Sirius monitoring system is used to provide net-based data of CO2 and temperature in incubator. It does not affect the CO2 level within the incubator. The CO2 level in incubator is regulated by CO2 reading by incubator's sensor. Since the CO2 sensor was functioning well as checked with reference device, it can be assumed that the CO2 level in the incubator was within the threshold limit. In addition, sirius logger shows values lower than actual CO2 level. So, the actual level of CO2 during the deviation period should be above what was recorded by sirius and within the threshold limit.   Thus, this incident is not considered to impact the production in the CL1000 flasks within the incubator at the time or the product quality.
Root cause description: Based on the investigation, it is likely that the sirius CO2 sensor was partially obstructed by CL1000 flasks. It is quite seldom such incident occurs due to placement of the flasks and as this had only a small impact on the CO2 level within the incubator, this incident is regarded as rare event. However, to avoid potential issues in the future, SOP4008 will be updated with a sentence to ensure sensor is not obstructed when placing objects within the incubator.
Classification justification: The drop in %CO2 was relatively small

Non Conformance Title: Customer audit: Control of CAPA effective
Investigation summary: CAPA90, established on 07.02.2024, aimed to transfer paper-based training records into the eQMS to enhance audit readiness and provide managers with a clear view of employee competence levels. However, it was delayed due to significant organizational changes, including structural adjustments and the integration of clinical production facilities, which shifted company priorities and strategies. While the impact of these changes on organizational areas was addressed per SOP5038, their effect on ongoing improvements like CAPA90 was not evaluated. What is more not a sufficient tracking system in place, course delays in observing and addressing the problem. By autumn 2024, the backlog of overdue records was identified and communicated to management and the leader group, prompting actions such as creating a CAPA overview, integrating QMS follow-ups into weekly operator plans, and tracking overdue records monthly to improve QMS performance.
Impact Assessment: There is no direct risk associated with CAPA90, as it was a preventive action aimed at improving audit readiness. Despite some training records still being paper-based, these documents remain easily accessible to employees, managers, and auditors. However, as part of this investigation, the risks associated with other overdue CAPAs were evaluated: -	Updating product specification template: The delay increased the time spent by QA during batch review. -	Implementing CEDEX for IgG analysis: The delay led to more failed IPC analyses, requiring additional resources to investigate, report failure, and retest analysis -	Updating flow cytometry procedure: No risk, as the analysis will not be performed until Q4 2025. -	Updating SDS-PAGE method: The delay presented a risk of increased failed analyses, but no failures were observed.
Root cause description: The CAPA was not executed within the timeline as the company went several through reorgisational changes (refer to CMID68 “Changes in Organisation structure, roles, and responsibilities”, CMID63 “	Integrating Clinical production site facility into Diagnostic site infrastructure”, CMID76 “Implementation of Equipment from Clinical site under the Diagnostic QMS”) which significantly changed company strategy and priorities. For all 3 changes, the impact on different organisation areas was addressed as according to SOP5038 “Change management” , however impact on already planned / ongoing improvement were not addressed or evaluated.  What is more, the CAPA monitoring system was not providing a clear overview of the CAPA timeline and is possibly overdue.
Classification justification: Lack of compliance with ISO9001:2015 standard: Nonconformity and corrective action. The company shall evaluate the need for action to eliminate the cause(s) of the nonconformity and implement any action needed.

Non Conformance Title: Isotype test not matching previous information
Investigation summary: All tests conclude that the antibody is IgG2a not IgG3 as previously classified by the clone supplier.
Impact Assessment: Ultimately the customer's application of the antibody determines its quality and suitability. The MY-11 clone has been in production for about 20 years without receiving any complaints or reports regarding its functionality. This suggests that the classification discrepancy has not impacted the antibody's ability to meet customer needs or intended applications. The antibody product 8510 is unaltered.
Root cause description: Incorrect classification during transfer of cells.  Product specification and CoA was not updated with new isotype after test in 2009.
Classification justification: This non-conformance is classified as a high risk as the very classification of the product produced by the clone is incorrect, IgG2a instead of IgG3 antibody production. Accurate product classification is critical to ensuring regulatory compliance and customer confidence in a product's specifications.

Non Conformance Title: Delivery of ADCF medium made to the wrong address and stored at 10+ degrees for several days
Investigation summary: Medium was delivered to wrong address due to errors in packaging at suppliers, causing it to be stored at temperature outside manufacturers specification..
Impact Assessment: Specification for material states storage temperature is to be 2-8 degrees, this is further confirmed by Cytiva when contacting them about the issue. There are no stability data available for temperature outside this limit.  Material was delivered to NMD the following week, however as DHL does not have a storage area with controlled temperature at 2-8 degrees the medium was in a loading dock for 4-5 days before being shipped to NMD. It is therefore not possible to determine excact temperatures the shippment was expsed to, but most likely a few degrees above outside temperature which was as follows: which makes it between 0-10 degrees  As it is safe to assume that the temperature did only deviate a few degrees, it is likely that the components of the medium have not been impacted and it works as expected.  the wrong temperature can impact several factors, such as chemical Instability where some medium components (e.g., amino acids, vitamins, growth factors) may degrade outside the recommended temperature range. It can also cause precipitation as certain salts or supplements might crystallize or precipitate at 0°C, altering medium composition. Microbial Contamination can be a concern as temperatures above 8°C could accelerate microbial growth if contamination occurs. Finally temperature can affect product performance as the functionality of the medium may be affected, leading to suboptimal cell growth.  To be able to use the medium, testing needs to be done to estimate impact to these factors. this is anchored in CAPA270. Until testing has been performed and concluded on, the medium must stay quaranteened and can not be used for production purposes.  As this medium has not been used for any productions, and will stay in quarenteen until either tested to be working or discarded, this has no impact to product quality. As stock of ADCF was sufficient at the time, this issue did not impact any production timelines.
Root cause description: Error in labelling of package at Suppliers, see SC2. Labell is generated by manufactureres system prior to shipping.
Classification justification: As this impacts the quality of critical material this non conformance is classified as high risk

Non Conformance Title: Weak band intensity in SDS-PAGE analysis
Classification justification: This is classified as a low-risk NC as there is no impact on product quality. An assessment was made to perform a new analysis to ensure that any potential bands would be detected, and we confirmed that no additional bands were present.

Non Conformance Title: Reslults from SDS-PAGE not approved according to specification in PS
Classification justification: NA. Ref NC346. Full investigation and conclusion will be conducted in that NC.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage in CL1000
Classification justification: Low risk, no impact on quality. Supplier only guarantee for leakage up to 30 days (about H10) We have enough product to meet delivery amount and timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Not comparable duplicates for ELISA analysis
Investigation summary: ELISA procedure is very manual, with a lot of steps in the procedure where the accuracy is very important. Even if the operators are well trained in the process, there are many steps where a small inaccuracy can lead to errors. The process is very manual and not very robust.
Impact Assessment: This is an in process analysis and this analysis does not have any impact on the product quality. A sufficient number of successful analyses have been performed for this production to ensure correct amount of product was produced.
Root cause description: Most likely the root cause is the process, which is sensitive to small inaccuracies in volume during the dilution steps. The process is very manual with many pipetting steps witch are dependent on each other.
Classification justification: This in process analysis does not impact the quality of the product. This NC has no impact on customer agreements, employee safety or the validated state of the equipment.

Non Conformance Title: Cells without documented negative mycoplasma test thawed in clean room
Investigation summary: Cells were thawed inside of the clean room because the operator did not have sufficient time to prepare for the thawing and due to lack of communication and incomplete information on the PS that was followed.
Impact Assessment: No mycoplasma contamination was detected, so there was no impact on cell culture and will not have any impact on the product quality. If the cells had been contaminated, it could have led to cross-contamination and compromised cell quality, loss of productions and it could have impact on the status of the clean room.
Root cause description: Multiple personnel involved in the process, increasing the risk of miscommunication.There was conflicting information on the two PS for the two projects, while only one PS with missing information regarding mycoplasma test was followed during the thawing.
Classification justification: This NC is classified as high risk because a potential mycoplasma infection in the cells could contaminate the cleanroom and other cell lines in the clean room and mycoplasma is extremely challenging to eliminate. This may potentially impact timelines for multiple customers and result in a financial loss for the company.

Non Conformance Title: Medium used for maximizer production not recorded in medium register
Classification justification: No impact on process or product quality, employee safety or customer agreements.

Non Conformance Title: Initial harvest delayed for CL1000 production
Classification justification: This NC does not impact the quality of the product. It has no impact on customer agreement, employee safety or validated status of equipment.

Non Conformance Title: Infection in CL1000 batch B9177
Investigation summary: Harvesting of CL1000 is a manual process and operators involved in this process harvests several CL1000 flasks every week. During routine harvesting, operator SBA observed turbidity in the outer medium of a CL1000 flask from batch B9177, indicating a possible infection, and the flask was removed and handled as per SOP1064. No other contamination signs were found in the remaining flasks, and samples from the affected flask and incubator taken for testing shows that the infection was limited to B9177. On 31.03.2025, the latest previous harvest from the affected batch were inspected, and H17 was discarded due to the potential infection.
Impact Assessment: Suspected infection in CL1000 production unit resulted in termination of production batch. B9177 was originally planned to be terminated at harvest 25, but due to the infection the batch was terminated at H18. Following termination, H17 (B9177-79) was also discarded after visual inspection. This impacts the total amount of harvest collected at the end of production from the batches. However, the loss of harvests is not large enough to make a significant impact on production and have no impact on the timeline as this production was for stock.       Infection possess a risk of cross-contamination. The incubator where the infected CL1000 was stored was sampled for infection and monthly cleaning was performed. The infection sampling result showed that the incubator was free from infection (Ref.: Infection sampling.pdf). Test performed showed that B9177 was infected, and visual inspection suggested that the lastest harvest (H17) was too. However, there were no signs of cross-contamination to other CL1000 flasks that were handled on the same time and stored in same incubator.  The infection was limited to a single CL1000 batch and thus the impact of the non-conformance as a whole is considered low.  Curida Diatec has the number of infections per year being five or less as one of the KPIs. This is the only infection registered for Q1 in 2025.
Root cause description: Training: The last recorded infection in a CL1000 flask was discovered on 24.10.2024 (NC371). Since then, operators have harvested several hundred CL1000 flasks without incident, indicating that the occurrence of contamination is low. All the operators working with CL1000 are approved for aseptic works. The fact that one production unit in clean room was infected without any spread is also a good sign of good aspectic work and cleaning routines, as this production unit was harvested in a series and kept together with other units in the CO2 incubator. Medium: Same harvest medium was used for B9177-94 and only B9177 was infected. A freshly opened medium is used for the outer medium of each CL1000 flask. So, it can be confirmed that the medium used was infection-free.  It is difficult to point out a specific root cause. It was one of the several CL1000 flasks harvested by the operator on this day, which have no supsicion of infection. The amount of CL1000 handled every week without being infected points to this being a a random compromise in sterile operation during handling of CL1000 flasks, something that is always a small risk for during manuel handling of cellculture. As this was an isolated incident, training and aseptic technique are not considered a likely root cause.
Classification justification: The infection was limited to a single CL1000 batch and thus the impact of the non-conformance as a whole is considered low.  Curida Diatec has the number of infections per year being five or less as one of the KPIs. This is the only infection registered for Q1 in 2025. One production unit in clean room was infected without any spread which shows signs of good aspectic work and cleaning routines, as this production unit was harvested in a series and kept together with other units in the CO2 incubator.

Non Conformance Title: Communication alarm in sirius logger connected to DIA169
Investigation summary: Data loss occurred because the Sirius logger ran out of battery unexpectedly within 24 hours, despite the system's design to alarm users at 10% battery and provide a 4-week replacement window, as per SOP4032 v6.0. Although multiple control layers exist—including daily checks, regular admin reviews, and SMS alerts to operations—the failure was compounded by unclear communication between the admin and superuser.
Impact Assessment: According to SOP4032 v 6.0, pt. 6.4.2, non conformance is registered if the data are outside the range for more than 24 hours. In this case, the loss of data was less than 24 hours. In addition, when operator HPJ went into the clean room, no unusual observation was made. The temperature was within the range just before and after the incident and the chances for dramatic change in the temperature during the non conformance period is low. So, the impact of the non-conformance is low.
Root cause description: According to the JEL, JEL observed the alarm on 08.04.2025 and notified superuser (SBA) about the alarm. However SBA couldn't recall receiving the information. In addition, the alarm was not considered very critical as the battery should hold up to 4 weeks after the alarm. But it was not the case in this non-conformance. It was discussed with the facility manager regarding this issue and is decided that no CAPA is performed for this incident. The potential breakdown on communication could lead to similar non-conformance in the future. But with the timeframe that should have been available to replace battery and the levels of control on the sirius system, the incident has not occurred often. However, the logger will be closely monitored to check if there is any failure in the logger that could have led to draining the battery faster. Followed up in trend review by Facility manager.
Classification justification: The incident occurred for less than 24 hours

Non Conformance Title: Identical CED numbers used for different CEDEX analyses
Classification justification: No impact on product quality, validated state of equipment/process, customer agreements or employee safety.

Non Conformance Title: Product stopped and returned by customs
Classification justification: The product was safely contained within correct temperatures as confirmed by shipping agent Biocair using a temperature logger.

Non Conformance Title: Missing CO2 sirius data from incubator DIA024
Investigation summary: A data loss occurred from 14:28 on 08/06/2025 to 21:46 on 09/06/2025 due to a Sirius logger failure. Other systems were functioning normally, indicating no widespread issue or risk to incubator function. The incubator was not in use, and no immediate action was taken over the weekend. The logger was restarted manually on 09/06.
Impact Assessment: On the 09.06.2025, JEL arrived at the lab and found that the logger display was blank. A simple restart (unplug battery) was sufficient to restart the logger. The incubator was running as normal with no indications of failure and similar CO2 measurments before and after the missing data period.. Batches 1A12 9233-38 og 9239-44 were in incubator DIA024 during the time 14:28 on 08/06/2025 to 21:46 on 09/06/2025. Based on the above obervations, it is unlikely that the batches have been in jepordy during this time.
Root cause description: The likely cause can be logger hardware related as the display was dead on arrival. Battery contact can be an issue. It is also possiblly due to a design flaw in older firmware. The issue will be tracked in routine reviews.
Classification justification: CO2 levels before and after the missing period are stable and similar to each other, and no apperant impact to cells within the incubator at the time of the incident.

Non Conformance Title: Failed QC-ELISA  MCB titer
Classification justification: SOP5037 pt 4.1 for Out of Specification results states that all potential OOS results from QC testing must be classified as High Risk, however since we knew the technical requirement of dilution were not followed it is classified as low risk. The analysis were repeated correctly and successfully.

Non Conformance Title: Thaw 961 started with too high volume
Classification justification: Classified as low risk because: - cells never made it to antibody production, i.e there was no product made that could have been impacted - no equipment, process or system was impacted - employee safety was not impacted - production was already delayed, as they were challenging to expand and customer was informed

Non Conformance Title: HE1/4b/3 did not pass purity specification during SDS-PAGE
Investigation summary: 16.07.2025 GYR interim closing:  Impurities in HE1 are under investigaion, however technical requirments of the analysis were met, and as SP2 and 931 passed their specification and were comparable to their reference batch, the results are deemed reliable and accepted. The Batches B9314 and B9280 can be released. Summary of investigation into the HE1/4b/3 bathes:During the Quality Control (QC) release testing of batch B9315 (HE1/4b/3), the product failed to meet the specified purity requirement of >90%. Additionally, the SDS-PAGE analysis revealed a band pattern inconsistent with the reference batch (B9028), specifically showing an additional band characteristic of IgG. Investigation Findings Contamination Confirmation: The presence of IgG was confirmed in batch B9315 using a Cedex Bioanalyzer (0.07 mg/mL IgG) and subsequently validated via ELISA. This is a critical impurity as the clone HE1/4b/3 is intended to produce IgE.   Affected Batches: The investigation was expanded to include all final batches from the relevant CL1000 production units. ELISA testing confirmed IgG contamination in batches B9315, B9317, B9363, and B9364.   Containment Boundary: Batch B9273 tested negative for IgG, establishing that the contamination event likely occurred post-Harvest 12.
Impact Assessment: 16.07.2025 GYR Interim closing:  The observed non-conformance is isolated to batches B9315, B9317, B9363, and B9364  (HE1/4b/3).  There is no indication that Sp2 or 931 are affected by this issue. The technical integrity of the gel run was confirmed, and all relevant specifications for Sp2 and 931 were fulfilled. Thus, product quality for batches B9314 and B9280 is not compromised, and these batches can proceed to release. Final impact assessment: The contamination is attributed to a specific, isolated handling error during manual harvest operations. While the CL1000 system is engineered to prevent contamination, the manual handling of harvest fluids presents a residual risk. Current routines are considered robust, re-engineering the process to eliminate this theoretical risk entirely is not considered cost-effective relative to the low likelihood of recurrence. The investigation is closed with the rejection of the affected batches.
Root cause description: The investigation concludes that the root cause is cross-contamination during the harvesting process. It is hypothesized that hybridoma cells producing IgG were accidentally introduced into the HE1/4b/3 process stream (likely via accidentally shared media sources during harvest handling). Once introduced into the CL1000 units, the IgG-producing cells proliferated and outcompeted the IgE-producing cells over time, correlating with the increased impurity levels observed in later harvests.
Classification justification: B9315 failed purity specificartion according to customer agreement

Non Conformance Title: Harvesting of Sp2 not performed within time regime of 6-7 days after inoculation
Classification justification: No impact on product quality, validation, HSE or customer agreements,

Non Conformance Title: Product vials for B9314 not stored appropriately with parafilm seal
Classification justification: No impact to product quality or timelines as sealing is mostly to ensure the product during shipping and the product was still located in-house.

Non Conformance Title: Operator working in cleanroom without valid gowning validation
Investigation summary: Two operators entered the clean room while waiting for their gowning re-validation results. The lack of operators during the summer month of July made it impossible to keep operators out of clean room while awaiting their revalidation results. Validation status are written into a manual list on BizzMine and has to be checked on regulaly. With the summer planning, this was not done in time and two dead lines came up too soon for it to be fixed in time.
Impact Assessment: This issue was discovered and solved within the same month for gowning re-validations that are done once a year. The two operators who entered the clean room while waiting for their re-validation results were both very experienced and passed their re-validation shortly after. There was no increase in bio burden levels or infections, and therefore it has no considerable impact on any product or the facility.
Root cause description: The root cause of this NC is defined as system/processes that doesn't work optimally. A separate manual validation list in BizzMine for the Production Manager to regularly check combined with the big amount of planning before a summer holiday where few operators are at work, makes this system prone to mistakes.
Classification justification: This has a direct impact on operators validation status and is therefore considered a high risk NC.

Non Conformance Title: Cell viability below specification for QC control
Investigation summary: During technical review of cell banks for EO-1 and ABS 079-02, it was discovered that % viability of QC thawing was below specification of >35% viability. Root cause is determined to most likely miscalculation of the volume and/or total cells loaded per vial during PSS/MCB generation, as a result of inadequate documentation practices - specifically, the lack of a field for volume of cell suspension used for spin-down and freezing in DOC0002.1. For ABS 079-02, there were too many high-risk NCs registered related to the generation of the cell bank to approve its quality. The cell bank was discarded, and no further investigation of the low % viability of the QC thaw was considered necessary.
Impact Assessment: The cell bank for EO-1 passed specification upon thawing a new vial according to SOP0002 reaching viability of 43% (attachment 3).  The quality of cellbank PSS2 and MCB1 ABS 079-02 can not be guaranteed, considering all high-risk NCs related to it. They must therefore to be discarded, and generation will restart from PSS1.
Root cause description: Root cause is most likely miscalculation of the volume and/or total cells loaded per vial during PSS/MCB generation, as a result of inadequate documentation practices - specifically, the lack of a field for volume of cell suspension used for spin-down and freezing in DOC0002.1.
Classification justification: Final product for clones ABS 079-02 do not conform to specification.

Non Conformance Title: Discrepancies in naming and labelling of cellbanks and number of vials produced
Investigation summary: During batch review of cell bank production for two projects, discrepancies were found in labelling practices and the number of vials produced.  While full traceability was maintained, gaps in procedures, operator training, and oversight contributed to inconsistent practices and a misunderstanding of requirements.
Impact Assessment: QC testing confirmed sterility and viability for all MCB vials produced and batch documentation provides full traceability between source and resulting vials. Therefore, no risk to safety, identity, or purity of the final product.
Root cause description: 1.    Labelling and Traceability    a.      SOP0003 lacks specific guidance on how handle or discard failed cell banks or how to continue labelling after a failed cellbank (e.g., recloning scenarios or failed batches) and does not touch upon cellbanks without numbering.    b.     Cell stock register allows continued access to unapproved or failed cell bank entries without system restrictions or flagging.    c.      Operators responsible lacked a clear overview of the multiple concurrent cell bank processes.         2.      Vial Count Discrepancy    a.      Lack of clarity in SOP wording regarding QC vial requirements being in addition to MCB vials.    b.     Miscommunication or outdated training passed on by a retired cell expert regarding the required number of vials.
Classification justification: As this non-conformance is regarding traceability of the product (labelling and naming) as well as in direct conflict with the agreement with the customer (number of vials), it is classified as high risk.

Non Conformance Title: Additive not added to medium
Investigation summary: During technical review, it was discovered that 8 of 13 medium batches used for ABS079-02 cell banking lacked sodium pyruvate, despite being required by the Product Specification. The root cause was identified as human error under high workload conditions, combined with limited oversight and operator inexperience. Medium register traceability was also found to be insufficient, prompting further improvements to the electronic system.
Impact Assessment: Sodium pyruvate was not added consistently during cell expansion prior to cell bank creation. As it supports energy metabolism and stress resistance, its omission may impact cell growth, viability, or product consistency. Ther risk to product quality is considered moderate and prior to using the cellbanks further testing is needed. Cellbank ABS079-02 has several other high risk non-conformances (NC394 and NC395) and when everything is taken in to consideration, the quality of the cellbank can not be guaranteed. therefore the ABS079-02 cellbanks will be discarded and generation of the cellbanks will start over.
Root cause description: Root cause is therefore determined to be a combination of capacity planning (high workload of barely trained operators) and human error.
Classification justification: Not according to costomer agreement, possible product impact

Non Conformance Title: Samples of high concentration not diluted during BioAnalyzer analysis
Classification justification: This is a production of EO-1 through DEV164 with the aim to implement Corning CELLine flasks. This product does not have a scheduled delivery date or specific aim in amount that would affect any delivery to customer. The estimated yield from this production corresponded well with the amount of product collected after purification. Therefore, these IPC results have no impact on the final product or its potential delivery.

Non Conformance Title: Inconsistent results from Cedex Bioanalyser
Classification justification: No impact on product quality.

Non Conformance Title: High temperature reading from DIA022 CO2 incubator sensor

Non Conformance Title: Delayed signatures of verification for use of correct material during thawings

Non Conformance Title: Monthly maintenance of pipetboys in analytical lab delayed
Classification justification: From the supplier it is recommended to change the filter every 3 month with normal use.The pipetboys used at analytical lab is not used on daily bases and all serological pipets used on those have an additional filter.

Non Conformance Title: Bacterial growth on settle plates for LAF bench during D-validation
Classification justification: This non conformance has no impact on customer agreements, employee safety or the validated state of the equipment. There have been several validations performed in DIA223 after HPJ's validation, all with 0 CFU for LAF settle plate, so there is no reason to suspect something is wrong with the bench. No product was made during this validation, so this non conformance has no impact on product quality either.

Non Conformance Title: Weekly maintenance not performed for DIA107
Classification justification: No impact on product quality or timeline.

Non Conformance Title: Stuck drain in cold room DIA283
Investigation summary: The blocked drain happened on the 25.AUG.2025, 31 days after the last semi-monthly maintenance with 1000 ml Latona drain opener. This could again indicate that 1000ml of Latona drain opener on a semi-monthly basis is not enough to keep the drain free from blockage.
Impact Assessment: No impact on the stored items as the leak only was on parts of the floor. This was quickly cleaned and sanitized with peroxide. the whole floor was cleaned with peroxide.
Root cause description: The semi-monthly maintenance of drain is not performed frequently enough.The blocked drain happened on the 25.AUG.2025, 31 days after the last semi-monthly maintenance with 1000 ml Latona drain opener. This could indicate that 1000ml of Latona drain opener on a semi-monthly basis is not enough to keep the drain free from blockage.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Root cause: System/process - the identified root cause is that the current process of semi monthly maintenance with 1000ml of Latona fails to keep the drain clear, most likely due to insufficient frequency.
Classification justification: The leakage leading to spillage of water and medium on the floor in cold room had little to no impact on Xcellerator or the environment of the cold room, as the xcellerator is running and outflow is unidirectional and small in amount.

Non Conformance Title: System pump P-900 in FPLC core system DIA005 stopped working
Investigation summary: P-900 system pump, SN1350719, did fail after a production run with lower flow speed, no impact on product. Pump was sent for external service/repair, but no direct failure was found.A spare pump/system i already in place for replacement due to previous NC's.
Impact Assessment: The last sample run (FPLC4106, B9370 pooled into B9372) before pump error did come out without errors. This is confirmed by the final results as a normal elution peak can be observed in the graph from run. Therefore this non conformance does not have a direct impact on product quality. Also the P-900 pump does not have any direct product contact. Spare system DIA284 will be out of function regarding P-900 unit.  It is identified a potential for delay of product delivery if purification was not performed following week, purification was planned the week after incident. But the spare pump was put in place in a short time (2 days including startup) and this was performed according to SOP for error handling.
Root cause description: The P-900 pump in system DIA005 has been in yearly service for since it was new.The pump error failures that need external service appears not very often, every third year or so.  This is consistent with that it is not expected but still could happen.It is difficult to set a root cause since it is not clear why pump did fail.
Classification justification: Stop in later purifications could turn into delivery delays and have economic impacts. It is critical for production that purification can be performed according to current scedules.

Non Conformance Title: Black particle discovered in medium (M250807A)
Classification justification: No infection observed throughout expansion of cells, therefore the particle can be considered sterile. This non-conformance is not considered to have an impact on production timeline or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Black particle discovered in CL1000 flask
Classification justification: No infection observed throughout the production. Therefore the particle can be considered sterile. No structural deterioration to the CL1000 could be identified. Therefore, production proceeded as expected. this non-conformance is not considered to have an impact on production timeline or product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Temporary shutdown of AcuSyst Xcellerator due to low system voltage
Investigation summary: Intermittent startup alarms and computer restarts were observed on the Xcellerator DIA408 during preparation and early operation. Voltage measurements showed that the 5 VDC supply to the computer board was consistently below specification, while other system boards maintained acceptable levels.  The low voltage caused the computer to intermittently interpret a power loss and restart, although all process-critical functions (circulation pump, flow paths, incubators) remained stable and unaffected. The supplier (C3) confirmed that the most probable root cause is a drifting/failing 5 VDC power supply.  Adjusting the terminal voltage temporarily restored stability, but repeated drift indicates that the power supply requires replacement.  No impact to product or process integrity was identified.  A replacement power supply will be installed during the scheduled October 2025 service.
Impact Assessment: No impact on cells or harvested product occurred during these events as the machinery e.g. pumps, heat and such still keeps running. This is isolated to the computer board (display touch screen), due to temporary insufficient voltage supply to the computer board.
Root cause description: Root cause is assessed to be failing 5 VDC power supply. The instrument is first purchased in 2014 and was purched used. Hence the equipment is old and has a long run time.
Classification justification: If the machine had not restarted automatically, it could have had major financial consequences, as the entire production might be restarted, and it would significantly affecting the timelines.

Non Conformance Title: Leakage in flowpath during fill flush for maximizer
Classification justification: The inoculation of the flow path was delayed by a couple of days. This did not affect the expansion of cells and the leak was detected before inoculation. Hence low risk.

Non Conformance Title: pH alarm in ASX production B9325
Investigation summary: pH stabilized once the medium pump rate was increased, this indicates that the current pump rate was insufficient.
Impact Assessment: All parameters such as pH, cell count, viability and concentration were carefully followed for the rest of the day and for several days afterwards and the system quickly stabilized. Concentration measured 04.09.2025 (before the pH alarm) : 1,01 mg/mL  Concentration measured 08.09.2025 (after the pH alarm) : 1,10 mg/mL Based on the previous run experiences and the current incident, it is highly unlikely that this has any impact on the product.
Root cause description: The medium pump rate was insufficient. Since we do not have an alarm system that provides notifications beyond the instrument itself, the alarm is not received outside working hours or during weekends. This pH drop occurred during a period of rapid cell growth, and the bioreactor should probably have been checked during the weekend.
Classification justification: This NC is classified as a low risk considering the alarm limit was marginally exceeded  and had no impact to product quality.

Non Conformance Title: Storage location N3.3.1.82 missing in cellstock
Investigation summary: Mistake during removal of information from Cellstock caused the location to be set as xxx" instead of specific locations. Data was available in version log in Sharepoint and was possible to restore. Storage column data is now restricted where "xxx" is not a valid data."
Impact Assessment: No impact to production, product quality or timelines, the vial was in the correct postion, removal was correctly documented and no information was lost as its readily available in Sharepoint audit trail
Root cause description: 1) System, where location data was available for editing. 2) Human error in removing information in cellstock that is not to be removed
Classification justification: This NC does not impact product quality, employee safety or customers agreements. However it is classified as high risk due the potential impact it could have for the customer that store their cellbanks at CD if this incident and potential similar incidents had not been detected, as this is our only database for LN2 storage. Back up of cell stock register is performed and stored by IT supplier Braathe therefore information regarding deleted location can be recovered.

Non Conformance Title: Dilution during ELISA analysis not in accordance with PS

The analysis was not performed according to the specification of the initial customer agreements (PS). However, as the analysis was performed to a higher degree of accuracy and was agreed upon with customer this does not have any impact to the customer expectations.
